Guide and Walkthrough (DS) by GrayFox2510

Version: 0.60 | Updated: 09/27/2008

All trademarks and copyrights contained in this document are owned by their 
                 respective trademark and copyright holders.

Version 0.60

Final Fantasy Tactics A2 - Grimoire of the Rift

This FAQ/Walkthrough is copyright of Alan Quirino (grayfox2510 (at) gmail 
(dot) com), 2008.

Ok, so let us go over a few basic things...

1) If you see anything that looks like my guide somewhere else, and you think
   it was stolen without my permission, please let me know. Thanks.
   
2) As of currently, I am not allowing any other sites besides GameFAQs to host
   my guides. IGN being a possible exception from time to time, but otherwise,
   I'm afraid I must decline your offers under most circumstances. You are,
   of course, still free to try. Who knows? Also, under the odds that you are
   granted permission, please check regularly the following link for updates.
   http://www.gamefaqs.com/features/recognition/8373.html
   Sites that have free permission to host my guides are:
    + GameFAQs.com (Remember kids, GameSpot is sort of an affiliate of
      GFAQs.com, so they link directly, and thus, they are ok with me)
    + IGN.com (Though they are from time to time)
    + NeoSeeker.com

3) If sending an inquiry about this game, be sure to put, at the very least,
   "FFTA2" in the title, just to help me sort my mail. Otherwise,
   it might get lost and you get no answer. Do note however, I have the right
   to answer you or not. That is, obvious questions or information you could
   easily reach in here might not be answered. I normally still answer almost
   every mail (Yeah, I'm a good person), though that's not 100% of all cases.
   Also, I know my English isn't perfect but at least I tried when writing 
   this, please do the same when you mail in a question.
   My mail is at the top and bottom of this file.
   
4) Needless to say, this guide is copyrighted according to the DMCA and stuff.
   So, please don't steal anything (If you want to use something, a bit of 
   information or whatever, go ahead, but give proper credit where due).
   Just don't take the whole thing.
   
5) Mails thanking me for the help and whatnot are always appreciated. ^^
   (Honestly, they alone are enough to make me keep going sometimes)
   Feel free to write in English or Spanish, whichever you prefer. ;p
   Technically, I can understand written French, but don't expect an answer in
   French.

-----------------------------01. Game Introduction-----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

[011]----------------------------01.01. Story----------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

_______________________________________________________________________________
[012]---------------------------01.02. Controls--------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Controls for a tactical game could never be easier, mind you.
Touch screen elements are simple and straight. You tap, you confirm whatever 
you tapped. 

GENERAL CONTROLS
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

D-PAD...........Move the cursor around the menu or on the world map.
A Button........Confirm the highlighted menu.
                Confirm where to move on the world map.
                Scroll text.
B Button........Cancel out of any selection, if possible.
Y Button........Display help information of the highlighted quest in the clan's
                quest report.
X Button........Open up the menu in the world map.
                Open up the Item List in the clan unit screen.
L Trigger.......Move to the previous unit in the clan unit screen.
                Move to the previous available quest in the clan's quest 
                report.
                Move to the previous zone in the world map.
R Trigger.......Move to the next unit in the clan unit screen.
                Move to the next available quest in the clan's quest report.
                Move to the next zone in the world map.
Start Button....No use.
Select Button...No use.


BATTLE CONTROLS
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Battle has technically two phases; placement (P) and combat (C).

D-PAD...........Select the unit to be placed. (P)
                Move the cursor. (C)
A Button........Select a unit and confirm its placement. (P)
                Confirm any command given. (C)
B Button........Cancels the placement of the current unit. (P)
                Cancels any selected command. (C)
                Press and hold over any unit to see its movement range. (C)
Y Button........Open up the unit information. (P)
                Displays the help for the highlighted command. (C)
                When in free-cursor mode, display the unit information. (C)
X Button........Start the 'engage' after units have been placed. (P)
                Open up the System menu. (C)
L Trigger.......Move to the previous unit during placement. (P)
                Scroll to the previous unit in the battle order. (C)
R Trigger.......Move to the next unit during placement. (P)
                Scroll to the next unit in the battle order. (C)
Start Button....No use.
Select Button...No use.

_______________________________________________________________________________
--------------------------------02. Walkthrough--------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
When you are looking for a particular mission/quest, just search for its name.
You should get there pretty quickly. 
It helps if you add a dash (-) after the name, directly after it, no spaces.
As a note, I won't list the Law Bonuses, since there seems to be either 
multiple sets per mission, or they are entirely dependant on your current
position on the game, items available, and what not.
_______________________________________________________________________________
[021]----------------------02.01. Story Walkthrough----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Well, before we get down to an mission, you will end up having to answer
some questions. These questions will decide which clan privileges you get from
the beginning.

Clan Privileges are basically small powerups that affect your whole side during
an engage. In the previous FFTA, when you broke the law, the character could 
go to jail. Now, when you break the law, you lose the effect of your clan 
ability, you can't revive allies and you don't get item bonuses at the end.
But no more jail anymore.

Start with Luso's name if you wanna change it.

Then you'll get your question homework. There are a LOT of options, so I won't
list out each result (maybe not yet). Here are some key abilities you might 
like having from the get-go.

 Bonus AP 1: B, C, C.
Bonus EXP 1: B, C, B.
     Luck 2: A, A, C. (Thanks to Javier)
 Power Up 2: B, B, B.
 Evade Up 2: B, A, A.
 Speed Up 2: B, C, A.
 Bonus CP 1: B, C, C. 
Bonus Gil 1: B, A, C.
Smash G.B.1: B, B, A. (Thanks to V. Orlando for the last ones)


Personally, I chose Bonus AP 1. Do note, there are other ways to get these
abilities. I just listed those I know.

1. This summer I'm going to...
  A) Hang out with my friends!
  B) Play video games non-stop!
  C) Study for next year!
  
2. As a personal goal, I want to...
  A) Be able to swim half a mile!
  B) Get my warrior to level 75!    (Hah, FF XI reference)
  C) Learn a foreign language!
  
3. Next year I promise not to...
  A) Misbehave in class.
  B) Oversleep in the morning.
  C) Let my dog eat my homework.      ...Maybe I can find a cat. =D
  
Busted. I mean... Yeah, follow along with the story, you'll be in Ivalice in
no time after a not-so-unfamiliar sequence...

_______________________________________________________________________________
--------------------Story Mission 01: Stranger in the Woods--------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Monster in Targ Wood!

We hunters of the village tried to stop 'im, to no avail. Somebody do 
somethin', please! You don't need to kill 'im, just drivin' 'im off will do.
  Heriward, Wood Village Hunter
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank:  1 | Fee:  100 | Days: -- |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|

|   -                   | Location: Targ Wood            |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 250 Gil                    |
|   -                   |   - Gihket Lead x2             |

|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? No                |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat Klesta!
Forbidden: Ranged Weapons.
           - Attack with bows, greatbows, guns, hand-cannons, and cards are
             forbidden.
             
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This fight might not be the hardest of the book (First and last pun, sorry), 
but it has it quirks. You control Luso, a Viera White Mage, and a Nu Mou Black
Mage (names are randomly chosen). I will pretend you know what they both excel
at.

Heed the tutorials from them before you start doing your own thing. It's a
quick refresher in case you haven't played a Tactics in a while. 

Luso starts decked out as a Soldier, the default Hume job, basically. That 
means that he can swing a sword and can take a bit of pain. It's up to you if 
you want to go directly for Klesta, or hit the small guys first. Just be
sure to back yourself up with the other two units. Cid will fight on his own,
so pay heed to that guy. 

Be careful of Klesta though. Its Territorial Marking can hit a + zone a good
distance away from him, so try to keep your units spread to minimize damage.
Though, if you are lucky, he might fancy your Black Mage over anything else.	
His peck is a melee-type skill, so use your White Mage to heal up. 

Luckily, you only have to bring Klesta to about half HP or so and it runs away.

----------

You might wish to save on the world map. Just press X, then go to System and
into Save. Otherwise, go ahead and move to Wood Village. 

You will get to name your Clan about here. Not sure, but Gully is not on my
list of ok names... So yeah. 

Eventually, you'll be back in the world map. Follow the instructions and head
into the pub again. Not that you can do much else, mind you...

You will only be able to get "A Paw Full of Feathers" for the time being. So
go ahead and accept that. It's your next story mission, after all. You can now
hit the shop. No, just do it.

The selection is... bad, so to speak. Try to leave the shop and the Bazaar 
option will be added. Feel free to head down to the section to see all the 
things you can get. Go ahead and get the new sword for Luso. Hop to your next
mission.
_______________________________________________________________________________
-------------------Story Mission 02: A Paw Full of Feathers--------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
What's in the woods? Wolves, that's what! And my chocobos are so put out, 
they're molting early. 
Somebody, rid us of these horrible howlers!
  Chocobo Corral "Sasasha"
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank:  2 | Fee:  100 | Days: -- |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |
|   -                   | Members: 5                     |
|   -                   | Location: Targ Wood            |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 1050 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Snake Skin x2              |
| Talents:              |   - Tiny Mushrooms x2          |
|     Negotiation       |   - Fresh Water x4             |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? No                |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Fire
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire are forbidden.
             
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Choose your units. You will only be missing one out of your whole rooster.
There's no real need to go into deep strategy just yet, so you can make your
own party right now without trouble at all.

A White Mage is always suggested though. Your Black Mage can be useful, as long
as you remember NOT TO use Fire. The Bangaa Warrior is a good hitter, and
the Archer can back you up from afar. The Thief is fast, and has decent hit.

Mix-and-match. Also, as a personal suggestion just in case. Try not to stick to
the same units all the time. Spread out, get more units, have different jobs
ready and on par with your level. You never know when you'll need 'em.

You will then be able to choose a Clan Privilege. They are small bonuses (at
least right now, since you only have the lesser ranks available). Take your
pick. 

On with the mission though. 

You can't exploit weaknesses yet, so just gang up on the same guy and bring it
down. Rinse and repeat. You'll see this strategy works a lot, but try to put 
the guys who can take the hit near the enemies so that they don't gang up on
your mages in return. 

--------------

Once you are done, you should head back to town. You will lose Cid for the
time being. Do the usual, shop, get your quests... Etc. To note, story missions
have no days limit when you look them up in the pub.

It's up to you if you want to rush the story or do the sidequests as they come
along. 
_______________________________________________________________________________
----------------------Story Mission 03: The Yellow Wings-----------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Drive off those brigands, the Yellow Wings, beforeI lose what little business
I got left!
They've been squatting the highroad, demanding gil from all what pass by.

If I can't use that highroad, I can't get to Camoa; and if I can't get to 
Camoa, I'm finished!
  Kagran, Merchant
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank:  3 | Fee:  100 | Days: -- |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |
|   -                   | Members: 5                     |
|   -                   | Location: Targ Wood            |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 1750 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Lamia Scale x2             |
| Talents:              |   - Sanative Needle x3         |
|     Negotiation       |   - Earth Stone x3             |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? No                |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Ice
           - Weapons and abilities that use ice are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You are probably at a slight disadvantage in terms of raw level, but at 
least you don't have the silly AI controlling all of your units. On the flip
side, that unit the AI is taking over on your side is lv6. =D

The Archer is going to be doing Focus before attacking. Her hits might put a 
nice dent on you, so be careful. The White Monk will require a beating, while
at the same time, you gotta make sure you don't cluster together for a magick
swoop from that Black Mage.

Since the White Monk and Thief are coming for you first, fight them on the 
way or deal with them quickly. The Animist will probably use 100% Wool on the
first turn, getting Shell and Protect for 3 turns. Not a problem, really.

Once you reach their back lines, avoid hitting the Animist if you can when he
has Protect, unless of course, he's the only one left. Kill them in your
preferred order. They shouldn't give you too much trouble.

----------

You can now leave this area and head into the next one, Camoa Highroad.
It's up to you if you wanna head right away or check the pub for more quests.

When you reach the city of Camoa, you'll have an event that'll unlock the
next story mission for you.
_______________________________________________________________________________
-----------------------Story Mission 04: You Say Tomato------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
You say tomato, I say kill them all!

Why kill vegetables, you ask? Har!

Did I mention quivering stalks, or slavering fangs?
I was out for a stroll the other day and one bit clean through me britches! I 
doubt me or me britches are the only victims here. Help!
  Gusah, Greengrocer
                        _________________________________

Writing this from memory. I was almost going to quit writing this thing, you
know? 

Besides the Alraune, you have 4 other freaks. They are all fairly weak, 
especially if you've been doing the sidequests. They will usually attack you
normally, or using a tackle they have that barely hits for more, if anything.

Aside from your team (5 guys, plus Cid), you'll have Adelle, a female Thief on
your side. She's controlled by the AI of course, but she should be able to 
pull her weight on a hit or two. The fight, overall, couldn't have been 
easier.

enough...

cockatrices, or chickatrices, whatever, and Ugohr himself.

Ugohr will, on occasion, target himself and some of your units inside a +
shapped earth attack that will heal him, but hurt you. Of course, it's only 
like a 40~ heal at most, so not a big deal. He should fall quickly in the
end. With or without everything else dead.

----------------------Story Mission 06: Wanted: Gilmunto-----------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
             -WANTED!-
Wanted: Gilmunto, possibly a variant strain of nidhogg, known to lurk in the 
foothills of the Aldanna Range.

Vicious and violent, should be considered dangerous in the extreme!

Those without faight in their sword arm, give this one wide berth.
  Jylland Defenders of the Peace
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 12 | Fee:  300 | Days: -- |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Headhunt                 |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: The Aldanna Range    |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 1330 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Succulent Fruit x3         |
| Talents:              |   - Storm Stone x5             |
|     Negotiation       |   - Quality Pelt x2            |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? No                |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯ 
Objective: Defeat Gilmunto!
Forbidden: Fire
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Welcome to Jellies. They take ok physical damage, but this one has Absorb 
Damage, so your damage will be setback just a bit. Magick is the way to go.

And Gilmunto takes half-damage from all three main elements. The Jelly will try
to cast Thunder on himself, taking some of your units as well, since he'll just
heal from that. The Worgen shouldn't pose much of a threat. Unless you really
want to sift through and take down Gilmunto, I suggest you take the Worgen
first while your Black Mage or equivalent takes charge of the Jelly. The
Thunder Drake in the end, and finally, Gilmunto. 

His Dragon Force is a Blue Mage skill, btw. Keep that in mind for the future
if you want a Blue Mage. And of course, be careful since he can put that on
anyone in range. Oh, and he can heal the Drake or the Jelly with his Lightning.

------------

Watch. Go back to Graszton when you can. 
Auction season close, guess we better get some extra Clan Points. Hit up the
pub, do any sidequests you want, then hop to the next mission.
_______________________________________________________________________________

¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Zedlei Forest has been bombed!

Looking for a clan to rid our woods of an infestation of bombs!

With auction season upon us, who doesn't want a few more clan points in the 
bank?
  Society for Woodland Preservation
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 14 | Fee:  300 | Days: -- |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Zedlei Forest        |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 2870 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Platinum x5                |
| Talents:              |   - Agathis x3                 |
|     Negotiation       |   - Suspect Mushroom x1        |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? No                |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Restoring MP
           - Actions that restore MP are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Obviously, you don't want anything with you that deals fire damage. Switch
out weapons, or whatever. Just, don't...

In case you don't know Bombs, they got this nasty habit of self-destructing,
dealing some very nice fire damage to units nearby. Of course, that shouldn't
happen if you gang up on them one by one so badly they can't even blink.
One of them has Critical: Quicken though.

Aside from that, if you got a Black Mage with Blizzara, you are in for
a ride. Or ice weapons in general. The Floating Eyes might bother you a bit,
so it's entirely up to you on how you wanna deal with all of the enemies here.

And let us not forget about our Marshmallow friend, who can use Fire to heal
itself, the bombs nearby, and hurt you. 

-------------

Head back to either Camoa or Graszton. Save first of course. You should see
the Action House option now. For more details, please head down to the
Auction House section of this guide. For the record, both auctions available
to you right now are winnable using only the tokens you get. 

Whenever you are ready, feel free to keep going. Also, as you keep winning
auctions, '?' points will appear on the regions you have under control.
Some will give you items, some will make you fight against other clans
automatically (they got pissed at you) and others have a risk of fighting.

---------------------Story Mission 08: Pearls in the Deep----------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 17 | Fee:  300 | Days: -- |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Defeat Mark              |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: The Galerria Deep    |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 3490 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Zingu Pearl x8             |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? No                |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat the Lord of the Flowsand!
Forbidden: Actions by Nu Mou
           - Nu mou may only move and perform basic attacks.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Well, you heard the guy. No Nu Mou. 

The Mark in this case can draw you in quickly for a Drain attack. Crush a
nearby foe and Immobilize. Or alter Gravity somehow to damage all of your 
units. Draw In can hurt, and he heals for the exact amount he deals. So it's
up to you to get some Protect going on, or just lay the smack down on him
as quick as you can. Stun Crush will hit those right next to the affected
unit, so try to keep yourself spread. Yes, in that little space. 

You know the Antlions by now probably, they can delay your attacks,
lower your defenses, or simply Slow you. These two avoid all basic attacks 
from guns and hand-cannons, but not A-Skills.

The big guy is immune to debuffs. So just focus on a strong attack force since
he has a LOT of life. That, and be sure to bring someone who can keep you up.
They are weak to Wind, so bring a Bishop if interested. The small ones absorb
Earth, and the big one is just immune. 

You might want to just kill them on the way and then focus on the big guy,
since the small ones keep coming. 

Maple Leaf sends that if you happen to have a Faerie Shoes (check the
Bonga Bugle Silversun edition for a possible spot), you can get the chest in
this mission in case none of your units can reach it to get an Angel Ring.

-------------

Oh, Luso. You sneak. Head back to Graszton whenever. Event, do the usual.

Objective: Defeat Ewen!
Forbidden: Ice
           - Weapons and abilities that use ice are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Don't even bother with a Clan Privilege...

Remember you can't revive on this battle, so either keep yourself up, or if
you have a death, make it count.

You'll be facing 2 Ninja; Ewel and a generic one. Ewel has nothing going for
him, unless Counter is that important. The generic one has a few veils but
also has Dual Wield, so be careul. Fortunately, Counter is the one thing he
is missing.

The Assassins, one can Silence or Slow, the other can Poison. Nothing too
big. The Time Mage can Haste/Reflect, and has Cure. Along with Critical:
Quicken of course. 

Knowing the odds, both Ninja are gonna get close to you since they will 
probably act before any of your units. It's up to you if you want to lay
a decisive strike on Ewel right away, or toy around with his units first. 
I double-KO'd him (Flurry, both hits were KO) right away since I wasn't in the
mood to fight... You don't mess with my Bonus AP 3 and get away with it.

-------------

Go back to Graszton whenever. After the event, you gotta head up to Moorabella.
You can now pick up the next story mission. I would say to do the usual, but
after this one you get access to Grias, so... Guess where my priorities are? =3

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Oh look, Vaan... And that law again. Guess what, the guys here go from 20 to
25, with Ed. Believe it. 

There's a White Mage with Cure and Esuna. A Time Mage with Quicken and Slow.
It also has Critical: Quicken for whatever reason and, oh. Shieldbearer for
good measure. 
The Fusilier can Blind and Silence you. The Berserker can counter and deal
some damage.

Our Genius is a Sage with Bio, Water and Aero. Also has Critical: Quicken. 
Vaan, being the Sky Pirate he is, has a few offensive skills but otherwise,
nothing that will aid you greatly. 

All in all, focus on not breaking the law if you can (that is, if you have
units at 25 or below who only focus on the big guy). Up to you if you want to
play around or finish it quickly. 

This is where I was kindly reminded that breaking the law on your winning
action doesn't matter. My Luso? Lv26. Ed? Lv25.
I go from behind, Beat Down him for 160, I win. No law broken. =D

           - Using the same action as the preceding unit is forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
There's a Wraith and 2 Ghosts. Only the Wraith can use Dark magick here, for 
a change. The other two only have Sleep and Silence touches. The Wraith has
that awesome Critical: Vanish. 
The Zombie has a drain skill. 
And the 2 Floating Eyes... They are both the same. A Poison skill, along with
their suicide skill. Both have Critical: Quicken to aid them on their
own suicide. And it hurts, mind you.

Luso is a forced unit, as usual. But Adelle and Cid are not, if you wish to
swap them out. Just remember 4 of your enemies here end up in a tombstone when
defeated. So either exorcise (or similar) them, or use a Phoenix Down once
they are down to get them out of the fight for good. 

-------------

Yeah, watch. After that, go back to town for the next event. Do the usual,
side quests as you wish, then move on.

----------------------Story Mission 12: Sleepless Nights-----------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
My dreams have been troubled of late.

It started the night after my visit to Nazan Mines. I cannot sleep, my arms
and legs are leaden, and even plump Bisga game-hen tastes like ash.

They say a creature within the mines weaves ill-omened dreams. I fear...I
fear I may be a victim. Please, stop it before I go mad.
  Dabool, Geologist
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 24 | Fee:  400 | Days: -- |

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Hah, boss battle music from FF XII. Remember, Cid is not necessary. You can
switch him if you wish.

The Zombie has Drain Touch, as usual. Plus Blink Counter.
From the Bloody Orb, Vampire is the only thing you can see.
The Wraiths both have Dark along with Flash and Wake the Dead. Don't forget
Blood Price.

Our mark, the Oversoul, is armed with Counter and Impervious. Darkga along
with Deep Sleep (Stop+Sleep) and Shackle (Immobilize+Disable).

Remember you only need to bring the Oversoul to a tomb-state. If you want to
deal with the rest, keep Phoenix Down in hand or Exorcise-like abilities
(Bloody Orb excluded). 

-------------

Watch. Then return to any town. Guess we know what to do next. Of course, you
know. Side quests if you want, move along at your pace.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The way we go into battle here doesn't even make sense, but whatever. 

Our Alchemist friend can use Astra and Rasp, plus he can control Headless.
He has Absor MP and Impervious.
The Raptor has all 3 Crushes.
The Ranger has Sten Needle and Leech traps. The problem is his Mirror Items.
Be very careful with that.

The Headless have Bone Shatter, and Sunder Earth, respectively. Both have
Brute Strength.

Kill them as they come for you, avoid the Gria or defeat him by Reaction
commands, careful with the Ranger, and knock out that Alchemist.

-------------

You know the deal, back to town. Hurdy will give you some Spiral Vine and
gil so you can go to the Bazaar and create the item he wants; it's right
at the top, as Hurdy's Request. As soon as you buy it, you automatically
enter the next scene. 

Hurdy will join your clan afterwards. Head back to Moorabella when you can/want
and check up on that stone.

----------------------Story Mission 14: Seeking the Stone----------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
I search for a piece of magicite the color of silver. I'm sure it was in my
laboratory just the other day...

Please, if you know something-anything at all-let me know. My laboratory is in
the Bisga Greenlands. 
  Narph, Magick Akademy Scholar
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 29 | Fee:  400 | Days: -- |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: The Bisga Greenlands |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 5480 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Einherjarium x2            |
| Talents:              |   - Adamantite x5              |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? No                |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Targeting All Units
           - Actions targeting all units at once are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Narph is a Scholar with Earth Dragon, Rime Bolt and Thunder Flare. Add 
Regenerate in there.
The Paladin has Defender and Cover, plus Counter and Doublehand.
The Dragoon can Jump, use Lancet and Thunder Breath. Plus Blink Counter.
The Ranger has Sten Needle, Silence Gas and Love Potion. Return Fire included.
Guile, I mean, the Hume Hunter has Sonic Boom and Rend MP.
The Gria Hunter has Vitals Shot.

You start dead in the center. Odds are, you can pretty much beat the crap out
of the Scholar before he can do anything, and I recommend you do so just in 
case. After that, it's up to you how they fall. 

Since you would be closer to, the Dragoon and maybe the Gria could go down
next. There really isn't much to this fight. Though the Paladin will be
covering random people every turn.

-------------

Story time. You know the deal afterwards, go back to a town, then do other
quests or whatever.
_______________________________________________________________________________
-------------------Story Mission 15: Wanted: Sky Pirate Vaan-------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Apprehend the sky pirates Vaan and Penelo!

Those scurvy scum of the skies infiltrated the Beltorey Manse and made an 
attempt on the baron's life. A commensurate reward is promised to the one who
can bring them in.
  House Beltorey
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 32 | Fee:  500 | Days: -- |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |
|   -                   | Members: 5                     |
|   -                   | Location: The Rupie Mountains  |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 5040 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Stormsoul Crystal x2       |
| Talents:              |   - Gemsteel x4                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? No                |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat all foes while defending the sky pirates!
Forbidden: Not Moving 1 Tile
           - Each unit must move exactly 1 tile before ending its turn.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You might just want to break the law right away, up to you.

There's a Lanista with Souleater and Charge!.
A Berserker with Hone Senses, Furore, and Smite of Rage. Death Strike included.
The Ranger, besides Sten Needle and Leech, can Mirror Items. Careful.
The Gladiator comes equipped with Rush and Thunder Assault, plus Blink Counter.
Our Bishop friend knows Cura, Dispel and Water; Magick Counter ready.

Vaan and Penelo are like before. They can hold on by themselves for a bit if
you want to try and follow the law, but I suggest you keep long-distance units
to help deal damage anyway if you go for it. Otherwise, bring whatever you want
and rush the enemy.

-------------

Guess we go to Graszton next. And again, you know the usual. 
_______________________________________________________________________________
--------------------------Story Mission 16: A Request--------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
For Clan <name>, a request.

I must speak with you.
I await in the Ruins of Delgantua.

P.S.
  I trust you enjoyed my gift?
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 34 | Fee:  500 | Days: -- |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Defeat Mark              |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Ruins of Delgantua   |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 6550 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Crusader Tonic x1          |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? No                |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat Illua!
Forbidden: Being Robbed
           - Having gil or items stolen is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Strangely, Cid isn't required on this mission if you want to swap him out,
same for Adelle.

There's a Ninja dualwielding that can use Throw and Unspell. He also has an
Orb of Minwu.
The Elementalist comes with Fire Whip (Disable), Boulder Crush (Immobilize), 
and Sliprain (Slow).
The Spellblade has Poison, Sleep and Doom Blades.
The Seer has all 3 -ra spells along with Esuna. He comes with Pierce and
Replenish MP too.
The Illusionist has Prominence, Tempest, and Freezeblink. He can absorb MP,
but fortunately, doesn't have Blood Price or Halve MP.
And Illua, of course, who is a special job of her own, the Nightshade.

Her Time Blade is a hit and delay. Saber raises her Attack and Accuracy.
Her Dimensional Rift however, reduces the HP of all enemies caught in the 
attack by 75%... And it's a good range, about the range of a Summoning
Magick.
She has Reflex and is also Impervious. Most of them are either immune, or 
absorb one element or two. 

You shouldn't have trouble with the law, and I mean it. Yep, that again.
Otherwise, remember the focus here is to defeat Illua. You can rush her down 
quickly, or toy with all of the other units. On your way up, though, be sure 
to take out the Elementalist. You should be able to without too much trouble. 
Just to avoid her sticking debuffs onto you.

It's not over yet! Watch the scene, then get ready for... whatever that is.

-------------

Objective: Defeat the Neukhia!
Forbidden: Opportunity Commands
           - Opportunity commands are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The two Plagues on this one have Demonic Gaze, Binding Circle and Dread. They
both have Counter. Behemoths or Sprites will replace them if you kill them; 
among notable skills they have, you'll find Cross Counter and Rend Shield on
the big guys. From the Sprites, you'll find White Wind and Haste. 

Our mark, the Neukhia, has too many new skills;
 Comsumpion deals damage to units in a large area.
 White Hole deals damage to units straight ahead.
 Syphonja depletes ALL of the units' MP in the area.
 Apocalypse confuses units in a small area.
 Catastrophe lowers Defence and Resistance in a small area.
It Absorbs Damage and is Impervious of course. It absorbs Dark and is half-
resistant to all other elements sans Holy. He doesn't care about Holy.

Pick a good law bonus here and get to it. Deal with the Plagues if you wish,
but do note what Luso says. See those purple things on the side from where you
start? Get units there and hit the Charge command to raise the power of the
cannon. You gotta get both before you can do anything else.

What do we do with that, though? Nothing, as soon as you charge it, it 
automatically fires, hitting all enemies. It's a cheap, quick source of damage
so you might as well use it. The Charge icons will then move randomly, and
next you gotta hit 3 of them. It might go down to 2 charges again eventually,
but it seems kind of random.

-------------

Well, that was a nice change of pace. From where you appear, just head into
the next '!' icon for the story. 
Then head back to town. 
_______________________________________________________________________________
---------------------------Story Mission 17: The Dig---------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
It's the 410th Goug Mines Salvage Run!

Ancient machineries lie in the dark depths below Goug, and we're going to dig
'em up, kupo!

You never know what you might find, kupo!
  Goug Machinists Alliance
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 37 | Fee:  500 | Days: -- |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Goug                 |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 7580 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Orichalcum x1              |
| Talents:              |   - Hurdy-Gurdy x1*            |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? No                |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
* Hurdy-Gurdy is an unlisted reward. 

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Fire, Ice, Lightning
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire, ice, or lightning are
             forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Lots of Kupos going around. Also, Mooglebanes? Chocobos and Hares? Fun.

Black Chocobo has Flame and Beak, plus Crit: Haste and Defense Up.
The Red one has Cure, Meteor, and Barrier. Crit: Quicken and Death Strike.
All four Mooglebanes have Illude, which is basically a Charm. They all have
Charged Attacks, two have MP Shield, the other have Magick Counter.

Pick your units, and look at the law to prepare accordingly. Fortunately, 
these hares won't be Haste'ing everyone and tossing all those buffs, but you
gotta worry about their charms. The Hares, of course, are weak to Fire, and
immune to Holy for what it matters.
Chocobos are weak to Water, Lightning and Holy, as usual.

Odds are, the Mooglebanes will move first. Use the chance to quickly get in
and beat them as they come close. Try to focus fire. Every one less of them is
one less Charm. ^.~

Oh, traps, yeah. A few of those are lying around.

-------------

Get to a town after the battle like, you know, that one right next to you.
"Let's get questing." Sounds like a true MMORPG...

Adelle will leave your clan for the time being. If you want her back, pick up 
the next story quests. And any items you might have had equipped on her are
gone. FOREVER.
.
.
Or not, they are back in your inventory.

Soon as you pick the next quest, a scene will trigger.
_______________________________________________________________________________
-------------------Story Mission 18: Through Another's Eyes--------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Monsters have settled in to the area around the ancient stone heads of Kthili 
Sands.

Not only are our surveys being delayed, I fear there may be nothing left to
survey if those monsters have their way with the ruins!

Please, somebody, rid us of them!
  Rothwall, Archaeologikal Survey Team
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 40 | Fee:  500 | Days: -- |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Defeat Mark              |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Kthili Sands         |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 9100 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Orichalcum x1              |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? No                |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Bring Adelle back to her senses!
Forbidden: Ranged Weapons
           - Attacks with bows, greatbows, guns, hand-cannons, and cards are
             forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Illusionists are good substitutes for the lack of Ranged.

Adelle will be sporting her unique job already, the Heritor. She comes armed
with Lennart only, which is a straight-line damaging skill. She is also
Impervious. 

Other than her, you fight a Thunder Drake, two Pit Beats, and two Zaghnal.
They have the standard array of skills you've come to known and love/hate
already, so I doubt you need my hand here.

What you want to do here, is move Luso next to Adelle (though be careful of
her Lennart) and select Talk. Keep doing it; after three times, it all should
be over. Kill the other enemies before if you wish, or not. Killing Adelle will
of course result in defeat.

Oh, traps. Yep.

-------------

Watch. Head back to a town afterwards. Adelle will be back with you back in
the job you had her. 

(This is NOT needed to progress the story) If you want her Heritor job, go to
Lezaford and follow his instructions by going to the Zedlei Forest. More on
this in another update.
_______________________________________________________________________________
[022]----------------------------02.02. Quests---------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Quests are arranged according to where they show on the Clan Report. Such as
section A4, B1, D3, etc. Then by the number slot they have, from left to right,
top to bottom. Just search for the name...

In most cases, advancing the story is enough to make a quest appear (assuming
you've done the preceding quest). If a particular month is required, it will
be noted as well.

If a Story Mission is required to complete to trigger the appearance of a 
quest, it won't be noted. 
_______________________________________________________________________________
------------------------------A1-02: Reagent Run-------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
The muskmallow, favored herb for treatment of ague, is again in season. 
Seeking herbalists to venture into Targ Wood and procude a supply. No previous
experience required. 
  Jylland Apothecary's League
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank:  8 | Fee:  200 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Satisfy Petitioner       |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Targ Wood            |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards                        |
|   -                   |   - 1580 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Cactus Fruit x3            |
| Talents:              |   - Nepenthis x1               |
|     Negotiation       |   - Spruce x3                  |
|        Aptitude  2    |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  4    | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯ 
Objective: Collect a sample of muskmallow!
Forbidden: Knockback
           - Actions that knock the target are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Welcome to one of the worst laws. Pray you don't get a critical hit and knock
back the enemy, like it happened to me... Thrice. You can always try flanking
an enemy unit or hit them against the wall, though. (I forgot to point this one
out, so thanks to like the 15 people that told me so. XD)

Here's how this one works. See all those sparking spots on the ground? You move
a unit next to it, use the special command, and search. If you find the 
muskmallow, you clear the mission.

Enemies are fixed. So after you kill these guys, you have all the time of the
world to finish the mission. Of course, you can pay no heed to the enemies if
you also wish to, they aren't that hard anyway.

Oh, on the odd chance you have a Blue Mage, the Baknamy can use Magick Hammer.

In my case, the muskmallow was in the northern spot. Though I'm sure it's
random anyway.

----------------------------A1-08: Kids These Days-----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Some of our younger clan members have been carrying on in the pub, disturbing
the peace, as it were. I reckon it's best to teach them a good lesson now, and
nip the problem in the bud. That's where you come in. Give 'em a good thrashing
for me!
  Criek, Clan Criek leader
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank:  8 | Fee:  200 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Targ Wood            |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 790 Gil                    |
|   -                   |   - Waltwood x3                |
| Talents:              |   - Xergis Tin x4              |
|     Negotiation       |   - Gihket Lead x4             |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  4    | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  4    | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Ice
           - Weapons and abilities that use ice are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Is it me, or is the Ice law too common? Not that I complain...

You'll be dealing with your standard assortment of enemies. The White Monk can
do Air Render, so you'll have to be careful on the range department around him
and the Archer, who can use Focus at any given time.

The Fencer can poison, but Fencers generally go down fast. The move fast, but
they go down fast. The soldiers might try to Rend your Power or Magick, but
it generally isn't enough to make them live longer. Don't even bother with the
Thief. He doesn't even have Steal Gil mastered... Thieves these days don't even
know the basics... At least he can counter.

Try to get the Warrior down quickly though. He'll go Berserk when he hits
Critical HP.

  Finnes, Watch Chief

                       | Rank: 33 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |

| Items:                | Type: Satisfy Petitioner       |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Fluorgis             |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   - Ranger            |   - 4830 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Star Fragments x2          |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation  15   |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  15   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat all foes and destroy all traps!
Forbidden: Not Moving 3 Tiles
           - Each unit must move exactly 3 tiles before ending its turn.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Retarded law time, yay.

Obviously, all enemies have Avoid Traps. You will fight 2 Berserkers, 2 
Vikings, and a Ranger.
The Berserkers both have different skills. One can raise his Crit Chance,
and deal lots of debuffs. The other has the Evasion skill along with Ground
Shaker. They both Counter.

The Vikings both have Thundara and Strong-arm, with Critical: Berserk. 
And the Ranger, to make things worse, can use Sten Needle along with Love
Potion, a Charm trap. He also has Critical: Vanish, so try to make the last
hit to him count.

You can do traps and enemies or vice-versa, and I suggest the latter. 
After you deal with them, you can worry about the traps. The law is your
problem if you wanna respect it, since you are going to have to move around
a lot in order to stop on a 3rd step in a trap.
_______________________________________________________________________________
------------------------A1-10: The Trappings of Failure------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
In response to monster attacks in the regions outlying the city proper, we 
set a number of traps to rid ourselves of the beasts once and for all. Alas, 
the traps had no effect. Worse still, they now pose a danger to our citizenry.
Please destroy these traps before they can do any real harm.
  Camoa Ministry of Wildlife

¯¯¯¯¯
Law shouldn't be an issue, unless you are overleveled...

Yes, you read the objective and what Luso said right. You have to trigger the
traps yourself, taking damage in the process. 

The damage is always half your current HP, so kill all enemies and spring them
to avoid problems.

-------------------------A1-14: A Voice from the Well--------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Every night I hear what sounds like a woman's voice calling to me from the rear
garden. I've just moved residences, and con't care to move again-- nor do I 
have the funds if I wanted to. It's rather disturbing to say the least, and I'd
appreciate it if someone would look into this.
  Lacado, Townsperson
                        _________________________________

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Ranged Weapons
           - Attacks with bows, greatbows, guns, hand-cannons, and cards are
             forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yeah, not a fun law. If you got archers, either switch their jobs or stripe
them of their weapons. Or just use them as meat-tanks. =D

Their Night skill might bug you off, since it puts everyone down to sleep.
And I do mean EVERYONE. Well, unless it gets resisted, of course.
Not to mention, the Lilith has MP Shield. Which means you are going to have
to gang up on her with everything you got. XD

Obviously, start with the units close to you and let the other two come your
way before you dispatch them. Remember, it's ranged weapons you can't use. 
No one said anything against ranged attacks, such as magick.

-----------------------------A1-15: The Star Seal------------------------------

Have you heard of the "Stone with No Name"? Those philistines at the Akademy
won't hear a word of it, but I think the Stone bears a message from the distant
past. According to legend, holding the Star Seal to the Stone takes one to a
hidden land, protected by watchful guardians. I ask you to find the truth.
  Mauri, No-name researcher

This quest is fairly simple. Kill the Baknamy as it comes to you, then approach
the stone and use the Examine command.

Of course, it doesn't end there. You'll have a second fight coming up.

-----

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Ice
           - Weapons and abilities that use ice are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This is the good one. If you have a Green Mage, don't bother. The Werewolf
will just Roar you out of whatever buff you can get.

Aside from that, focus on the Sprites since they can slow you. Work your way
through them as they come. Remember the Werewolf can set itself to counter.

-------------------------------A2-01: Camoa Cup--------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Tourneys are Under Way!
The next tourney will be the Camoa Cup.
Rules are as follows:
 -Maximum team size: 6
 -Bouts to be won: 3
 
 Tourney Guild - Camoa Branch
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 19 | Fee:  300 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      | Required Month: 
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |   Silversun
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Camoa                |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   - Lanista           |   - 2400 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Gigas Pendant x1 *         |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  4    | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  4    | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
* The Gigas Pendant doesn't appear in the actual quest information, but is
a reward you get for ending in 1st place and is used to summon Belias.

Objective: Defeat all foes in the entry bout!
Forbidden: Using MP
           - Actions that consume MP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
That law is so not in your favor. Especially since you can't repay in kind,
knowing the other guys will be using magick. Of course, you can simply forgo
the law... Which is always the same among all 3 fights.

You might want to try and snuff out the Black Mage first. The Animist might
bother you with its Sheep Count skill, which can put everyone in range to 
sleep, not counting that he will 100% Wool himself. 
The White Monk has Air Render. 

It would be in your favor to use high-level Clan Privileges, Power or Speed,
or the like. Unless you are looking for breaking the law. Otherwise, mow
them down to one unit and do whatever you can to disable it (Stop, Disable,
Sleep, up to you) and then use items to heal up before finishing it off.

Even though the enemies here level with you (so you can't outlevel them), 
they don't get new skills after time. You can always use that to your 
advantage.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the elimination bout!
Forbidden: Using MP
           - Abilities that use MP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
No rest for the weary, straight into the next fight. Your units will end up
starting with whatever HP/MP they had. So be careful.

This party is magickal in all aspects. 3 Black Mages, a Green Mage, and a 
Time Mage. The Viera can only Blind and Silence you.
Out of the Black Mages, only the Hume has Fira. Everyone else is up to the 
3 basic spells but they all have Geomancy. The Time Mage has Haste.

Try to disable the Black Mages and deal with them as you see fit. Silence,
Disable, drain their MP; and keep your units spread. Same deal as before, knock
them down to one, disable it, and heal up for the final bout.

If you brought purely melee units in order to keep the law there, you should
be able to bring them down easily, even though they got high Magick values.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the final bout!
Forbidden: Using MP
           - Abilities that use MP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The Camoa Braves... There's a Warrior without rends, thankfully.
The White Monk has Air and Earth Renders. The Hume Thief might be able to 
Disable or Immobilize you in melee range with his Archer-borrowed skills.
The Moogle Thief has two borrowed skills from the Moogle Knight, including
Moogle Lance.
The Black Mage only has the three basic spells. And no Geomancy, thankfully.
Plus, its Magick isn't as awesome as the previous guys.

So, all in all... Take the Hume Thief first I say, or disable it. You don't
want to be crippled on this fight. This is the last one and you get a 
summon, go for it.
_______________________________________________________________________________
------------------------------A2-02: The Sun Seal------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
I begin to grasp the message with the Stone, yet only dimly... Perhaps I have
erred, made some fundamental mistakes in my research. I need you to try the Sun
Seal to know if I am right or wrong.
  Mauri, No-name researcher
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 28 | Fee:  400 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |

|                       | Location: Graszton             |

|        Aptitude  13   |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: The Moon Seal (A2-03)

Objective: Examine the Stone with No Name!
Forbidden: Items
           - Items are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Remember that, as usual, this is a double battle. Do check the law on the next
part, since if you use purely-melee units, you might break it right away.

For the first part, you are dealing with two undead; they can Sleep, 
Silence or Blind you. And they can always heal by casting Dark/Darkra on 
themselves.

The Baknamy... They suck, you know that. The Lilith, as usual, can Doom/Charm
or Addle.
It's up to you if you want to bring an Archer or White Monk to get rid of the
undead, since if you use a Phoenix Down on their tombstone, you would break the
law. 

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Targeting Adjacent Units
           - Actions targeting adjacent tiles are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Oh yes, the laws can go even funkier. Standard melee attacks? Not a chance.
Or at least, I pretended they counted towards breaking the law. Didn't want
to test. I suppose skills that knock away will help you bypass this law, but
again, I didn't want to test it. 

If you want to heal up, move your healers away and cast, or heal yourself
with items while the enemy catches up.

Speaking of... Baknamy? Who cares. The Drakes only got Thunder Breath.
Doesn't mean they can heal with it as usual. The Wendigos, besides having
Absorb Damage, can delay your next turn, deal increased damage, or earth
elemental damage. 

The Zaghnal is the upgraded Werewolf; it can still Roar you out of any buffs
you might have. But this one can also confuse your units. Since the Wendigos
will probably Focus before they get to you, you can have the Zaghnal Dispel
them if you buff one of your units with whatever as well, making the Wendigos
waste more turn. 

Deal with them as they come to you and you should be fine. Even if you have
a melee-oriented group going on for this mission, you can still build a
(somewhat) successful offense out of Counter and a lot of Potions if you don't
want to break the law. 
_______________________________________________________________________________
-----------------------------A2-03: The Moon Seal------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
The Stone with No Name was no mere stone!
What wisdom did the ancients leave for us to find? I'll need to know more in
order to decipher their message to us.
The legend tells of three seals. I want you to try the second, known as the
Moon Seal.
  Mauri, No-name researcher
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 18 | Fee:  300 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |

|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: The Star Seal

Objective: Examine the Stone with No Name!
Forbidden: Lightning
           - Weapons and abilities that use lightning are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This is like the previous time. Approach the stone and use the Examine command.
Killing the enemies is optional. 

Careful about leaving the Floating Eyes in Critical HP near their turn, since
they will suicide themselves, dealing a good shot to you as well. Otherwise, 
they can Poison or Silence you.

The Baknamy is the same as usual. Magick Hammer and its Goblin Attack. Split
your party in two to take the Eyes, then converge on the Baknamy. Examine
the stone and... yes, get ready.

WARNING! Since you are warped and enter combat right away (no choosing
different members), be sure to look at the law for the next fight to avoid
any troiuble.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Bludgeoning Weapons
           - Attacks with rods, staves, poles, knuckles, hammers, and maces 
             are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This law shouldn't affect you greatly unless you are running with Seeq 
(doubtful), meleeing with a mage, or using the 'martial artist' Bangaa jobs.
And assuming you took my tip before.

Werewolf, Roar. You should know the deal by now. The Lamashtu knows Night,
a learnable Blue Magick spell that sends all units (sans her) into sleep.
Or she can Poison/Toad you with some distance, but that's less of a problem
than having everyone asleep. The Liliths only got Kiss, a Doom/Charm spell-like
ability. Hope you got some Phoenix Downs ready.

If you can, take out the Lamashtu, but don't rush her if you can't. Lest you
take more damage than with everyone asleep. But definitely focus on her to
avoid the trouble; just remember she has Counter. The Baknamy got Critical:
Berserk, but by the time it reaches Critical HP, it should be dead by next 
turn anyway since they are so weak.

------------------------------A2-05: Graszton Cup------------------------------

|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  8    | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  8    | Cancellable? YES               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: Camoa Cup (A2-05)

* The Corsage of Corruption is an unlisted reward, and is also a summoning
item.

Objective: Defeat all foes in the entry bout!
Forbidden: Opportunity Commands
           - Opportunity commands are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Remember, enemies scale up with your level. Your only advantage here are the
abilities you've learned, since they don't get more at all.

On our first bout we have a Soldier (Yay...) with all 3 Rends. Plus Counter
and Defense Up.
A Blue Mage with Screech, Cornered, and Unction. Cornered is a skill that can
only be used when the user has one digit of life left, but it always hits for
999. So be careufl.
The White Mage has Cure and Esuna. For good or worse, he also has Turbo MP.
The Black Mage knows all basic spells and Fira. He also has Archer's Bane.

There's no real way to deal with this. I would probably deal with the Black
Mage first as usual, then it's up to you. Silence them if you can and you
deal with the Soldier and Blue Mage first. 

Also, remember you can't use Clan Privileges such as Bonus AP or the like
here if you usually stick to those (you can on the last bout), so pick Speed
or Power or whichever you prefer. 

Oh, the Smash Gauge resets between fights. So don't try "farming" Smash Gauge
Bonus Points to blow a summon on the last round or something.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the elimination bout!
Forbidden: Opportunity Commands
           - Opportunity commands are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Round two, and we got a ranged team.
The Gria Hunter has Sonic Boom and Vitals Shot; your worry is her Speed
Crush. Either get to her first, or it might get ugly.
The Viera Archer barely has Focus and Blackout, but she amazingly has Blink
Counter.
The Hume Archer has Leg Shot and Blackout, plus Blink Counter too.
The Hume Hunter has Advice, plus Beat Down and Blitz from the Fighter.
Both Hunters have Regenerate. 

Personally, I say the Gria dies first. After that, it doesn't really
matter much. They didn't attack me even once because they just kept doing
random stuff all the time.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the final bout!
Forbidden: Opportunity Commands
           - Opportunity commands are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
How did these guys get here? Anyway, you can use all of your Bonus whatever
clan privileges here if you want.

A Juggler, beware. He has Weapon Toss, Ring Toss (Stop), Molotov Cocktail
(Berserk) and Dagger Toss (Disable). 
Lord Grayrl has Thundara, Pickpocket and Strong-arm. 
The other Viking has Thunder and Pickpocket, plus Counter.
The Thief can steal Items and Gil, along with some loot. He has Cuisine too,
which is like the most broken healing ability if you ask me.

Get the Juggler quickly. Disable, slow, stop, whatever you can do, get to it.
He is fast, and he will try to do random crap to you if you don't get to him
first. 'sides the Juggler, the rest suck.

You'll get the summoning item for Mateus as a reward.
_______________________________________________________________________________
-------------------------A2-06: The Stone With No Name-------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
My teacher, Mauri discovered the message within the Stone with No Name, yet he
passed away before he could see his research through till the end. He once told
me that the "Stone with No Name is three stones in one, the magickal instrument
of antiquity that sealed off an Otherworld from our knowledge." He told me 
never to go near the Stone, nor pry too deep into its secrets. But...I would
see his research completed.
  Taura, Favored pupil
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 42 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|

|     Negotiation  39   |                                |

|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: The Sun Seal (A2-02)

Objective: Examine the Stone with No Name!
Forbidden: Restoring HP
           - Actions that restore HP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
As usual, this is a multi-staged battle.

You'll be dealing with 3 Luchorpan in this fight.
The middle one comes with Goblin Attack and Mutilate, the other two only
have Mutilate.
They all know some Green Magick; Sleep in the middle. Tranq and Leap on the
left. Blind and Silence on the right.
They have varying R/P abilities, such as Magick Counter, Bonecrusher, and
Return Fire. Coupled with Resistance Up, Death Strike, and Concentration.

Just rush the three enemies and easily take them down. 
Afterwards, examine all 3 stones. 

-------------

Objective: Defeat the demon wall!
Forbidden: Reaction Abilities
           - Reaction abilities are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Well, snap.

This fiend packs 3 special abilities.
Telega: Teleports your units in front of the wall and Immobilizes them.
Repel: Pushes forward, damaging and pushing back all of your units in the
way.
Shining Darkness: Hits units in front of the wall with Stone, Sleep, and
Immobilize.

Not a fun pack of skills if you ask me, plus it also has Absorb Damage, like
any good boss, and Impervious. The R-Ability restriction is not a good one if
you plan on following it, but it can be doable. If you have any Esunaga or
equivalent ability, you should be fine here, at least if the one who casts
it isn't turned to Stone.

Fortunately, the wall doesn't come with thousands of HP, only about 600 to
700, so a few turns should be more than enough, depending on your current
level of strength. It's also not very fast, so even if you get hit by one of
the ugly skills, you should have time to fix up. 

It's weak to Holy. And, I'm guessing here, that you can probably teleport
behind it with a set of Faerie Shoes to get that chest, or maybe even a 
Gria could fly over, but I had no time to check. 
_______________________________________________________________________________

---------------------------A2-08: An Unfamiliar Land---------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
I am an itinerant merchant, and on my travels have seen much of this world.

I wandered the Empire of Rozarria for a time, and upon coming to Jylland 
found myself uncustomarily surprised. What are these "Judges" that affix
themselves to the clans in this land?

They are no doubt different from the keepers of the law that I know- yet 
perhaps there is some connection?
  Aega, Traveling Merchant

PRECEDING QUEST: Graszton Cup (A2-05)

* The Raging Brooch is an unlisted, summoning item reward.

Objective: Defeat all foes in the entry bout!
Forbidden: Items
           - Items are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Remember there's no point in trying to level up. They scale up with you.

Defender with Hibernate and Mow Down.
Gladiator with the 3 elemental skills, and Strike Back.
Bishop with Water, Aero and Break. Plus Blood Price.
Templar with Piercing Cry, Rasp and Silence.
Cannoneer with Foresight, Mortar and Target.
Warrior with the 3 Rends and Counter.

The Warrior might bother you, but it's not that big of a deal. I say take out
the Templar, Cannoneer and Bishop as you can. Gladiator too if he starts
hurting you too much.

Remember to bring one or two units that can stall them, and to use Clan
Privileges to boost your Speed or Power, or what not.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the elimination bout!
Forbidden: Items
           - Items are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The Nu Mou Nobles now? As expected from Nu Mous, this is a very magickal
party, so watch out.

The Black Mage can counter Magicks, and has the 3 basic elementals, besides
Blizzara.
The White Mage only has Cure, Cura, and Esuna.
The Alchemist can use Protometeor, Rasp and Poison. 
The Illusionist has Prominence, Tempest, and Freezeblink, plus Blood Price.
The Time Mage has Haste and Reflect.
Finally, Madreth the Arcanist has Death, Syphon and Graviva. Plus Halve MP.

The Illusionist is never a good enemy, since they can hit your team entirely,
but they are slow. Problem is, since this one can Blood Price... Silence or
Disable it if you can. After that, it's up to you who you want down next.
I would say the White Mage to avoid healing, or the Time Mage.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the final bout!
Forbidden: Items
           - Items are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The Illusionist has Deluge, Rockfall and Wild Tornado, plus Halve MP.
The Dragoon only has the breaths, and Counter.
The Sniper can Doubleshot and has Beso Toxico, plus Regenerate.
The Sage only has Water and Aero, but he can use Items with Item Lore.
The Berserker can Scream, Furore and Smite of Rage. Counter included.
The Hunter has Vitals Shot, but borrows Power/Mind Crush.

The Illusionist is up front, so take it out quickly. Then it's up to you.
The Hunter might bother you with the crushes, but at least she doesn't have
Speed Crush. Otherwise, it doesn't really matter.

You'll get the summoning item for Adrammelech here.
_______________________________________________________________________________
-------------------------------A2-10: Odd Places-------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
There is a well about which strange rumors always spring up around this time of
year. They say it glows in the depth of night, when all lies sleeping, or that 
monsters not usually seen in these parts appear, even that there are sometimes
more wells, or fewer. Could someone please find out the truth?
  Typical Camoan Housewife
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 39 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Satisfy Petitioner       |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Camoa                |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 9450 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Fire Sigil x3              |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation  28   |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability  28   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Examine the wells!
Forbidden: Items
           - Items are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
There are only two wells, one at the northeast, and another around the west
side. 

You'll be facing bombs with Self-destruct (Blue mage, hint hint), some
Axebeaks, a Fire Drake with the usual, and an Adamantitan with Matra Magick
(Blue Mage hint again).

Bring whichever units you want. You don't even have to do much here if you 
want, you can just mad dash to the wells and use the Examine command. 
I found out by mistake that you only have to examine one to clear this...

Oh what the... You might end up in Sant D'alsa Bluff, Goug, or even in the
Neslowe Passage after this quest, each time opening up a new spot in the
region. So be sure to repeat this quest!
(Thanks to Edan Nadano for this note)
