--------------------------A2-12: The Way of the Meek---------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Someone's after me, I'm sure of it. But why?
I haven't a clue. I'm so very frightened.

Please, someone- protect me! If it's gil you want, I can pay!
  Dayvis the Meek
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 15 | Fee:  300 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Escort                   |
|   -                   | Members: 5                     |
|   -                   | Location: The Bisga Greenlands |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   - Defender          |   - 3400 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Superior Silk Thread x3    |
| Talents:              |   - Earth Stone x3             |
|     Negotiation  8    |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  8    | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Protect Dayvis and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Items
           - Items are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You'll be fighting a generic team. White Mage, Black Mage, Archer, Soldier,
White Monk and Warrior. 

Of course, you can only bring 5 units, and since items are forbidden, I 
strongly suggest someone adept in healing magicks (read: White Mage) so that
you can keep this guy alive, because he'll probably need it. 

Take the guys as they come, and careful with the archer, since she'll be
snipping you out from above. If you got a unit with a Jump of 4 or more, send
it up to take her out. 

I didn't have much trouble here, surprisingly. I had my Elementalist with
Fire Whip Disable as many as she could while Luso (going as Thief at the time)
climbed up and destroyed the White Mage and White Monk with just a bit of
help from my Black Mage throwing spells from below. I had them all pinned
back by then and they just started to fall dead. =D

PRECEDING QUEST: Moorabella Cup (A2-09)

* The Pin of Order is an unlisted reward, and also a summoning item.

Objective: Defeat all foes in the entry bout!
Forbidden: Buffs
           - Buffs are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Well, we are doing one-on-ones. So try to pick your best unit based on the
notes below. 

For the first guy, we fight a Blue Mage with Sandstorm, Bad Breath and
Dragon Force. Two of those are not good for us unless your unit is equipped 
with an immunity item. That, or you have the Debuffs Resistance CP.

Pick a fast, but strong unit for this one and slap a Speed Up CP to bring
this guy down in a blink.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the elimination bout!
Forbidden: Fire, Ice, Lightning
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire, ice, or lightning are
             forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Woah, they changed the law, for once.

You'll be pitted against a Red Mage on this fight. She comes armed with Fire,
Silence, and Doublecast. And also borrows Doubleshot from the Sniper. 
With Counter and Attack Up, be careful of her.

She is slightly faster than the Blue Mage from the previous round. Here, you
should be more interested in either speed or raw power to bring her down as 
your Clan Privilege.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the final bout!
Forbidden: Reaction Abilities
           - Reaction abilities are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yep, and you don't even get the chance to change your skills. 

This time it's a Master Monk. He comes with Dark Fist, Withering Strike, and
Cross-counter. Magick Counter shouldn't be a problem, and his Destroyer is
only a small boost.

This guy is faster than the Red Mage by a little. If you want the upper hand
in speed, a Speed Up CP should do it. Otherwise, if you have R-Abilities on,
you might want the raw power in an attempt to finish the fight before he gets
to melee you (he uses Dark Fist a lot).

Winning this will net you the Pin of Order. Summoning item for Hashmal.

PRECEDING QUEST: What Was Lost (A2-11)

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Targeting Self
           - Actions targeting the user are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The law includes using a Cure on someone else, but you getting affected as
well. Other skills such as Focus from the Archer and what not also fall on
this law.

You'll be fighting a White Monk with Air Render. A Warrior with 3 Rend skills,
an Archer with Leg Shot. A Soldier who is useless, a Berserker with the
usual array of skills and a... that's a familiar looking Thief... 

You all start on opposite sides of the map, so play it however you see fit.
If you have a Fusilier, he can start sniping them out well before you get into
melee range with them. Take them out as they come for you. 

Did I mention there are traps? And these guys apparently have an uncanny knack
for trying to get rid of ALL of your gil. I swear... I must have lost about
2000 or more Gil.
_______________________________________________________________________________
-----------------------------A2-15: Pirate Attack------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
We've received word that one of our merchant ships is being pursued by pirates.
They're likely after her cargo of gemstones. She's docked in Graszton Port-
someone go to her aid!
  Galerria Jewelers
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 13 | Fee:  300 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Satisfy Petitioner       |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Graszton             |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 2450 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Malboro Vine x2            |
| Talents:              |   - Foul Liquid x4             |
|     Negotiation  7    |   - Sturdy Bone x3             |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  7    | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat Lord Grayrl before he boards the ship!
Forbidden: Harming Humes
           - Actions that harm humes are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
It's up to you if you want to break the law. I would say you can get rid of
the Hume by using Counter or the like, but with him being an Archer...

The ship is, as you can see, on the left side. Left of your starting position,
so you can form a barricade between the ship and the enemies. Carve through 
them, ignore the Archer and Black Mage, and take out Grayrl.
Using debuffs without a source of damage, such as Silence from a Green Make or
Blackout from an Archer on the Humes will still count as a violation of the
law. (Thanks to lone_gamer)

The Black Mage only has the 3 basic spells. The Moogle Knight has Moogle
Guard only, a skill that raises his DEF and RES for that turn only. Grayrl
can cast Thundara, while the other Vikings have Thunder. They all got 
Strong-arm, and attack that deals damage and steals a random item.

--------------------------------A3-01: Goug Cup--------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Tourneys are Under Way!
The next tourney will be the Goug Cup.
Rules are as follows:
 -Maximum team size: 6
 -Bouts to be won: 3
 
  Tourney Guild - Goug Branch
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 26 | Fee:  400 | Days: 10 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|

|   -                   |   - Ewer of Darkness x1 *      |
| Talents:              |   -                            |
|     Negotiation       |   -                            |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  20   | Dispatch?                      |
|    Adaptability  20   | Cancellable?                   |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: Fluorgis Cup (A2-13)

* The Ewer of Darkness is an unlisted summoning item.

Objective: Defeat all foes in the entry bout!
Forbidden: Grouping
           - Ending the turn next to another unit is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yeah, this should be another easy cu-- what the hell...
A Chocobo Knight and a Rider of each freaking color sans Yellow.

They all have varying R-Abilities, from Counter, to Blur, to Blink Counter,
Archer's Bane, etc. 
Of course, being Riders, they have no sub-job skill set. Still, you know how
Chocobos act, they are going to Barrier everything in sight...

Bring some heavy-hitting units or a Blue Mage with Roar to break that stuff
off. And no, you can't steal the Chocobos for your own Chocobo Knight. 
Remember they are weak to water and lightning, all but the White Rider are
also weak to Holy (The Knight not included).

This law is no fun sometimes.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the elimination bout!
Forbidden: Grouping
           - Ending the turn next to another unit is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
There's a Green Mage with Oil and Sleep, borrowing Swallowtail and Nighthawk.
The Sniper has Doubleshot, Beso Toxico and Death Sickle. 
A Spellblade with Sleep, Slow and Confusion Blades.
Elementalist with Dire Whip (Disable), Bould Crush (Immobilize) and 
Sliprain (Slow).
Fencer with Swarmstrike, Swallowtail and Piercing Blow. + Counter.
White Mage with Cure and Cura.

Disable if you can the Elementalist or what not. If you want to go with the
law, your problems here are the Elementalist and the Spellblade, since they
can mess you up in one way or another.

The White Mage needs to die quickly too, and she's immune to some debuffs
(She has a Cachusha on). So you need to focus some ranged damage on her too.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the final bout!
Forbidden: Grouping
           - Ending the turn next to another unit is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Prima Donna, look at that.

Mayhew, a Raptor, has Soul Crush, Cyclone and Shield Bash. She has Reflex and
Monkey Grip. She wears a Cachusha.
Valentyne, the Spellblade, has Poison and Stun Blade. From the Sniper, she 
borrows Death Sickle. Counter and Doublehand.
Aluette the Fencer has Nighthawk, and Earth Heal from the Elementalist. She
has Regenerate on and Charged Attacks.
Lili, the Hunter, has Advice. And from Geomancy she has Shining Flare, Venom
Squall (Poison), Life's Embrace (Immobilize) and Earth's Embrace (Disable).
She also has Halve MP.

This is a 6vs4, assuming all your units are alive. Personally, I would try
and get Lili down fast. Her Embraces can put you in the spot, and no one
else can make your life difficult. Plus, if you are trying to follow the 
law, her Immobilize can get you in trouble unless the unit can knock back.

Win this, and the Ewer of Darkness is yours.
_______________________________________________________________________________
----------------------------A3-03: The Mirage Bunny----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
             -WANTED!-
Wanted: The Mirage Bunny, for 1,268 counts of footpadery and 4,232 counts of
eating and running.

Small in size, adorable in aspect, and quick to flee the scene of the crime.
  Jylland Defenders of the Peace
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 19 | Fee:  300 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Headhunt                 |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: The Galerria Deep    |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 5710 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Pagoda Wood x5             |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  11   | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  11   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: Wanted: Big Eyes (A2-16)

Objective: Defeat the Mirage Bunny!
Forbidden: Instruments and Books
           - Attacks with instruments and books are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You heard the guy.
The Drakes have their elemental breaths. The Thunder has Shockbolt, and the
Fire has Mesmerize, a Charm/Speed Down skill.
Companion to them, there are two Marhsmallows of Thunder and Fire who have
the level one spells of their kind; Thunder and Fire. 
The Wendigo has Blink Counter, a knock-back counter and the usual array of 
'raise my strength, delay your attack' skills.

The Mirage Bunny has a R-Ability you don't want to let it trigger unless you
got someone who can detect. If it goes into Critical HP, it will Vanish.
So try to make the finishing blow a good one. Otherwise, it can also charm.

Your objective the the bunny. Enemies will keep appearing, so might as well
make it quick. And there are traps.
_______________________________________________________________________________
--------------------------------A3-05: Loar Cup--------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Tourneys are Under Way!
The next tourney will be the Loar Cupo.
Rules are as follows:
 -Maximum team size: 6
 -Bouts to be won: 5
 
  Tourney Guild - Jylland Branch
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 34 | Fee:  500 | Days: 10 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      | Required month:
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |   Skyfrost
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Moorabella           |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   - Lanista           |   - 4290 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Ring of the Wheel x1*      |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  28   | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  28   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: Goug Cup (A3-01)

* Ring of the Wheel is an unlisted, summoning item reward.

Objective: Defeat all foes in the entry bout!
Forbidden: Using MP
           - Actions that consume MP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yep, 5 fights. Isn't that great? Laws will be changing, so read on ahead to 
plan a good team.
 
It's up to you on how to deal with the law. Since the third match's law is 
items. Of course, breaking the law isn't that much of a deal if you do it at
the end while the last enemy is stopped...

Let's see... There's a Warrior with Body Slam, Greased Lightning, and 
Lifetap. Plus Blink Counter.
The White Monk has Roundhouse, Air Render, and Earth Render.
The Hume Thief has Steal Items/Gil, and borrows Leg/Arm Shot from the Archer.
He's dual wielding melee weapons, so he has to come close to disable you.
The Moogle Thief has Steal Gil and Loot Lv. 3, Moogle Lance/Rush from the 
Knight and uses Doublehand.
The Moogle Black Mage has all three -ra and Blizzaga, plus Geomancy. Beware.

If you are following the law, a Juggler will make your life pretty here. 
Or units who can play with statuses, as you have to make the most out of your
turns to make this work, and if they are disabled (by one mean or another), 
you get more time to deal damage since you don't have to focus on healing.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the second bout!
Forbidden: Actions by Viera
           - Viera may only move and perform basic attacks.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
4 enemies on this one, but don't get cocky.
Lord Grayrl, a Viking, has Thundaga, Strong-arm, and Pillage. He can Absorb
Damage and has Immunity.
That other Viking has Thundara, Tsunami and Pickpocket. He can Counter.
The Thief can Steal Gil and borrows Cuisine (the broken heal), Chocobo Rush
and Catnip (Berserk) from the Animist. Nevermind his MP Shield.
Finally, a Juggler with Weapon Toss, Ring Toss (Stop), Molotov Cocktail 
(Berserk) and Smile Toss (Quicken). He also has Critical: Quicken to boot.

If you can get ahead of time from the Juggler, try to pin him down or Disable
him. Even a Blind works, to avoid all of his status effects. None of them are
resistant or whatever to an element, so have fun. 

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the third bout!
Forbidden: Items
           - Items are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Hope you came prepared. 
The Sage has Scathe, Esunaga, Gigaflare and Bio. He can Pierce right through
any Reflect too!
The Illusionist comes with Star Cross, Stardust, and Wild Tornado. And of 
course, he has Blood Price.
The White Mage has Cura and Blind, Silence and Sleep borrowed from the Green
Mage.
Finally, the Black Mage has all -ra, along with Thundaga and Blizzaga. He has
Halve MP too.

If you can Silence, go ahead and do it. Other statuses that will deny their 
magick are also useful, such as Berserk. They are all immune, take half-damage 
or absorb one different element, so be careful.

Take care of the Illusionist first, at least with a Silence. The next turn get
the Black Mage, or vice-versa. If the White Mage bothers you, take her out, but
you have some time with the Sage. Both Scathe and Gigaflare cost a bit.

Also, try to keep your units spread out of course.

It's funny how my character said less magicks flying around would have been
nice, yet they never even managed to lay off a cast...

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the elimination bout!
Forbidden: <50 Damage
           - Dealing <50 damage is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This might be a law you won't have fun with, especially since we are back 
against 6 enemies. Remember, you have to deal OVER 50 damage to keep the law
up.

You are facing an Illusionist in the front line with Deluge and Rockfall. He
has Absorb MP and Halve MP. 
The Dragoon can Jump, use Lancet and has Ice Breath. He can Counter too.
The Sniper has Doubleshot and Marksman's Spite, borrowing Protect and Shell 
from the Green Mage. She has Concentrate on.
The Sage comes with Water, Aero and Esunaga. Next!
The Berserker has Hone Senses, Furore, and Ground Shaker. Nothing too serious,
including that Counter.
The Gria Hunter has Sonic Boom and Vitals Shot, plus Regenerate. 

If you can (and should), bring that Illusionist down quick. You should have two
turns unless you give him enough MP. 
Afterwards, it's up to you. If you hit the Sniper, try to finish the job
quickly, or she might pull a Spite on you.
The Dragoon can hit hard with the Jump, but a Blind should help you. Remember
the law here and try to strike from advantageous points (read: behind).

My Fusilier couldn't keep up with this one. :/

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the final bout!
Forbidden: Targeting Distant Units
           - Actions targeting units two or more tiles away are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Will you look at that. House Bowen!

Veis is quick, damn quick! She sits at 107 speed, has Shadowbind (Stop) and
Ague (Slow). Plus Doubleshot and Vanish from the Sniper.
Lola, the Ravager, has Overpower, Tenacity, and Blast Wave. Add Counter too.
Tweigel, the Illusionist, comes with Stardust and Wild Tornado. He also knows
Stop and Slow. Plus has Blood Price.
Bowen himself, being a fighter, has Rush, Wild Swing, and Backdraft. He has
Bonecrushed and Doublehand.

Odds are, Veis will get to act maybe even twice before you do. For this fight,
my recommendation lies in taking out the Illusionist or Veis, whichever you
prefer. They can both put nasty statuses on you, or Tweigel can hit everyone.
Lola and Bowen should be left for last. Also, Bowen has an Orb of Minwu.

Veis is not very smart though. She Stopped then Slowed the same unit. o.o

Win this one, and the Ring of the Wheel is finally yours. Phew.
_______________________________________________________________________________

A young girl, Florah exhibits a strange talent for controlling vegetative
life.

|        Teamwork  13   | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  13   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUESTS: Wanted: Tonberrion (A3-10)
                  Wanted: The Mirage Bunny (A3-03)

Objective: Defeat Florah!
Forbidden: >50 Damage
           - Dealing >50 damage is forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
One of those crazy rules.

Florah is a Geomancer, she can probably Immobilize you with one of her
Geomancy skills. If it's raining, she might be able to Poison you.
...But I doubt she'll have to. She has 3 Malboro-type enemies with her.
Now, as sick as the idea of a Malboro singing might be, they can. And it
increases their attack and gives them Regen. Rejoice.

Stay away from their breaths. Deal however you see fit with the law (even
breaking it, if need be) or use low-hitting attacks constantly aimed at
her to finish this quickly. I'm not sure what I did wrong here, because unless
my math is wrong, the law means "Do not do damage greater than 50" and I broke
it with a 18 hit. o.o

----------------------------A2-16: Wanted: Big Eyes----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
             -WANTED!-
Wanted: Big Eyes, for 3 counts of the disruption of business and 25 counts of
inducing shock.

Big Eyes possess a single large eyeball and generally travel in packs.
  Jylland Defenders of the Peace
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 17 | Fee:  300 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Headhunt                 |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: The Aldanna Range    |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 2940 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Prime Tanned Hide x1       |
| Talents:              |   - Wyrm Carapace x1           |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  9    | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  9    | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: Wanted: Lang Bros. (B2-09)

Objective: Defeat the Big Eyes!
Forbidden: Knockback
           - Actions that knock the target back are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
My favorite law is back. If you really wanna win this, either use skills all
the time so you don't knockback, or flank the enemy against another unit or
a 'wall'.

Careful on this one. The Bloody Orbs (the small ones) can Silence you, as well
as drain your HP to replenish their own. 
The problem comes with the Plague (the big ones), who can Petrify you, as well
as Confusing, or doing a Slow/Charm skill. If it's like usual (and it seems
it works that way), their Gazes can only work if you are looking at them. 
They can hit hard if you give them your back, but you weight out the
consequences for not doing against doing it.

I would suggest taking out the Bloody Orbs on your way to the Plague, then
quickly burn them down before taking whichever small ones you got left. 
The Bloody Orbs are weak against Earth and Holy. The Plague are weak to the
latter.

PRECEDING QUEST: Death March (B2-01)

Objective: Uphold the law and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Not Moving 1 Tile
           - Each unit must move exactly 1 tile before ending its turn.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yep, retarded law time. Bring units that know some degree of ranged attacks.
Though in the end, the enemies are going to close in on you right away...

They have a Parivir with Iai Blow. A Soldier with the usual crap.
A Juggler with Smile Toss, Weapon Toss and Dagger Toss. A Fusilier with 
Blindshot and Silenceshot *groan*. An Elementalist with Fire Whip (Disable),
Shining Air (Blind) and Sliprain (Slow). And of course, a Trickster.

I would worry about the Elementalist since she can mess you up with Disable,
or the Fusilier. After that, it doesn't really matter.

------------------------------A3-09: Ordalia Cup-------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  39   | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  39   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: Loar Cup (A3-05)

* The Tainted Cufflink is an unlisted, summoning item for Cuchulainn.

Objective: Defeat all foes in the entry bout!
Forbidden: Knockback
           - Actions that knock the target back are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You know the deal with the law. Attack using skills, or be careful when you 
melee, flank the enemy if you must.

Ninja with Fire Veil (Confuse) and Shimmering Blade from Parivir (Confuse as 
well). He Counters and has a Shield.
Fighter with Backdraft and Doublehand.
Dragoon with Fire Breath and Meltdown from the Defender.
Black Mage with Fire and Firaga, and then oddly enough, Water from Sage.
Summoner with Ifrit, Fire Whip (Disable) and White Flame; a healing magick.
The Chocobo Rider is on a Red Chocobo, he has Choco Meteor and Beak, along
with Counter and Death Strike.

This is a fire-heavy fight, as you can see. Bring your standard tourney party
and get to it. Be sure to look below to see what you are fighting though. 

As usual with such long tourneys, you better bring someone who can heal, like
a Summoner or a White Mage; otherwise you might have trouble.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the second bout!
Forbidden: Buffs
           - Buffs are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The Master Monk has Dark First, Withering Strike, and Cross-counter. He has
Bonecrushed and carries an Orb of Minwu.
The Gladiator has all 3 elemental techniques and Bonecrusher.
The Templar has Rasp and Silence, from the Bishop he has Dispel, and
Bonecrusher too.
The Fighter has Wild Swing and Back Draft, plus Dual Wield.
The Berserker has Scream, Furore, and Smite of Rage.
The Ravager has Tenacity, Full Assault, and Sweeping Spin. Last three have
Critical: Berserk.

The Master Monk is the quickest, and can't be debuffed too well, so try and
take him out quickly. 
It's up to you who you want do disable or the like to give yourself some
time. 

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the third bout!
Forbidden: Solitude
           - Ending the turn without a unit in an adjacent tile is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yes, I hate this law.

The Thief is more of an Archer, has Steal Gil, but also Arm/Leg Shots. He has
Counter and Concentration on.
The White Mage has Cura, Refresh and borrows Fire Veil from Ninja. Oddly 
enough, he's Dual Wielding.
The Defender comes with Whirl Burst, Defend, and Rend Weapon.
Black Mage has Thundaga, along with Slow and Stop from Time Mage. No useful MP
abilities.
The Sniper has Doubleshot, Beso Toxico, and Death Sickle, plus Regenerate.
The Moogle Knight has Moogle Attack, Lance, and Rush. Along with Critical: 
Haste.

The Time Mage's Stop is a big concern. But also remember they have a White 
Mage. Try to Silence one or the other, and kill the one up. Afterwards, the
Sniper or the Thief can be trouble too, since they can hurt you or disable
you. The Moogle Knight and Defender aren't big trouble, unless you are really
wary about Rend Weapon.

That's about it.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the elimination bout!
Forbidden: Not Moving
           - Each unit must move at least 1 tile before ending its turn.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Prima Donna again. 

Mayhew, as usual, only has Soul Crush, Cyclone, and Shield Bash. She has 
Reflex and Monkey Grip.
Valentyne has Stun and Maim Blades, along with Death Sickle from Sniper.
She comes with Counter and Doublehand.
Aluette has Shadowstick, Nighthawk, and Earth Heal from the Elementalist. She
has Regenerate and Charged Attacks too.
Lili only has Advice from Hunter, but borrows Shining Flare, Venom Squall, 
Life's Embrace, and Earth's Embrace. She has Return Fire and Halve MP.

It's a 6vs4, assuming none of your units are down. 

This is possibly one of the easiest fights, even if some of them have status-
immunity items equipped.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes in the final bout!
Forbidden: Restoring MP
           - Actions that restore MP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
*Groan*
Three Parivirs. Center one has Shimmering, Skyfury and Hoarfrost Blades.
Right one has Wind Slash, Iai Blow, and Skyfury Blade.
Left one has Wind Slash, Blade Bash, and Hoarfrost Blade. They have a 
combination of Strike Back and Counter.
The left Ninja has Wood, Earth and Water Veils, plus Items. He has Dual Wield.
The right Ninja has Throw, along with Prominence, Tempest, and Freezeblink 
from the Illusionist, but also has Dual Wield.
The Assassin has Shadowbind, Aphonia and Ague.

Your priorities, unless you are status-protected, is to take care in one way
or another of the Assassin (Shadowbind), and the Parivir with Hoarfrost.
This is one fight you don't want to be debuffed with Stop or Slow. If you can 
do something to hit them all or a lot of them, good.

Most of them are immune to Stop due to their Black Garbs. But you can still
Disable them.

This is the last battle, and you shouldn't have much trouble, assuming half
of your team isn't dead yet.

With this, you get Cuchulainn's summoning item.

¯¯¯¯¯
Oh yes, a Tonberry. On a time limit. With a retarded law.

If you've done some of the Clan Trials, you should know these guys already.
They have to Stalk someone first, and then they can let all hell loose on that
character. And as usual, the standard Tonberries have Critical: Quicken.

The King however, has Absorb Damage. And his Voodoo skill, whatever that is.

Bring your fastest units. Haste yourself if you can. Remember that you can
avoid casualties by moving your 'stalked' units away from the tonberries.
Remember they take half-damage from pretty much all elements.

|        Teamwork  39   | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  39   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: Death March, II (A3-08)

Objective: Uphold the law and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Not Moving 3 Tiles
           - Each unit must move exactly 3 tiles before endings its turn.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
In this short arena, you'll be facing a Ninja with Gold Veil, Water Veil,
Counter and Doublehand.
A Trickster with Suggestion, Hypochondria, Traumatize, and Concentration.
Time Mage with Haste, Quicken, and oddly enough, Magick Up.
Green Mage with Tranq, Blind, Silence, and Spellbound.
Assassin with Last Breath, Aphonia, Ague, Counter, and Doublehand.
Elementalist with Fire Whip, Shining Air, Sliprain, and Magick Up.

Both the Ninja and the Assassin have a Black Garb on, making them immune to
Stop. The thing about upholding the law here is just watching your steps.
Don't let yourself get surrounded, spread your units if possible, and always
try to keep a route out.

Using a Elementalist a lot, myself, I know how troublesome they can be 
sometimes when they stick so many debuffs on you, so careful with their
Elementalist.
The Ninja and Assassin aren't strong phisically, so they shouldn't give you
too much trouble. The Time Mage might be a nuisance, Silence him if you can,
or take care of him quickly before he can Haste.
Same with the Green Mage, though she isn't that much of a problem.
The Trickster is just filling, if you ask me.
_______________________________________________________________________________

 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: Wanted: Tonberrion (A3-10)

Objective: Defeat Gaitsnipe!
Forbidden: Ranged Weapons
           - Attacks with bows, greatbows, guns, hand-cannons, and cards are
             forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
How fitting. A purely ranged battle in which we can't respond in kind.

Oh well. We'll be facing a Hunter with Vitals Shot, borrowing Rend Power/Magick
from our Soldier friend. And has Concentration.
The Archer has the Leg/Arm Shots, along with, surprisingly, Rush.
That Cannoneer has Foresigh, Buckshot and Mortar under the belt.
The Sniper has Doubleshot, Beso Toxico (Poison) and Death Sickle (Doom), plus
Regenerate...
The Flintlock... 'sides Prime, he has Cure and Protect Cannons ready.
Last but not least, the Fusilier has Confuse, Blind and Silence shots.

All in all, this doesn't look good from our starting point. Of course, you
can still blow magick at them. Try to move as fast as you can, Haste if you can
and want, but focus fire them down. They'll be running a lot, so Immobilize
might be handy. That, or units that can hit the entire battlefield (Scholars
or Illusionists, if you have them). 

Oh, and beware of the traps.

front of the gem to the furthest left on the back. And one to the left of
the rightmost gem at the top.

PRECEDING QUEST: Wanted: Gaitsnipe (A3-14)

Objective: Defeat Icicle Ark!
Forbidden: Fire
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
There's a Gladiator with Blizzard Tackle and Ice Breath.
The Parivir has Wind Slash and of course, Hoarfrost Blade. Nevermind his
Ninjutsu. He can Counter too.
The Illusionist, besides having Blood Price, can cast Freezeblink and Deluge.
That Summoner has Blood Price too. She has Shiva, Shining Air and Sliprain from
the Elementalist.
The Scholar of course has Rime Bolt Tome (Ice) along with Water and Aero.
Finally, is a crappy Moogle Black Mage with only Blizzard and Blizzara, yes.

If you want to be cheap, gear up with ice resistant (or absorbing) gear and
laugh throughout the battle.
Otherwise, the Illusionist might give you trouble, along with the Scholar.
Only the Gladiator can absorb Ice, the Summoner is resistant to it.
Though the Black Mage is resistant to water, and the summoner immune.

Pick some good units and head for the Illusionist and Scholar. Remember they
have Blood Price, so you don't have a few turns to get to them.

flower of doom.

¯¯¯¯¯
Don't you just love that law?

The Moogle Mark I is a Knight with Attack, Lance and Rush. He can also Blink

and Pierce at the ready. Guess what element he can absorb.

Mark IV is a Rider with Choco Beak and Recharge.

They aren't THAT fearsome, but they can still toy around with you, especially
the Juggler. If you can, take care of him first. Also, don't worry about the
law, seriously. 

There are a few traps around too.
...They remind me of the Rainbow Rangers from Disgaea...

Yes, Rouge (Ruuj, Rouge, same deal) is lv44. 
From Red Magick, she has Doublecast and Silence.

even bother bringing in debuffers.

to her. You'll get a Sequencer for your victory too.

PRECEDING QUEST: Wanted: Moogle Rangers (A4-10)

Objective: Defeat the Mutadragons!
Forbidden: Targeting All Units
           - Actions targeting all units at once are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Let's see... All of them carry Orb of Minwu and Empyreal Armbands, so don't
bother with debuffs.

The Gaiasea Warder has all 3 breath weapons, along with Bonecrusher and 
Destroyer. Weak to Ice, Absorbs Fire.

The Flaresky Warder has the next 3 dragon skills, Fireball, Ice Orb, and
Lightning. Blink Counter and Destroyer too. Weak to Holy, Half Damage from
Fire/Ice/Lightning, and Absorbs Dark.

The Lightland Warder knows Rend Defense, Hell Assault, and borrows Holy
Strike. He comes with Absorb Damage and Destroyer. Weak to Earth.

Bring whatever you prefer. There's no perfect combination to abuse their 
weaknesses, but as long as you attack mostly with skills, you shouldn't have
much trouble with their counter abilities. That's about all I can say for
these guys.

Oh yes, traps, you know the deal.

PRECEDING QUEST: Red King of Cinquleur (A4-10)

Objective: Defeat Blue King Bliu!
Forbidden: Using MP
           - Actions that consume MP are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Oh yeah, you better take him out quick or have items on stock.
He has Quake, an AoE Earth magick and Expose Weakness, a DEF/RES lowering
skill.

And, he packs Magick Frenzy from the Seer job. Coupled with his moderately 
high Attack, you don't want several of your units getting caught in it are
the same time.

Did I mention he's Dual Wielding and has Counter? He absorbs Dark and is 
immune to almost everything as well. Pack some hard-hitting units and lay it
to him. Yeah, he nailed my Chocobo Rider with a Triple-KO. XD

...Also, did they try to make Bliu sound like Bleu (Blue in French) like with
Ruuj (Rouge, red in the same language) or what? Silly SE... Oh, there are
traps too. See if you can lure him around.

You'll get a Peytral from this one.

PRECEDING QUEST: Blue King of Cinquleur (A4-15)

Objective: Defeat Green King Verre!
Forbidden: Restoring HP
           - Actions that restore HP are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Again with the French (Vert, Verre).

Green King? She should be the Black King or something because of her
Assassination skills. Never you mind she has Tranq (and only that) from the
Green Mage, she has Last Breath (Insta-KO), Nightmare and Ague.

Plus, with Tank, she sports some pretty heavy defense and has Blink Counter.

Again, bring your best units to lay it to her; you can't recover HP without
breaking the law here and since she's immune to everything as usual, you
wanna take her down fast.

Her normal attack is lightning-based, and her Mirror Mail gives her reflect.
Play accordingly. And there are a few traps around. 

As a tip, you can dispel her buff with whatever means, including Roar or 
Eerie Sound Wave from the Blue Mage. There's a high chance she'll recast 
Tranq on the next turn, effectively wasting time. (Thanks to Nikki Nicholai)

Oh, guess that's why she isn't the Black King... You'll get a Save the Queen
out of this one.

PRECEDING QUEST: Green King of Cinquleur (A5-03)

Objective: Defeat Black King Nware!
Forbidden: Debuffs
           - Debuffs are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Ho-ho. More French. (Nware = Noir, Black of course)

A Moogle Black Mage. But don't be fooled! This guy, besides actually having
some decent Life (Very decent) and mildly good speed, will completely absorb
the three basic elements; and he knows the -ga versions of all three elemental
spells of course. Plus Toadsong.

Besides, he has Reflex, rendering him immune to most physical attacks unless 
you are using a skill to attack. You might wish to either protect yourself 
against the elements if you want a painless (or as painless as it can be) 
battle, or protect yourself against the Toad debuffs. 

Either way, there's no easy way to look at this. Oh, the traps won't help much
unless he triggers them of course. If you can drain his MP, this fight should
be a bit easier, since his spells HURT.

You'll get a Crown Scepter out of this one.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
There we go with that law.
Also, if you ask me, Blanch sounds more female than male. And yes, it's Blanc
for French this time.

This time we got a (obviously) White Mage with Cura and Refresh. He also
borrows Scathe, Gigaflare, and Bio from the Sage. He has MP Shield, but also
Blood Price, for better or worse.

He has 3 accessories on him, if you wanna try your luck at stealing. Otherwise,
you shouldn't have that much trouble on this one. His Scathe REALLY hurts, 
by the way. And, as luck would dictate, there are a few Charm traps around.

Skills that lower Speed can be useful here. Or heck, even a Rend for his
Magick. 

You'll get a Cheer Staff out of this one.

PRECEDING QUEST: The Next Step (B1-04)

Objective: Protect your charge and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Solitude
           - Ending the turn without a unit in an adjacent tile is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
I hate this law, but meh.

The Seer has Cura, Esuna and Fira, with Blood Price.
The Scholar has Thunder Flare Tome and Shadow Shade Tome.
The Time Mage has Slow, Reflect, and most importantly, Stop. And Critical:
Quicken.
The Black Mage has Fira and Firaga, coupled with Geomancy.
The Green Mage has Oil and Sleep.
The Arcanist has Gravity, Lv. 3 Dark, and Lv. ? Shadowflare. 

The Scholar on your side has Force and Mad Scientist, along with Item + Item
Lore.

Bring whichever units you want. If they can work from afar, like an Illusionist
then it's even better if you want to keep the law up. Try to get the other
Scholar down quick. After that, just kill whatever you want.

|     Negotiation  8    |   - Dragon Bone x2             |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  8    | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: Beetle in a Haystack (B1-05)

Objective: Weaken Goud and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Ice
           - Weapons and abilities that use ice are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Pay attention to the objective.
To make it easier, leave Goud to the very end. Kill everything else first,
then try to just bring his HP down. Killing Goud (as it happened to me once
'cause he decided to attack right before I killed the last one, and countered,
killing it) will end in failure. 

Aside from Goud, a Fire Drake, you'll be fighting two Thunder Drakes and a
Werewolf. In case you don't remember, the Werewolf will Roar you out of
whatever buffs you have (and if you have a Blue Mage, learn the skill).
Of course, you can work it to your advantage if you want to make it lose its
turn.

The Drakes only got their Breath weapons, which are just like your Dragoon's,
if you got one. Hit the square in front, then the next three to the side. 
Goud has Dragon Force as well, though.
Remember that while Goud is weak to Ice, you are forbidden from using it.

Did I mention there are traps around?
_______________________________________________________________________________

PRECEDING QUEST: Wayward Drake (B1-06)

Objective: Protect Chari and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Fire, Ice, Lightning
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire, ice, or lightning are
             forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The tri-fecta is out. Not much of a problem, really.
Just be sure to check your equipment as well.

There are only 3 enemies, all of them can Silence or Poison you with their
skills. Chari, the Bloody Orb and the one you gotta protect, has Vampire,
so she (she?) can heal herself a bit with all the beating she's going to
take. You might still want to consider a White Mage for ranged healing.

Otherwise, there's not much else to this fight. Focus fire them down one by
one so Chari takes less damage. Oh, yes, the Floating Eyes are immune to basic
archer attacks. Mind you, she will move in to attack. So hurry up.
_______________________________________________________________________________

I was taking one of the cockatrices for a walk in the Menagerie grounds when it
flew into a frenzy and lashed out at me with its beak. 
Before I knew what had happened, it had fluttered over the pen fence and run
away. 
This is the second time one of our monsters has gotten away from me in recent
days, and if Mr. Grann finds out, I won't hear the end of it!
I need someone to recover the cockatrice before anyone notices it's missing. It
answers to the name of Okta.
  Keeper Lloyd, Grann's Menagerie

PRECEDING QUEST: The White of Its Eyes (B1-07)

Objective: Weaken Okta and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Bladed Weapons
           - Attacks with knives, swords, blades, sabers, katana, axes,
             knightswords, greatswords, and broadswords are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Look at that. You might have to make it a ranged battle.

Okta is there along the center of the Cockatrices.
Of course, you must be careful with it. Avoid hitting it if you want or
whatever, and take the rest down. If Okta takes damage for whatever reason
(Counter, an AoE chance to hit so many you couldn't just pass it up, etc.), 
throw a heal its way in the meanwhile. 

Don't try weakening it first then getting rid of the rest. Do it the other
way around to save you some headaches. There are a few traps around too.

¯¯¯¯¯
This one is an easy quest, if you know what you are doing. Remember the 
instructions? If not, read the description above.
 
Basically, kill TWO DREAMHARES (Leave the Hoppy Bunnies alone) and then talk 
to the Bishop there. Just stand next to him and the special command should
show up. Tell him you've gathered the stuff AFTER you kill TWO of the white
bunnies; Dreamhares, if you must. There's no problem in killing Hoppy Bunnies,
but it's not required. 

Do note more enemies keep coming.

That's it.

Objective: Procure the requested ingredients!
Forbidden: Knockback
           - Actions that knock the target back are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
My favorite law.
This time you are hunting Malboro, You gotta take down 4 of 'em, and
since you are going to be seeing too many Tomatoes, kill 'em as you see fit
to open up space for the Malboro.
Aside from them, you have Alraune and Deadly Nightshades. I would almost
suggest not having Counter-like R-Abilities equipped to avoid killing them
by accident. That, or keep a White Mage around to heal them if need be.

Especially, since Malboros aren't so common in here. Once you got all four
of 'em, talk to the Bishop.

PRECEDING QUEST: Foodstuffs: Aroma (B1-11)

Objective: Procure the requested ingredients!
Forbidden: Fire, Ice, Lightning
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire, ice, or lightning are
             forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Oh yes, this is a fun one. Hope you got Elementalists, Sages, or the like to
deal magick damage other than those 3 elements. 

We'll be hunting for Yellow Jelly. Of course, accompanying them are Red
Marshmallows and Ice Flans. 
You should know how they operate by now. They all have the 2nd level spells
of their element, by the way. Their Acid can be fun, especially if you have
a Beastmaster to abuse it. XD

Do note that if you have a Blue Mage, the Red Marshmallows can throw Unction
at you to learn it.

Kill 3 Yellow Jellies and talk to the Bishop when done. Remember, you can take
care of the other kind without problem. Just be sure to take 3 Jellies before
talking to the guy...
_______________________________________________________________________________
-----------------------------B1-14: Our Playground-----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Some old creeps moved in our playground!
The place with cool old well! Now my old woman's sayin' it's a good opportunity
for me to study and stuff. Can you believe it!? Someone drive those creeps
away!
  Obuta, Camoa Kids Leader

                       | Rank:  7 | Fee:  200 | Days: 20 |

|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |

| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Ice
           - Weapons and abilities that use ice are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Nice lineup. A Berserker, 2 Ranger, a Grenade, Yellow Jelly and, of all
things, a Malboro.

Beware the plant's breath, the Grenade doesn't have Self-Destruct, but it
absorbs Fire and can target itself with Fire. Much like the Jelly using
Thunder. 
The Berserker can raise his Critical Hit chance along with his Evasion.
The Rangers can set Silence and MP reducing traps. 

It's a straight-forward fight otherwise. Since you start near a Jelly and
the Grenade-Ranger combo, take them out first if possible before the rest
of the units catch up to you.

-----------------------------B1-15: Mushroom Chef------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
I am a mushroom chef by trade. Know any dish with fungi in it? I cook it. So 
you can imagine why I'd want one of those fabled Baptiste mushrooms. Careful,
though, there are poisonous mushrooms growing in the same place. I'm sure 
you'll be fine, of course. Just try them when you're out there and you'll know 
the difference.
  Sawah, Mushroom Chef

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The quest isn't hard. I mean, sure, you can't do back-attacks for added 
damage, but even so.
On that note, be careful. I can't quite remember if using magick from 
behind counted towards breaking the law (I think it did).

Kill the enemies if you wish first, you'll be dealing with sprites (They can
Slow), Dreamhares (buff attack and stuff), and Lamia/Lilith (can sleep
everyone). I'd say the Lamia/Lilith go down first, but they are the furthest
away, so hack your way through to them then start testing every mushroom.
In my case, it was the one right ahead of the team. I barely got to fight. XD

PRECEDING QUEST: Throw Down (B2-03)

Objective: Defeat Klesta!
Forbidden: Back Attack
           - Attacking a unit form behind is forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Good ol' Klesta. 
He comes better than ever. Can hit all units, raises his own attack, or just
hurt those in a nice area. And as usual, it has a damn lot of HP. And yes,
while Piston Drop hurts, it's AWESOME when you have a lot of units (I saw 4
in my case) next to him with Counter, since they will all counter. 

Bring your best damage-dealers and then consider someone who can heal, or 
whether to blow items. This is not a speed battle. It doesn't matter who
lands the finisher, just get the beast down.

Support House Bowen by keeping them alive if you want. After bringing it down
to half, it will escape ag-- Oh no, not this time. You came prepared, right?
Oh yeah, time to finish this guy off.

Objective: Defeat Klesta!

           - Actions that harm a lower level unit are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This law generally isn't a problem... Unless you are severely overleveled.
Fortunatelly, Klesta is lv16 here. 

You'll be bringing 3 units to the fight only. A Ravager (Probably the first
Gria you'll see), an Illusionist and a Fighter. I'm pretty sure you know
how Klesta attacks and how the AI behaves, so I hope you got a White Mage
for some nice healing, or deal with it, let them die and finish it yourself.
Oh, did I mention he got a new attack? He hits everything in front of him. 
EVERYTHING. So try to reach around him if you are meleeing its feather behind.

Otherwise, stack up on ranged strong damage (Black Mages, or even Hunters if
you got some good skills for them, which I doubt). Did you notice how much
HP it has? Have fun. He doesn't run away until you "finish" it off.

PRECEDING QUEST: It's a Trap! (B2-08)

Objective: Destroy the traps in 3 rounds!
Forbidden: Targeting All Units
           - Actions targeting all units at once are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
As usual of these missions, you can't lure the enemies into the traps, since
they all have Avoid Traps.

With a time limit, we gotta make it fast. The Baknamies as usual are meh.
Magick Hammer and Goblin Attack.
The Luchorpan has Mutilate, which might actually hurt you.
The Tonberry, as usual, must Stalk you before being able to do anything
else.

Pick your units, get them some backup if you want and go wild. Units that
can push back might be useful as enemies might step on the traps.
Oh, the purple traps will Charm you. =3

¯¯¯¯¯
Head to the location, which is the hut of the mage Lezaford. He will give
you the Clan Note "Concerning Judges". That's it. Feel free to read it if
you wish. =D

¯¯¯¯¯
You know the deal, spring the traps, you win.

Baknamy, as usual, are pretty weak. They all have Goblin Attack, the Luchorpan

first before working on the traps, otherwise you might end up with a weak
character they can actually get to.

And no, I know what you are thinking. You can't lure THEM into the traps.
They won't spring for whatever reason.

PRECEDING QUEST: Wanted: Gilmunto (B2-06)

Objective: Defeat the Lang Brothers!
Forbidden: Actions by Nu Mou
           - Nu mou may only move and perform basic attacks.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
A Thief, Fighter, Black Mage and Archer. Those are your enemies. 
As usual, Black Mages should be considered one of your biggest threats, since
they can hurt several of your units in one turn if possible, and they
hit nicely. Of course, all of the others are on the way.

Carve your way, silence the mage if you can and then defeat it. The Fighter
along with the Archer should be your next kills, then finally, the Thief. 

The Mage only has the three basic spells. The Fighter can use Air Render,
Archer has Focus, etc. Everyone has Arts of War as their sub-job skills.
There are traps, too.

You get rewards based on the total amount of Gil you've thrown in after 
repeated clears of this quest. The rewards are:

10,000 = Elixir
20,000 = Eureka Crystal
30,000 = Grimoire Stone
40,000 = Dark Matter
50,000 = Einherjarium
60,000 = Orichalcum
70,000 = Demon Feather
80,000 = Ribbon

Speaking in Gil terms, of course. Good luck with that Ribbon.

¯¯¯¯¯
There's only one enemy, and that's the Ivory Wyrm, who is lv25. Hope your
units are not of a too high level for this one.

The Wyrm can use Sleet. A skill that Dooms a target while putting it to Sleep
as well, otherwise, it has an Ice Breath similar to that of a Dragoon and
an Ice Orb, a ranged ice attack.

Syrenead is a green mage who can only use Leap. And most probably, she will
use that to get to the Wyrm faster. So haul tail; he takes half damage from
the 3 basic elements and is weak to holy. There are a few traps around. If
you are lucky, you might get him to one of the half-HP ones. =D

Fencer set. The Hume Archer has 2 rends and Blackout.
The Black Mage only knows Blizzard and Blizzara.
The Warrior might hurt, with Doublehand; and it can always Rend you. Not to 
mention he'll heal himself for what he hits.
And that Soldier is a Paladin wannabe. With Counter, of course.

You don't start with quite a distance. Bring anything you got ranged and snipe
them out while they come to you or quickly crash their party. 
They shouldn't give you much trouble, but try not to stick two or more units
together to avoid a Sheep Count from the Animist.

battlefield if you let her.

------------------------------B3-14: Making Port-------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
There's a ship coming in to port soon I got a bad feeling about. The chief 
says the paperwork checks out- no problem. 
No problem with the paperwork? That's suspicious right there! You show me a
seaman's shipping writ, and I'll show you a document not fit for feeding to a
goat! 
Someone look into this, would you?
  Anonymous, Graszton Port Authority

|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: Duelhorn (B3-11)

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Harming the Opposite Sex
           - Actions that harm members of the opposite sex are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
I hope you got some Viera or Gria with you (and if you don't, shame on you).
Give the fact we are facing a Ninja, a Summoner, a Trickster and an Arcanist,
you can pretty much deduce it's a 3:1 boy:girl ratio here.

The Ninja has a pair of Ninja Tabi, if you wanna try your luck stealing.
Otherwise, he has Magick Frenzy from the Seer.
The Summoner has Shiva, Kirin and Maduin, along with Magick Counter.
The Trickster has Charisma (Charm) and Traumatize. Borrowing Lancet from the
Dragoon and MP Shield.
The Arcanist has Death, along with Scathe and Gigaflare. That Replenish MP
R-Ability makes things just a bit more fun, of course.

Defeating any unit here will stop the battle in your victory, so focus fire
one down and don't care about the rest.
(Confirmation provided by Steven Ness)
_______________________________________________________________________________
---------------------------B3-16: Knowing the Beast----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Never become attached. The rules on this are clear. But it is not so easy to 
leave one who has fought so long at your side. A dreamhare I had ensorceled 
lamed its leg and lies wounded in the mountains near Camoa. Will you help me
rescue the poor creature?
  Ceva, Beastmaster
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank:  6 | Fee:  200 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Satisfy Petitioner       |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Camoa                |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 830 Gil                    |
|   -                   |   - Danbukwood x4              |
| Talents:              |   - Skull x3                   |
|     Negotiation  4    |   - Clock Gear x5              |
|        Aptitude       |   - Beastmaster Job            |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability  4    | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Guard the bridges for 4 rounds!
Forbidden: Reaction Abilities
           - Reaction abilities are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Your concern here is not to kill, but to put someone in the bridge (or in both,
rather) and not let any enemy through. At all.

Stall them with your own units, blocking the pass and snipe them from afar. 
That meaning, use your mages and archers. Don't think about just killing them
all, since they keep coming anyway. Remember, they can stay on the bridges. 
Even the last tile. Don't let them touch ground on your side.

This will unlock the Beastmaster job for you.

PRECEDING QUEST: Making Port (B3-14)

Objective: Defeat the Night Dancer!
Forbidden: Harming the Weak
           - Actions that harm a lower level unit are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Guess that's how a female Bangaa looks. If they even exist.

The 'Night Dancer' has Traumatize and Charisma only. Well, and Lancet out of
Dragoon.
There's a Templar with Haste, Cura and Dispel, Counter included.
That Gladiator has Fire Soul, along with Lancer and Bangaa Cry.
All three White Monks are the same; Earth Render along with Snigger and
Hypochondria, and they all carry shields instead of weapons; all of
different elements.

There's not much I can say, some are low level (32) while the Night Dancer
goes up to 38. Rely on Reaction Abilities for the lower levels if it 
applies to you. If they are all below you, giggle at the law and break it,
along with them. 

The problem if you are trying to play this the fair way, is the low level
monks can Berserk you.

----------------------------B4-03: I Want to Forget----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
I love someone whom I can never have. The very thought haunts me each moment I 
draw breath. It is more than I can bear. And so I wish to quit this mortal
life. There is a substance known as zombie powder which may grant me the
relief I so desperately seek. Please, find this and bring it to me. End my
suffering.
  Gleifen
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 33 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |

| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |

|        Teamwork  18   | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability  18   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Fulfill the obligations of your quest!
Forbidden: Using >= 20 MP
           - Actions that consume 20 or more MP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
First of, you must head to the Witch of the Fens. You will then open the path
to the next area where you will fight. 

You'll fight Zombies and Dreamhares. Now, regardless of which enemies you
decide to kill, you will succeed on the quest. So take all hares, or all 
zombies.

Question your morals if you feel like it, it doesn't really matter which one
you choose. Remember that if you go the Zombie route, you must either HP 0 
them all down, or banish/etc them.

--------------------------B4-04: The Goug Consortium---------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

  Goug Consortium
  Department of Ranged Mutilation

                       | Rank: 17 | Fee:  300 | Days:    |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Zedlei Forest        |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 2890 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Silver Liquid x2           |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |   - Fusilier Job               |
|        Teamwork  9    | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability  9    | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Targeting All Units
           - Actions targeting all units at once are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Unless you have a Tinker or an Illusionist, this law shouldn't affect you...

What can affect you, however, are the 6 freaking Fusiliers you are fighting.
Including one perched at the top of the world. If you have a Dragoon, bring it.
Offensive magick support? Highly recommended, since those things ignore
height. Also, anything you can do to Blind or somewhat delay their actions 
could be useful. You are gonna have fun with the guy perched at the top, oh
yes you will.

I know my Elementalist with Fire Whip had fun here. You can try splitting up
your group if you want in two parties and go around killing.
The one perched at the top can Blind. One on the left side can Silence.
The left-most guy can Confuse and Charm though.

Have fun with this one. Worth it though, Fusilier is a very nice Moogle
job if you ask me.

PRECEDING QUEST: Strong Lady (A4-06)

Objective: Protect Alys the Ensorceled and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Opportunity Commands
           - Opportunity commands are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Thief with Steal Items/Gil and Leg Shot. Counter + Concentration.
Another Thief with Steal Gil/Loot Lv. 4, borrowing Molotov Cocktail and
Dagger Toss from Juggler.
Alchemist with Flare and Poison.
Viking with Thundara, Pickpocket, and War Cry.
White Mage with Cure, Cura, and Esuna.

Alys, our charge, has Shiva, Kirin and Maduin. She also has Magick Counter
and Blood Price.

Since she starts completely surrounded, we gotta get someone in there fast.
That, or get to disable enemies. She should still be able to hold herself up
perfectly fine for a turn or two, but you shouldn't need THAT much time to
get there.

Once you are there, everything should go a lot easier.

                       | Rank: 25 | Fee:  400 | Days: 20 |

Forbidden: Summoning Scions
           - Summoning scions is forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You will be fighting 3 Parivir, 2 Ninja and an Assassin.
All Parivir have Iai Blow, a hit with a small chance of 1-hit KO. One of them
can Confuse, and the other can Immobilize. Two can counter, the other will
evade all basic attacks and return fire.

For the Ninja, none of them got Dual Wield. One of them has Throw only, the
other has three Veils. The Assassin can Stop, Silence, or Slow.

There's not much to say about this one. Disable if you want to get rid of
some trouble, and take them out one by one as usual. While the Ninja got
Critical: Haste, it should be too much of a problem.

----------------------------B4-10: Aid the Serpent-----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
We've been after a certain mark for some time now, but have been foiled in our
efforts to stop it. Who better to aid us than everyone's favorite swords-for-
hire, Clan <name>?
Come fight with us!
  Clan Serpentina

PRECEDING QUEST: Caravan Cry (B4-06)

Objective: Defeat the mark!
Forbidden: Actions by Viera
           - Viera may only move and perform basic attacks.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Duke Snakeheart, our Arcanist friend equipped with Death, and Scathe/Gigaflare
from the Sage. Also has Replenish MP and Halve MP.

One of the Black Mages has all 3 version of Thunder, while the other has all
the versions of Blizzard. They both have Sticky Fingers and Pierce. They 
absorb the element of their spells too.

The Beastmaster comes equipped with Shadow Shade Tome.
The White Mage has Cura and Esuna, along with Flare from Alchemy. MP Shield 
included.
Finally, the Alchemist has Flare and Poison. Magick Counter and Item Lore.
He can use items, of course.

You are facing a purely magickal team here. They all have high Resistance
(well, except for the Beastmaster), so while you can definitely bring them
down with brute power, most high-attack jobs don't offer great resistances.

If you have units that can deplete MP, they are always useful in making your 
life easier here, since odds are, the MP-depleted unit will try to Attack you,
which you can easily counter for more damage.

Of course, you can ignore all and go straight for Snakeheart. Defeating him
ends the mission. There are a few traps around.

---------------------------B4-12: To Be a Spellblade---------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Magick and steel: two paths seemingly at odds. 
Yet both heed the spellblade and her will. 
One may not undertake the trial to become a spellblade alone, and I would have
you fight at my side.
  Hana, Red Mage
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 28 | Fee:  400 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Escort                   |
|   -                   | Members: 3                     |
|   -                   | Location: Moorabella           |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 8190 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Unpurified Ether x2        |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation  18   |                                |
|        Aptitude       |   - Spellblade Job             |
|        Teamwork  18   | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Protect Hana and defeat 10 foes!
Forbidden: Actions by Gria
           - Gria may only move and perform basic attacks.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
In order to have access to this quest, you must have done at least the
11th story mission, 'Rumors Abound'.
Oh, and you might HATE this quest.

You can only bring 3 units, so make them count. You want someone with either
the Item command or with healing magick available, 'cause this will get ugly.

You'll be facing several Viera, of all kinds of jobs. To kick it off, there's
a Green Mage with Silence. An Assassin with the Slow and Silence skills, and a
Fencer with Swarmstrike and Featherblow. Aside from these, I've seen White
Mages, Archers, Snipers, Red Mages... In the end, once you fell 9 of them, 
a Spellblade appears. She has Poison, Confusion and Sleep Blades. Along with
Blood Price. Shouldn't be too hard compared to the rest unless you are pretty
much dying already.

None of 'em have secondary, reaction or passive skills though. Hana, the Red
Mage on your side has Doublecast along with Cure and the basic elemental 
spells, but don't count of her for healing. You still want someone on your side
who can help.

Otherwise, I suggest one good damage dealer and someone who can disable enemies
in one way or another (A Juggler is always fun, or an Elementalist, or what
have you). 

If you brought in people for disabling, you can use whatever clan privilege
you wish. Otherwise, I can recommend Speed Up or Power Up if you have good
ranks. In the end, the Spellblade job should be yours.

PRECEDING QUEST: Aid the Serpent (B4-10)

Objective: Protect Duke Snakeheart and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Non-elemental Effects
           - Use of non-elemental weapons and abilities is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
A Master Monk with Withering Strike and borrowing Body Slam plus Greased
Lightning. Has Counter and is using Doublehand.
Maquis, the Ninja, has Earth Veil, Oblivion, and Magick Frenzy. Blink Counter,
and Shieldbearer.
The Night Dancer has Charisma and Traumatize, plus Lancet. With MP Shield, and
Ribbon-bearer.
That Seer over yonder has Esuna, Sandstorm and Expose Weakness. Plus Archer's
Bane, and Immunity.

Of course, Duke Snakeheart is on our side. Armed with zero Arcane Magick 
(what?) and Scathe plus Gigaflare from the Sage. Replenish MP and Halve MP.

The law is a bit tedious if you plan on following it, either equip elemental
weapons, or stick to using skills only with elemental effects. The good part
is, that killing only one will get you through.

PRECEDING QUEST: A Chill in the Night (B4-11)

Objective: Protect Ghi and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Targeting an Area
           - Actions targeting two or more tiles are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The so-called Swordking comes equipped with Thundara, Tsunami and Pickpocket.
He also has a Mirror Mail on, so no magick.
Alura, the Defender, only has Defend and Bulwark. 
Elura, the Templar, knows Silence and Haste.

Our guard, Ghi, starts Disabled and Immobilized. He knows Shimmering, Skyfury,
and Hoarfrost Blade. It's up to you if you want to heal him quickly, or use
the time he can't act to surround him with your units if you need.

The three enemies shouldn't give you too much trouble considering it's 6vs3...
Or well, it's a 7 vs 3 since Ghi recovers on his first action.

------------------------------B4-16: Lord Grayrl!------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
You be wanting that rare gem of gems, the noble pearl, eh? Perhaps I might part
with it for 10 million...no, 30 million-wait, 50 million! A good price, no?
Bwa ha ha!

Come to the wharf in Graszton. Bring yer money and nothing else...'cept maybe
a few wheelbarrows to carry it in! Bwah ha!
  Lord Grayrl, Gentleman Trader
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 28 | Fee:  400 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Defeat Mark              |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Graszton             |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 5110 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Wyvern Wing x2             |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |   - Viking Job                 |
|        Teamwork  22   | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability  22   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat Lord Grayrl!
Forbidden: Not Moving 3 Tiles
           - Each unit must move exactly 3 tiles before ending its turn.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Between the two Vikings at the top, they have Thunder and Pickpocket or
Thundara. Plus Counter or Return Fire.
The Juggler has Weapon Toss, Ring Toss (Stop), Molotov Cocktail (Berserk), and
Dagger Toss (Disable). Plus Critical: Quicken.
The Thief has Steal Items/Gil, and Cuisine from Animist.
Grayrl himself has Thundaga, Strong-arm, and Pillage. 

You start dead in the center. Get someone to disable the Juggler quickly, send
someone to deal with the Vikings if you want. I suggest you disable or take
down the Thief quickly, since Cuisine is broken. Then finally, go for
Lord Grayrl. If you can equip Thunder-absorb/resisting armor, better.

The Viking Job should be yours in the end.

PRECEDING QUEST: Caravan Cry II (B4-14)

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Actions by Bangaa
           - Bangaa may only move and perform basic attacks.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
I hope you don't have many Bangaa. Also, I hate this map.
Alys the Ensorceled will be assisting us on this map; she can call forth Shiva,
Kirin and Maduin. Has Magick Counter and Blood Price.

On Khamja's side, there's a Soldier with 3 Rends, Counter, and Attack Up.
Master Monk with Dark Fist, Lifebane, and Cross-counter. He can Blink Counter
and has Defense Up.
The Black Mage? All -ra and Thundaga, Magick Counter and of course, Magick Up.
Tinker with Green Gear (Poison), Silver Disc (Blind), and Gold Moogletron
(Barrier). Cross your fingers with this guy. He has Critical: Quicken to boot.
The White Mage has Cura, Curaga, and and unlearnt Refresh, plus Archer's Bane.

If you have a Fusilier, it could be handy here if you put him at the top 
starting point. Otherwise, get some high MOV people and rush that Tinker. Lest
he gets lucky and your whole team suddenly is Blinded. From there on, it's
pretty much your choice. Though units with good JMP could be useful too.

PRECEDING QUEST: Show of Strength (B4-15)

Objective: Protect Frimelda and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Ranged Weapons
           - Attacks with bows, greatbows, guns, hand-cannonds, and cards are
             forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You are fighting with Luso, Cid and Adelle. No questions asked. 
Frimelda is on your side, she has no abilities, and remember she counts as
undead, so no healing spells. Though I doubt you'll need them.

Luc Sardarc is a Paladin with Saint Cross, Holy Blade, and War Cry. He has
Blink Counter and Destroyer.

He isn't so fast; or rather, this fight in itself is not so impressive, you
shouldn't have any trouble at all.

You'll get The Fallen Angel sword, and the High Seraph's Plume, Ultima's
summoning item.

-------------------------B5-04: Geomancer's Way - Snow-------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Geomancers bend nature to their will.
Our society has established a series of trials for prospective geomancers to
determine whether they are worthy to share in our knowledge.

The Trial of Rivening Snow is the third trial.
You have traveled far, but your journey is not yet complete. Come when you are
prepared.
  Naturalist Society

PRECEDING QUEST: Geomancer's Way - Rain (C5-01)

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Using >= 20 MP
           - Actions that consume 20 or more MP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
That's an easy law to abide to.

All 3 Geomancers have Shining Flare and Nature's Embrace. One has Life's
Embrace (Immobilize) and Earth's Embrace (Disable). The other two have
Artifice's Embrace (Slow) and Avalanche (Sleep).

The Zahak has Fireball and Ice Orb, plus Expose Weakness and Mighty Guard 
(double Blue Mage hint).
The Ice Drake, being what it is, only has Ice Breath and Sleet.

You start between all 3 Geomancers, and near the Drake. Pick some long
distance units, specially if they can debuff, and get to work on this one. 
You shouldn't have much trouble. Just remember that the Geomancers are your
priority since they can debuff a lot.
_______________________________________________________________________________
---------------------------B5-05: Wanted: Assistant----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
I need an assistant to assist me in the laboratory with my research, kupo. But
not the dangerous kind of assisting with explosions and fires, kupo. Oh no. 
I'll only need you for seven days, and you'll only be required to work a mere
eight hours each day. I'll provide your lunch and afternoon tea, as well as 
colognes and perfumes to help mask the smell, kupo!
  Malbolabs Alchemical Research
