-------------------------B5-08: Geomancer's Way - Mist-------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Geomancers bend nature to their will. 
Our society has established a series of trials for prospective geomancers to
determine whether they are worthy to share in our knowledge.

The Trial of Seething Mist is the fourth and final trial. Pass, and our wisdom
will be yours.
  Naturalist Society
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 38 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: The Aldanna Range    |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 4850 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Mahbeny x2                 |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |   - Geomancer Job              |
|        Teamwork  33   | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability  33   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUEST: Geomancer's Way - Snow (B5-04)

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: HP <= 100
           - Having less than 100 HP is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Well, that's a first.

They all have Shining Flare and Nature's Embrace. The one furthest away has
Mist Storm; which besides doing damage, also depletes MP.
The one in the middle has Artifice's Embrace (Slow).
The closest one has Life's and Earth's Embraces (Immobilize/Disable).

You are also fighting a Toughskin (turtle) with Munch and Resonate. 
And a Lamashtu (Lamia) with Poisonous Frog, Hand Slap, and Twister.

The Geomancer further away has Halve MP. The rest have assorted skills 
including Magick Up and other. Both Lamia and Turtle can counter.

A Regen-type Clan Privilege is good for keeping up the law, but then again,
what would be the point... Bring a healer and be careful (especially with the
Lamashtu's Twister) or just rush through not caring. You've won 3 fights of
this kind already, you shouldn't have much trouble here. 

The Geomancer will be yours in the end.
_______________________________________________________________________________
-----------------------C1-01: It's a Secret to Everybody-----------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
My pet shelling, Titoise, has run away, kupo, and I need someoen to find him 
for me! You see, I've been keeping him in secret, kupo. If Mr. Grann finds out,
this little moogle is going to be in big trouble. Kupo!

If that wasn't bad enough, Mr. Grann's daughter, Fes, can't stand shellings.
So I'm sure to catch a pom-pom lashing from both of them, kupo!
  Keeper Pirilo, Grann's Menagerie

PRECEDING QUEST: Flown the Coop (B1-08)

Objective: Weaken Titoise and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Ice
           - Weapons and abilities that use ice are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
There's a Red and White Chocobo here, in case you have a Chocobo Knight.
They come with the usual, Choco Meteor/Break, and Cure/Recharge, respectively.

Out of the three Great Tortoises, those that are not your marks have Limit
Glove (A Blue Mage magick), Headbutt and Sonic Spin. They both have Critical:
Quicken and Defense Up.

Titoise has Headbutt, Sonic Spin, and Munch. Along with Counter and Immunity.

Remember you MUST NOT kill Titoise. Leave that one for last, kill everything
else first, then start bringing it down.

Helda's one of Mrs. Zamaree's favorites, and you can be sure if she finds her

PRECEDING QUEST: It's a Secret to Everybody (C1-01)

Objective: Weaken Helda and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Bludgeoning Weapons
           - Attacks with rods, staves, poles, knuckles, hammers, and maces
             are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
All three Worgen at the front have Fangs, Chilling Blow (Immobilize), and
Fiery Blow (Silence). They have Counter and Reveal on as well.
Both Hellhounds have Screech (Confuse) and Fangs. They also pack Blink
Counter and Reveal.

Our mark, Helda, has Screech (Confuse), Fangs, and Shadowy Blow (Blind). She
has Counter and Pugilist. 

If you have a Hunter, this is a good place for one. The Hellhounds are weak
to Holy, and all of them are weak to Water. Illusionists could also be 
useful for that. Otherwise, avoid earth-based attacks as usual. There's no 
real challenge here.

PRECEDING QUEST: Foodstuffs: Appearance (B1-12) 

Objective: Procure the requested ingredients!
Forbidden: Copycat
           - Using the same action as the preceding unit is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Floating Eyes. Not Ahrimans. Kill 4 Floating Eyes and however many Ahrimans
you need to open up space for more eyes.

Among the Floating Eyes, you should know their skills already, such as
Supersonic Wave and Gnaw.
On the Ahriman department, you can get Glare, Demonic Gaze and Bewitching
Glaze. But no Roulette, thankfully.

Get to it. I can't say much else here.

PRECEDING QUEST: Showdown (B1-16)

Objective: Speed Battle vs. House Bowen
Forbidden: Targeting Self
           - Actions targeting the user are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Remember this is a speed battle. The finishing blow is what matters. You can
even be so cheap as to heal the enemy if House Bowen is going next so they
don't get the killing blow.

The Rafflesia has a lot of HP. Well... Above 1000 at least. The problem with
this thing is that they can do a lot of crap to units directly surroudning
it. That is to say, let's say all 8 units in play are around it. The plant
can put you all to sleep. Or Poison you. Or Confuse... Or Blind... Or Slow...

It's not a fun thing, I'll tell you. So it's up to you if you want to bring
more ranged support and let House Bowen bite it so can deal with the plant. 
And since you can't target yourself, healing any debuffs you get might get
tricky. 

The plant is weak to fire, by the way. And I'll tell you, my Dragoon was
awesome in here. XD

PRECEDING QUEST: Seeding the Harvest (C1-13)

Objective: Gather as much fruit as you can in 4 rounds!
Forbidden: Harming the Weak
           - Actions that harm a lower level unit are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Bring your fastest and best runners to this one. Enemies should be around your
level, so even if you feel the need to fight, you can.

There are two Baknamy, a Werewolf, an Ahriman and of course, a Floating Eye.
Or at least, those were the starting enemies for me, as more appear and of
different breeds. I saw Lamia in there too!
You should know how they all work by now; though remember battle isn't the
focus of this mission.

To gather fruit, just stand next to a tree and use the special command.
Yes, it's possible to gather them all without Speed Up or Haste. It's just
a royal pain in the ass sometimes. (Read: Enemies might box you in; Disable is
your friend here)
Unfortunately, you still gotta wait 4 rounds.

¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

If anyone happen on any they'd be willing to part with, they can find us cmaped
along the highroad in the Bisga Greenlands for a time.
  Mocedad, Bangaa Brotherhood

PRECEDING QUEST: A Fatal Mistake (B2-04)

Objective: Uphold the law and destroy the traps!
Forbidden: Attack
           - Attacking is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yes, that's a Tonberry King right there.
Remember that you can still use skills, just not attack. 

You should be well used to fighting Baknamies by now, and the Tonberry King
should be easy to eliminate, since you can just lure him around while your
other fighters take him out.

Remember the purple traps are charm. So I highly suggest you keep someone
on the ready to attack in one way or another (But NOT with the attack command)
the charmed unit or you might fail the quest by accident.

That's all I can say. Oh, right, the King has an Orb of Minwu. 
I hate this quest. Failed by accident 2 times while clearing the traps after
killing everything because I forgot the Attack law... *Sigh*

           - Actions that restore HP are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yes, you got the law right. No healing. So you better focus fire those enemies
down QUICK before they can start hurting you. 
The Drakes only have the Ice Breath, a T-shapped skill. Flans got Acid.
The Wendigo might be the problem, in terms of raw strength at least. They
can deal damage and delay your turn, plus they got good HP and decent 
defense.

As luck might have it, the flans and drakes absorb Ice, and guess what they
all can use? =D

Remember the objective is to gather all the potions (the sparkly things on
the ground), so it's up to you if you want to fight or not.

PRECEDING QUEST: Komodo Search (C2-02)

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Targeting All Units
           - Actions targeting all units at once are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
There's a Black Mage with Blizzard/ra. A Geomancer who can use Avalanche, 
which might Sleep you. There's a Time Mage with Halve MP, along with Slow
and Stop. Surprisingly, he also knows something of a Scholar, being able to
deal Ice damage to EVERYONE. And of course, there's an Illusionist with
Freezeblink. Never you mind the Archer with Cupid or Blackout.

The Scholar might pummel everyone with his ice skill; and every 3 turns, that
Illusionist is going to shove a Freezeblink on you as well. So I hope you
got some good healing; or some ice-absorb/immune gear. What I'm getting at
here is, KILL THOSE TWO QUICKLY. 

The Komodo guy can't die.

                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 16 | Fee:  300 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Escort                   |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: The Bisga Greenlands |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   - Defender          |   - 2200 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Zodiac Ore x5              |
| Talents:              |   - Cruzle Brass x3            |
|     Negotiation  7    |   - Rabbit Tail x1             |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  7    | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Protect the Komodo Trader and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Targeting an Area
           - Actions targeting two or more tiles are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You are going to hate this guy, trust me. He has God knows how many missions
involving him and keeping his ass in check. And the plot in his quests are
generally... stupid.

Btw, don't bring magick users unless you wanna break the law. But be sure to
have someone with the Item command ready to keep that guy alive. That, or try
to make a barrier between you and the enemies so he nor them can't get through.

That is, for the guys down with you. You gotta deal with a Gunner and an
Archer up top, which are the ones who will give you trouble unless you can
lock them down (An Archer with Blackout can be useful). The problem with them,
is that if you try to approach from the right side, they go to the left and 
climb down. So you gotta pincer them in one fell swoop.

To make things worse, there are traps around. If you got Libra by having a
FFTA on your GBA slot when starting the game, you could use that to lure the
enemies into them. Taking down half their HP doing nothing is quite nice.

Oh, one more thing. Be careful if the guy starts heading into that small place
at the south, south-west corner of the map. Enemies can trap him there and 
quickly do him in, considering all he does is run away.

malboro- a vastly inferior specimen. We must act quickly if we are to protect
the reputation of our house. Look into the matter with all haste.
  Galmia Pepe, Office of Quality Assurance

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Let's see... The law shouldn't be too hard.

There's a Ninja with Throw and Fire Veil... Oh, and Magick Up. The horror...
The Hume Thief only has Steal Limelight, and Critical: Haste.
The Moogle Thief has Steal Gil and Lv. 3 Loot. 
The White Monk can actually fight. Air Render, Aurablast, Counter, etc.
A Fighter with Rush and Wild Swing. Oh, and Archer's Bane.
Last but not least (or maybe least indeed), a Viking with Thundara and
Pickpocket.

Overall, not too bad. They don't have too much in the damage-dealing 
department, so use whatever you wish. They won't hold it against you, really.

This is not fun, if you ask me. They still fall like everything else, but
dear God, 6 of those things...
Bring good damage dealers, especially if they can fight at range. Or Sages
(or the like) with Aero magick. 

Careful, I broke the law by having them Charm my Sage into Blinding one of
my own. Yes, it's retarded. And the Charm breath apparently has a 100% 
chance to hit.

to you.

¯¯¯¯¯
You might want someone who can heal. Might.

Summon Pack and Fangs. Each one has a different elemental wolf skill.

Beastmaster to win this one. No need to kill the Hellhounds if you don't want.

Objective: Clean the airship withing 4 rounds!  *
Forbidden: Fire
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire are forbidden.

* Due to unknown factors to me, it seems the number of rounds you have might
change. It might be affected by the amount of days you have left to clear
the quest, but that's me guessing.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This is a bothersome quest. Not only are some of the cleaning spots on the
other freaking side of the airship, you have enemies messing up with you.
All of these Flans know Blizzard and Blizzara. So you know that unless you
really kill them quickly, they are going to heal up right away. 

That is, assuming you wanna fight since that will push back your turns
even more, not quite helping with the time limit; and since you can't use
Fire without breaking the law...

Get your two fastest units to the other side, while the rest focus on the 
nearby spots. If you have someone with Silence or similar debuffs, try to
stick it on the Flans.

Haste also works for you. And you might want to consider a Speed Up Clan
Privilege; though you shouldn't need it.

Forbidden: Ranged Weapons
           - Attacks with bows, greatbows, guns, hand-cannons, and cards are
             forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Three Chocobos; a Yellow, Green and a Brown. If you have a Chocobo Knight, this
is a good chance to get one. The Yellow one has Choco Beak, the Brown has Cure,
Beak and Guard, along with Spellbound. The Green one has Cure, Esuna and Beak,
plus Counter and Death Strike.

Remember all Chocobos are weak to Lightning, Water, and Holy.
The Sprite has White Wind, along with Quicken and Extend. Now, Extend might
be bothersome combined with Choco Guard.

The Great Tortoises have Sonic Spin, a Roundhouse move, and Headbutt, which
might Immobilize you.

Strike them as they come, and search after the fight if you wish. In my case,
the ring was found in the middle of the right side, near where the Brown
Chocobo was.

PRECEDING QUEST: Monster Poaching (C3-05)

Objective: Go to Calea!
Forbidden: Harming the Weak
           - Actions that harm a lower level unit are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Enemies go from 39 to 41, so odds are, you might be over it.
Two of the Floating Eyes have Supersonic Wave, Gnaw, Counter and Concentration.
The other two have Supersonic Wave, Death Dive, Gnaw, Critical: Quicken, and
Reveal.

Your objective here is getting to Calea. No need to kill the enemies, so a
unit with Faerie Shoes could help to go through them, or counter your way
through. You have to end your turn by Calea.

Cid and Luso are forced units in this round. Since this is a multi-staged
fight, pick your units wisely.

-------------

Objective: Defeat all foes and keep them away from Calea!
Forbidden: Copycat
           - Using the same action as the preceding unit is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Assassin: Shadowbind, Last Breath, Aphonia; Return Fire and Safeguard.
Master Monk: Pummel, Withering Strike, Lifebane, Cross-counter; Critical:
Quicken, and Reveal.
Parivir: Wind Slash, Iai Blow, Shimmering Blade; Strike Back, and Death Strike.
Seer: Cura, Esuna; Star Cross, Wild Tornado; Replenish MP, Halve MP.
Arcanist: Gravity, Lv. 3 Dark, Lv. 5 Haste, Lv. ? Shadowflare; Absorb MP, and
Resistance Up.
Lanista: Sword of Darkness, Sword of Light, Haunting Vision; Blink Counter and
Avoid Traps.

Cluster by the rock Luso mentions, position your melee units in front, and if
you have excess, go for the Seer. Otherwise, keep a line formed with all of
your ranged damage dealers behind. You shouldn't have much trouble.

           - Reaction abilities are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
They are all Green Mages. Though only one of them has Blind or Sleep, the rest
can only do physical attacks. 

If you do this when first available, you should have 6 units. So deploy them
all. This fight shouldn't challenge you at all, so I won't even bother.
They will probably even pile up for the perfect magick attack. 

There are 2 chests you can get here. Just make a unit stand by the chest/pot,
and select the special command. 

Completing this quest will unlock the Green Mage job for you, by the way.

Fun fact. Midori is Japanese for green.

¯¯¯¯¯
You saw the law. If you bring any Moogles, they can only do a standard
attack. No items, no nothing. 

On this fight, you are competing for the finishing blow to that Malboro
King. House Bowen can do everything else, you just need the finishing blow.
This King is immune to a lot of things because of its Orb of Minwu. 

If you haven't, they got your basic Malboro array of skills. Putrid Breath
for several statuses, among other things. 

Update: If Veis deals the finishing blow to the mark, you still get a
success (Thanks to Peri).

           - Actions that restore MP are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You are fighting 3 Fighters; one of them has Air Render, and the other has
the Bonecrusher R-Ability, which will counter melee hits for higher damage
(no exceptions), so try to snipe him out with magicks or what else.

Aside from them, you have a Thief, a Warrior and a Soldier, who as usual, will
try to Rend your stats down.

There's really no good way to get this done. Beat up on them one by one, as
usual and as they come for you. 'cause I can't really say much more here. 
Ok, maybe I can. There are traps.

The Fighter job should be yours.

---------------------------C4-05: Popocho's Chocobos---------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Kupo! My precious chocobos have all run off!
Every last one of them! It looks like they've headed for the mountains in the
Aldanna Range. I need someone to find them before the monsters do, kupo!
  Popocho, Chocobo Knight
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 27 | Fee:  400 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Escort                   |
|   -                   | Members: 4 x3                  |
|   -                   | Location: The Aldanna Range x3 |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 4360 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Blood-darkened Bone x4     |
| Talents:              |   - Adamantite x3              |
|     Negotiation  13   |                                |
|        Aptitude       |   - Chocobo Knight Job         |
|        Teamwork  13   | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Protect the chocobo and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: >50 Damage
           - Dealing >50 damage is forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Oh God, I still remember this quest from back in the Japanese version.
I hated it for whatever reason. Maybe the suicidal Chocobos had something
to do with it... 
Anyway, it's a 3-staged battle with no rewards in-between.

For round one... have fun with that law. I personally don't care about 
upholding the law in this battle. You gotta move fast, and I mean FAST.
Otherwise that stupid Chocobo is gonna commit seppuku (assisted suicide); 
unless on the odd chance you get a smart Chocobo. 

If you have someone that knows Cure or Cura and has high speed or MOV, by
all means, bring that person. Hasten it or whatever, but get it on the line
of fire quickly to heal the Chocobo. Odds are, it might almost be dead by
the time you get there.

Don't bother fighting just yet. Disable enemies by any means (Disable as the
word, not the debuff necessarily) and move to protect that Chocobo. Then feel
free to deal with everything else. As usual, a critical hit made me break the
law. XD 

-------------

Objective: Protect the chocobos and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Actions by Moogles
           - Moogles may only move and perform basic attacks.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Round 2, go. Don't bring Moogles, kick up Speed Up and you should be fine.
Even though you gotta protect 2 Chocobos this time.

You might have to split your party in 2. Each Chocobo is being attacked by a
Blue Flan and a Wendigo. You know how they work by now probably. Flans heal
themselves, Wendigo Focus then smash. 

Divide and conquer. 

-------------

Objective: Protect Popocho and his chocobos!
Forbidden: Actions by Humes
           - Humes may only move and perform basic attacks.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This is the last round, so use whatever Privilege you wish.

You are fighting a Ghost here. Since you can't use Burial without breaking the
law, go for Phoenix Down on the tombstone or Exorcise form a White Monk.

The Drakes are the same; they got their breath weapon and both have Sleet.
A Doom+Sleep skill, along with Counter.

The Zahak however, has Mighty Guard (raises DEF and RES) and Expose Weakness
(opposite to Mighty Guard). Besides Ice Breath, it has the Ice Orb too. So
you might expect some healing on their side if you let them.

Deal with this fight as you see fit. I'm not a big fan of the Chocobo Knight,
but it's needed for some quests. Enjoy it.
_______________________________________________________________________________

I can scarcely focus, never mind study, and so I find myself turning to 
frivolous fictions.

Predictably, my schoolwork has begun to suffer and my very future is now in 
peril. Please find the cause of this nuisance and put a stop to it!

waste a turn to load their cannons.

the others can Protect/Regen using Protect Cannon.

Feel free to spread out and take them out. Might as well thin their numbers
before more come. Or get two healers, stick in the center and yourself back
to back. 

Yes, they seriously come from the well. No, I don't know what's up with that.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Retarded law time! That's right, either you Ribbon/Orb of Minwu/Whatever
yourself up, or this law will be broken in no time flat.

(Berserk), Hypochondria (Poison), and Shadow of Doubt (Immobilize).
The big guy, the one by the rigth who also has Rend Speed, instead knows
Charisma (Charm) and Traumatize.

There's no real way to do this one, since you are facing debuffs everywhere.
As I said, either use items to immunize yourself, or have someone with Astra.
Unless you have Debuff Resistance. 
Though remember, they are Tricksters. They move fast.

Beating these guys nets you the Trickster job.

PRECEDING QUEST: Instrument of Inspiration (C4-07)

Objective: Defeat all foes before they enter the water!
Forbidden: Back Attack
           - Attacking a unit from behind is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Well, this is an odd one.
See that pool by where you start? If they go in there, it's over. Not a fun
mission.

You might know the Prima Donna gang.
Aluette, the Fencer has Nighthawk and Earth Heal from the Elementalist.
Valentyne the Spellblade has Poison/Stun Blades, and borrows Death Sickle from
the Sniper; she can Counter too.
Lili the Hunter only knows Advice. Her real strenght lies in her Geomancer
ways.
And Mayhew of course. Besides Soul Crush and Cyclone, she has Shield Bash 
along with Reflex. 
I won't bother with the Devotees... I just won't.

Try to barricado the way into the Water. The two Gria are your problem, since
they can fly over you. Also, Immobilize or other debuffs (even Disable) work
to your advantage. Heck, even sleep if you want. Do note Mayhew and Lili both
have Cachushas on though.

BANBANGA!

Err... Sorry, got caught up. 
Two of the Master Monks can use Air Render from the regular White Monk. The

more the lower his HP is.

fit, or Disable/Rend their power if you can, since they can really hurt.

           - Attacks with bows, greatbows, guns, hand-cannons, and cards are

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yeah... The White Mage kitty hood should stay on females...
I digress. It's 6 against 3, but some very far away three.

Thw Zaghnal comes packed with the usual Roar, Slug, and Assault. 
The Wolf has Summon Pack and Fangs.
And our Wendigo friend comes with Bone Shatter, Brute Strength, and 
Helmsplitter which, like its namesake, might destroy your helm.

Our Seer friend has Magick Frenzy, Cura, and Thundara. And Ribbon-wearer...
Christ.

Lay down your units, start he fight and... Oh dang, more enemies. Add a second
wolf and a Wendigo to the mix. Better get there quickly. The odds just went 
to 6 against 5.
The Seer might be smart and barricade himself against the treasure, leaving 
only one way open to attack him. 

Enjoy your new job.
_______________________________________________________________________________

PRECEDING QUEST: Geomancer's Way - Sun (C5-02)

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Swimming
           - Entering a water tile is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Three Geomancers again. They all have Shining Flare and Nature's Embrace 
(Immobilize). One has Artifice's Embrace (Slow), another one has Venom
Squall (Poison) and the third one has Earth's Embrace (Disable).

The Nagaraja has Ice Orb/Breath, along with Expose Weakness, Mighty Guard 
(Blue Magick) and Sleet.
The Wendigo has Bone Shatter, Brute Strength, and Sunder Earth. Plus
Bonecrusher.

The law is only broken if you end your turn in a water tile. You can go through
them as much as you want.

Try to get the geomancers disabled or something if you can. A Dragoon is always
useful to get the Nagaraja, but you shouldn't really need one that badly.
_______________________________________________________________________________
-------------------------C5-02: Geomancer's Way - Sun--------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Geomancers bend nature to their will.

determine whether they are worthy to share in our knowledge.

tested, come.
  Naturalist Society

           - Reaction abilities are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
There are 3 Geomancers, all have Shining Flare and Nature's Embrace. Two also
have Artifice's Embrace (Slow) while the third one has Life's Embrace 
(Disable) and Earth's Embrace (Immobilize). 

There's a Hellhound with Screech, Fangs, and Shadowy Blow.
And a Nidhogg with the Fire and Lightning skills, including Dragon Force.
Both beasts can Counter, but the Nidhogg has the Blink version.

If you really want to obey the law, go ahead and take off your R-Abilities.
If you have a Fusilier, you can try silencing the enemy from afar, or what
have you. The Geomancer with the Life and Earth Embraces is probably your
biggest threat here, and also the furthest away at the northwest. 

Deal with them as you wish.

-------------------------------C5-03: Starstruck-------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
We've been so busy with our singing we've entirely neglected our stage combat. 
Sparring is well and good, but our adoring public wants authenticity-only a
proper melee with beasts and blood will do! I'm sure we can handle ourselves,
but we are out of practice, so a bodyguard would be welcome. Will no one come
to our aid?
  Prima Donna

PRECEDING QUEST: The Storage Shed (C4-15)

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Restoring MP
           - Actions that restore MP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Let's see what we got here...
Deathscythe with Doom, Shackle, plus Darkga. MP Shield and Bloodprice.
Nidhogg with Fire/Ice Breaths, and Expose Weakness. Absorb Damage and Pugilist.
Great Malboro with almost everything. Blink Counter and Immunity.
Lamia with Night, Hand Slap, and Twister. *Groan*
Wendigo with Quake, Bone Shatter, and Sunder Earth. Also packs Bonecrusher.

Of course, the ladies from Prima Donna will be tagging along with you. You
should be well acquainted with them all already.

There's no protect clause, so you can let them do the rushing and then just
back them up, or mop the floor quickly. Just remember you need to do something
with the tomb of the Deathscythe, or killing it last.

Otherwise, lay it to them. Wind will take care of the Malboro and the Wendigo,
but the Lamia will shrug it off. Holy can bring the Scythe and the Nidhogg 
down. Stay away from Thunder though, the Nidhogg and Lamia will just shrug it
off otherwise.

                       | Rank: 44 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |

| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability  44   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Speed Battle vs. Tigrina 
Forbidden: Targeting Distant Units
           - Actions targeting units two or more tiles away are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You'll be fighting the Flowsand Lord once again, with his huge amount of HP 
as well. And unless you plan on breaking the law from the get go, be sure to
get either melee units, or you are going to have to move in your mages into
melee range on this one.

Tigrina is a Ravager (as expected) with Overpower, Tenacity, Sneak Attack,
and Blast Wave. She has good Attack and Defense, and can Strike Back.
Remember she's on your side, technically.

The Antlions have the usual, Sandstorm, Suffocate, and Subsidence.
The Flowsand Lord, if you remember, has the Draw In, which probably won't 
matter if you are going for the law. The Stun Crush is a melee-ranged attack
hitting both side entirely (hint hint, massive counter time) and the oddly
placed if you ask me Gravity Flux, which is mostly a long distance attack.
He comes, like all big marks, with Absorb Damage.
Remember Draw In is basically an absorb.

You know the deal with Speed Battles. Either beat the crap out of that thing,
heal it up if she's next and your blows will only leave it at death's door and
stuff. 

I guess I should mention it... He has a LOT of HP. Maybe around 1500~1700.
Enjoy your Ravager job.

¯¯¯¯¯
YES! I love this quest just for the fact that you get to smash the Bonga
Bugle Head Editor, right there.

Besides those two, an Assassin, Parivir and Ninja will join the fray.
They are fairly fast, but that's about it.
The Ninja has Throw and Daal Wield. Oddly enough, he has Prominence and

The Parivir has Shimmering, Skyfury and Hoarfrost Blades. He's wearing a 
Genji Armor set and comes with Strike Back, but isn't that strong, for being
a Parivir at least.

I seriously won't bother with those two... 
You shouldn't have much trouble in this fight, truthfully. Just be sure to 
really lay a heavy hit on the editor as payback. 

Enjoy the Lanista job in the end.

The three Bombs are the same, basically. Flame Attack and Self-Destruct,
including Fira. Two have Pierce, the other has Critical: Quicken.

really want to obey the law, just rush to the pieces and live out of Reaction
Abilities.

------------------------D1-05: The Bangaa of the Rupies------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

Those cowards in the Nu Mou Nobles have had it coming, and with a little extra

  Mocedad, Bangaa Brotherhood

                       | Rank: 50 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |

|    Adaptability  20   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
PRECEDING QUESTS: The Nu Mou Nobles (C1-12)
                  The Nu Mou of the Rupies (D1-10)

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Fire
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This is basically the reverse of the 'Nu Mou of the Rupies' quest.

You are dealing with a Time Mage who knows Slow, Reflect and Stop, and has
Spellbound.
A Black Mage with all -ra and Thunaga, with Geomancy.
A White Mage with Cura and Esuna, only.
The Illusionist has Prominence, Tempest, Freezeblink and Stardust.
And Madreth, the Arcanist, has Drain, Syphon, and Graviga. Plus Pierce.

On your side, there's Mocedad, the Cannoneer with Foresight, Mortar, and
Target.
A Gladiator with all 3 elemental skills.
And a Bishop with Barrier, Water, and Aero, only.

Bring whatever you prefer, but physical fast units would be preferred, since 
the Nu Mou have high Resistance usually. On importante, the Time Mage can
debuff you with not so good things. The Black Mage can hit you hard if you are
not prepared, and the Illusionist can damage everyone if he gets the time.

Being the Nu Mou they are, they are a bit slow too.

Try to Silence or Disable or what have you and go at them. It's not that 
difficult of a fight.

 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯ 
Objective: Defeat 10 foes and keep them from the tree!
Forbidden: Restoring MP
           - Actions that restore MP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
When they say 'keep away from the tree' it means just don't let them stand
next to it. If you want, you can just stack to ranged units to the sides of 
the tree and bulwark yourself by it, let them come to their own death.

This fight might take a while, since you gotta kill 10 enemies. Fortunately,
they won't come all at you at the same time. I mean, they do keep coming, but
the fight only starts with 4 of 'em.

Mindful that they can lower your DEF and RES. Can SLOW you, or lower your 
SPEED with all their skills. Well, the Antlions. 
The Pit Beasts (they show up eventually) can Blind people in an area or
delay their turn.

frontlines with some melee-offensive units.

Then it's your standard battle.

PRECEDING QUEST: Chita on Weapons-Adepts (C3-12)

Objective: Speed Battle vs. Chita's Weaponers
Forbidden: Piercing Weapons
           - Attacks with rapiers and spears are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Your mark today is a Reaver. Between his skills, he can destroy your shield
and armor, as well as doing holy damage. He Evades all sorts of Magicks and
is Impervious.

You should already be acquainted with Chita and his weaponers. Remember this is
a speed battle though. Since you can't do squat with magick, bring either
ranged units (a Hunter could do nice with Sidewinder) or what have you.
He, like the Werewolf brethren, is weak to Earth.

Surprisingly, Chita has Faerie Shoes. You will also get a Materia Blade for
doing this one.

ZEDLEI FOREST BATTLE

Objective: Defeat all foes except the blue antlion!
Forbidden: Ice
           - Weapons and abilities that use ice are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The Tortoise, besides doing a complete paradox by having Critical: Quicken
(A quick turtle? Hah.... No, I don't wanna hear about Aesop's fables), can
Immobilize you.

Both red Antlions have Bile (Lowers DEF/RES), Mucus (Slow) and Saliva (Delay).
Both also have Unscarred. 

The Chocobo has Beak and Cure, along with Counter.

It shouldn't be a difficult fight. Pack wind stuff you have it, and lay it on
them.

-------------

BISGA GREENLANDS BATTLE

Objective: Defeat all foes except the green antlion!
Forbidden: Fire
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The red antlions are all surrounding the green one.
They have the standard repertoire of skills. Lower DEF/RES, Slow, the Delay
one, what have you. Most of them have Attack Up, two got Counter, one
has Blur and the other Archer's Bane.

If you have wind-related skills or gear, bring it. There are traps here.

-------------

MOORABELLA BATTLE

Objective: Defeat all foes except the white antlion!
Forbidden: Lightning
           - Weapons and abilities that use lightning are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Two Werewolves, two Lamia and a Sprite.

You should know them by now. Werewolves will Roar you out of everything as
usual. One of the Lamia can use Night and Twister. The other has the Poison +
Frog combo along with the delay hit.
The Sprite only has Meteorite, along with Reflect.

Simple fight. Toss a heal to the Antlion if he gets caught in some damage
and finish the other things.

------------------------------D2-08: Fluffy Flier------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

  Uwattyo, Adventurous Boy
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 38 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Investigation            |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: The Neslowe Passage  |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 5410 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Emperor Scale x1           |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation  44   |                                |
|        Aptitude  44   |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Summoning Scions
           - Summoning scions is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Three Sprites. One of them has White Wind, if you have a Blue Mage without it.
Another has Lv. ? Holy. All of them have Meteorite. The one without a second
Mischief skill has Stop and Undo from Time Magick, and they have assorted
R/P Abilities.

The Brown Chocobo has Choco Cure and Guard, along with Spellbound.
The Fire Drake comes with Fire Breath, and Mesmerize.
And the Banshee, of course, has Meteorite and Petrifying Rattle with
Counter and Death Strike.

Remember to get your Blue Mage and Beastmaster combo if you need White Wind 
here. Otherwise, bring whatever units you feel comfortable with. Those on the 
left side need good movement and standard jump in order to make it across, 
while those above will find less enemies in the beginning, so ranged units 
might favor the left side.

The Sprite and related share a weakness to dark, while the drake is weak to 
ice. The Chocobo as usual is weak to water, lightning and holy. So there's no
real mass exploit here.

If you take too long, the volcano will 'erupt', dealing damage to everyone,
but nothing too great.

----------------------------D2-09: Cake: The Recipe----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

Forbidden: Lightning
           - Weapons and abilities that use lightning are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Cockatrices, Floating Eyes, an Adamantita, and a Lilith. 
The Adamantitan has a Roundhouse-equivalent skill, among another that might
be able to destroy your armor. 

You should know the Lilith can Sleep everyone. And her Twister will drop your
HP in half too. The Axebeak, that red-colored Cockatrice, has Wake-Up Call,
which is like Smile Toss from a Juggler. It Quickens the target and has a very
nice range.

There are several recipe pages way at the back. So unless you have someone with
very high Jump, you'll probably have to go all around the path killing 
everything before you can finish this one. Of course, the traps might slow you
down.

PRECEDING QUEST: Cake: The Ingredients (D2-10)

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Bladed Weapons
           - Attacks with knives, swords, blades, sabers, katana, axes,
             knightswords, greatswords, and broadswords are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
The Rocktitan comes with Headbutt and Rain of Stone, nothing too impressive.
Both Pit Beasts have Sandstorm, Suffocate, and come equipped with Blur along
with Reveal.
Both Cockatrices have Scurry, Peck, and Scatter. They can Counter and have
Unscarred.
The Axebeak comes with Stone Breath (careful), Peck, and Wake-Up Call. It
has Bonecrusher and Death Strike.

Seriously, your main problem would be that Stone Breath, if it's even a 
problem. You can do the usual disabling or what not and be done with it. 

Some are weak to Water, the other to Wind. An Illusionist can always be 
useful here. There are a few traps around, too.

At least we got side-attacks... Anyway, yes, you are going to have to gather
all of the shimmering spots.

You have your standard array of beast enemies. Dreamhares with Blind and
Silence. Deadly Nightshade who can lower your Speed or Berserk you.
A Hoppy Bunny who can Sleep, Charm, or Haste a small area. And of course, a
Sprite with Slow, Stop, and her Meteorite. She will auto-Regen whenever she
gets hit, so make them count.

Choron. Err, I mean. Get the enemies as they come for you and then gather
the papers at the end if you don't want to be bothered. I suggest taking out
that Hoppy Bunny QUICK before it Hastens everyone.

------------------------D2-14: Maintaining the Balance-------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
The creatures of Tramdine Fens are on the bring of catastrophe! A swell in the
number of antlions has driven the native cockatrices into decline. If something
isn't done, they'll disappear from the fens altogether. Please travel to 
Tramdine Fens and thin the antlion population.
  Baran, Society for the Protection of Monsters
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 27 | Fee:  400 | Days: 20 |

Forbidden: Reaction Abilities
           - Reaction abilities are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Again, go through your deployed units and take off R-Abilities if you don't
want to break this one.

Ok, so it's 5 Antlions. Between all of them, they have a combination of the
usual moves. From Sandstorm (Blue Magick), to Mucus, Saliva, Bile, and
Suffocate.

They also have varying Reactions. Counter, Blue, Archer's Bane, even a 
Regenerate! 

But in the end, they are Antlions. Nothing too awesome.

---------------------------D2-15: The Natural Order----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Natural habitats in the Tramdine Fens are in danger! Cockatrice wattles blot 
out the sun, and they're eating everything in sight!

We need someone to eradicate those cockatrices. They are a blight upon the 
land.
  Goran, Friends of the Fen
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 29 | Fee:  400 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|

PRECEDING QUEST: Maintaining the Balance (D2-14)

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Grouping 
           - Ending the turn next to another unit is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yeah, not a fun law.

You are fighting 5 cockatrices. They have a combination of the next skills:
Scurry (Haste), Peck, Stone Breath (Petrify), Wake-Up Call (Quicken), and
Scatter (Evasion Up).
R and P Abilities vary from Unscarred, to Avoid Traps, or Counter to Critical:
whatever.

You can either bring some Illusionists or the like to weaken them from afar
and deal the finishers using ranged units (or ranged attacks, like Rend Air)
to avoid breaking the law if you are going for it. Otherwise, just rush the
lights out of them.

My Illusionist+Scholar combo brought them down in 3 actions.
Deluge, random tome, Illusinist gets some MP, Deluge again. Win.

           - Weapons and abilities that use lightning are forbidden.

the quest, but that's me guessing.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This is the lite version of 'Cleaning to Ordalia', basically.
In some cases, you might not find enemies, making this is an easy success.
In odd ocassions, you can find yourself facing a few Mimics, which your
characters might conclude being the rats. They are mimics, they are easy.
(Thanks to Jan Chu for this note)

Someone with high MOV or someone with Haste could be useful, but not
necessary.

--------------------------D3-10: Teach a Man to Fish---------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
The ultimate fisherman needs no rod, line, or hook. All he needs is what he was
born with. I am in training to become such a master, but monsters keep 
intruding on my fishing hole.
Someone drive them off!
  Enchan, Aspiring Fisherman
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 28 | Fee:  400 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Escort                   |
|   -                   | Members: 5                     |
|   -                   | Location: Tramdine Fens        |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   - Defender          |   - 4260 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Screamroot x1              |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation  18   |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  18   | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Protect Enchan and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Harming Nu Mou
           - Actions that harm nu mou are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
There are quite a few enemies in here. None of them too impressive, but a
good number no less. 

Your client will wander around the field (don't ask me why, last I checked,
he was training to catch fish, not to attract monsters).

Stop/Disable will be your friends here if you want to breeze through, unless
you are heavily overleveled.
You are facing a Nagaraja; using Ice Orb and Breath, along with the usual
Blue Magicks of a dragon. There's a Wendigo with the stapple, and three 
turtles: one Adamantitan that knows Crush and Matra Magick, and two Rockbiters
who aren't much of a problem. 
There's also a Worgen with the usual long-range skills.

It's not a difficult battle, actually. Just, don't use Scholars or the like; 
lest you hit your charge. Someone who can heal can also be useful, since he
might take a few hits.

to buy chocobos!

fine.

You can find them in...

Goud during Ashleaf, in random encounters.
You can also find them on both previous seasons in Skyfrost.

Or my favorite, the Teamwork II Clan Trial.

           - Weapons and abilities that use fire are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You'll be dealing with 3 Malboro and a Malboro King.
I'm sure you already know how they work; that is, stay apart to avoid getting
many of your units hit with their breaths.

The Fire law is not a problem here, since Malboros aren't even weak to the 
element to begin with. Pack an Illusionist, Elementalist, or any other magick
user that can blast them with Wind elements and you got yourself a party.

How you do it is no different, get them down, and victory is yours.

--------------------------D4-07: I've Been Had, Kupo!--------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Kupo!!! I've been had, kupo, had!!!

"Super special hammer," my pom=pom! This is just an ungainly lump of iron!
That alchemist pulled a fast one on me!

Someone get that ne'er-do-well and take back my 20,000 gil, kupo! His name's
Nezzel!
  Craftsmoogles' League Foreman
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 32 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Defeat Mark              |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Bisga Greenlands     |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 1960 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Kalos x3                   |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  28   | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability  28   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat Nezzel the Alchemist!
Forbidden: Height >= 10
           - Moving to a tile with a height of 10 or more is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Well, that's certainly new...
Remember, you gotta end the turn there to violate the law. Walking through
but ending below that height is okay.

There's a Black Mage way back with all -ras, and Blizzaga, plus Pierce.
The Time Mage has Slow and Stop.
A Green Mage with Blind, Silence and Sleep.
The White Monk knows Roundhouse, Air Render, and Earth Render. 
The Thief has Steal Gil, and borrows Iai Blow and Blade Bash from the
Parivir. He can Counter too.
Nezzel, our mark, knows Flare, Poison, and Toad. For better or worst, he has
Absorb MP coupled with Turbo MP.

Remember your objective is taking him down. Since he starts close enough,
and isn't quite to immune to anything, you can get someone fast to take care
of him with Stop or Immobilize and quickly finish him off to end the
battle quickly. 

Oh yes, it's Ezzel. Enjoy your new gift.

Magick might be difficult to use on this one. In fact, you must downright melee
them down unless you wanna break the law.

Both Marshmallows are the same, Unction, Merge and Fire.
The Bombs, two have Self-destruct, but only the one without it has Critical:
Quicken. All can use Flame Attack and Fire.
The Grenade has Flame Attack, Spark and Fira. Plus it comes with Bonecrusher 
and Geomancy.

You can use magick, but the mage has to stand next to the unit and not hit 
anything else inside the AoE. 
Otherwise, equip some ice/water weapons, and bring it to them.

--------------------------D5-01: Graszton Nightwatch---------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

a similar pattern, indicating that a single group may be responsible.
For the swift resolution of this case, and the safety of our citizens, we are
currently accepting applications to join the Graszton Nightwatch.
  Jylland Sovereignty Society
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 40 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |

|     Negotiation  44   |                                |

Forbidden: Targeting Distant Units
           - Actions targeting units two or more tiles away are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Like all the other Nightwatch missions (if you've done any), you'll be facing
3 Baknamy and a Luchorpan.
All 3 Baknamy come with Magick Hammer and Goblin Attack. They have a 
combination of Counter, Archer's Bane and Blur. For Passive, they have
either Death Strike, Reveal and Spellbound.
The Lurchopan has Magick Hammer along with Mutilate. He can Blink Counter,
and has Resistance Up.

On this one, you'll be supported by Chita's Weaponers, a combination of a
Gladiator, and 2 Warriors. There's no real logic on this fight, and no
difficulty at all.

As with all other Nightwatch missions, you'll get a scrap of paper in the end.

The Ghosts can use Sleep/Silence Touch. One has Flash, the other has Dark.

Both Ahriman have Demonic Gaze and Bewitching Gaze.

to them sometimes. Especially if you are trying to banish them from the 
battlefield.

You'll end up in Moorabella after the quest.

-------------------------E1-01: Bonga Bugle - Goldsun--------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

This month: the Field of Hidden Treasures! A place where treasures are said to
spring into being beneath the ground...all by themselves!

Reporting assistants wanted.
  Head Editor, Bonga Bugle
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 22 | Fee:  400 | Days:  8 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      | Month required:
| Items:                | Type: Meet Objectives          |   Goldsun
|   -                   | Members: 1                     |
|   -                   | Location: Baptiste Hill        |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 1750 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Recall Grass x1            |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork       | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability  9    | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Find three treasures!
Forbidden: Attack
           - Attacking is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Oh yeah, I remember this one... It's time for some badly drawn ASCII map.
Basically, you can search around for items. There is no real time limit, but
you don't want to be here half a day, do you?

I'm sure you can figure out the point of view on this map. Just tilt it a bit
and you should get it. Stand on the spot (not in front, to the side, IN the
spot) and use the special command.

Treasures are picked first from a pool of treasure, and then you get a random
treasure from said random pool of treasure. So it's a double random chance.
So no, I won't even bother trying to log them all. Just cross your fingers and
hope you get cool stuff. =D

I'll just say the game tells you the 'pool'-type of treasure when you check. 
So feel free to go around looking for the best spots.

------------------------E1-02: Bonga Bugle - Silversun-------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

                       | Rank: 18 | Fee:  300 | Days:  8 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      | Month required:
| Items:                | Type: Escort                   |   Silversun
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Graszton             |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 3030 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Trusty Frying Pan x4       |
| Talents:              |   - Turtle Shell x2            |
|     Negotiation  7    |   - Dipraeu Bronze x7          |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  7    | Dispatch? No                   |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defend the Head Editor and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Copycat
           - Using the same action as the preceding unit is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
I hope you got good memory. The moment you do the very same action as the
last guy (enemy or ally, doesn't matter), the law is broken. Categories don't
matter. Meaning if you have two Black Mages, you could do Fire and then
Blizzard and it's ok. This applies to the Attack command to! Wait is ok.

You are fighting undead enemies. These guys will come back to 'life' after a
certain amount of turns have passed once you've downed them.
So you gotta move quickly. Even if you dispose of them with Burial, 
Exorcise or using a Phoenix Down on their tombstone, more will appear. 
You gotta keep all 5 down at the same time. 

Remember the Head Editor must remain alive. Toss him an item or a heal if
he takes a hit for whatever reason. 

The Ghosts have mostly status skills, from Blind, to Silence to Sleep. One
of them also has Dark magick. The Deathscythes are the fun ones. They can
Doom you, put you on Stop and Sleep at the same time, or simply go bondage
with you and do a Immobilize+Disable combo breaker.

There's no real way to get this done easily. Break your way through their
lines and focus-fire them down. They can heal themselves by casting Dark
or Darkra. Alternatively, you can Cure/Cura/Curaga yourself and hurt them
at the same time. =D -- If you are really desperate, Potions and Hi-Potions 
will hurt them as well.

There are traps around, too. This fight requires a lot of horsepower, or
some horrid luck to get several exorcisms in a row.

Maple Leaf sends a note that Faerie Shoes, the teleport-granting accessory can
sometimes be obtained from the treasure pot in this quest.
_______________________________________________________________________________

Objective: Help the Bonga Bugle get its scoop!
Forbidden: Ranged Weapons
           - Attacks with bows, greatbows, guns, hand-cannons, and cards are
             forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
I hate the Bonga Bugle quests for some reason...
Anyway, no ranged weapons. Make it up close and personal, or rain magicks from
behind.

I love this. Your enemies are the "Jylland Free Press". That's just priceless.
(No pun intended)
Anyway... You are fighting a crappy Ninja (he doesn't even have Dual Wield!)
who can use Water Veil (low chance of Silence). A Time Mage with Haste and
Quicken. A Green Mage with Shell and Leap, and a Geomancer with Shining 
Flare (usable when sun is shining) and Nature's Embrace (usable only on natural
terrain). 

They shouldn't give you too much trouble; compared to say, the Silversun 
edition. Just rush them and try to keep them from getting close to that
Illusionist all the way up (The Guest unit) and the Head Editor. In order
to finish the quest, stand next to the Illusionist and WAIT. There is no
special command.

Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Harming the Weak
           - Actions that harm a lower level unit are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
I broke this law in two seconds flat. Enemies go from 36 to 40.

All enemies are like you know them. Hoppy Bunny can Haste and use War Dance.
Nidhogg has Fireball and Fire Breath, plus Dragon Force.
Malboro... You know the deal with them already.
Werewolf can Roar, as usual. Assault and Slug, plus Blink Counter.
Deathscythe can use Doom, Shackle and Darkra.

A simple, straight-forward fight. Get your weakest units if you don't want to
break the law (assuming your weakest units are indeed weaker than the enemies)
or stick to R-Abilities for damage purposes, but that would take too long.

If you want, remember to get some of the skills here for your Blue Mage.

Oh, yes, there is a time factor in here.
With just a bit over the 2nd round (freaking enemy lived with 2 HP...), I got
a Nirvana.

 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Place 5 pots on the tree stumps!
Forbidden: Piercing Weapons
           - Attacks with rapiers and spears are forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This is a weird one, I'll tell you. I would say to kill them all, but they
keep coming. 

The Sprite has White Wind, a learnable Blue Magick that restores HP equal to
the amount of HP you have. So, let's say she has 100 HP. She will heal for 100
to whoever she uses it to. 

I think you can get from that she needs to go down quickly. Fortunately,
Sprites have very low HP compared to everything else. The Worgen can Immobilize
or Silence you with some of it Maw technicks. That's it, the Baknamy has 
nothing worth mentioning (no, Goblin Attack isn't worth mentioning). 

To put a pot, simply move next to a stump and select the Special Command,
"Place Urn". Be careful, enemies can run away with them.

|        Aptitude       |                                |

|    Adaptability  6    | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
You'll be selling stuff.
Customers will come in, asking for a certain kind of item (two-handed
weapons, for example) and you'll be given 3 choices, one of which fits their
petition. You can always close the DS and hop down to the item section if 
you need to check something. Sometimes the petitions are just downright 
simple.

Like a Tonberry King asking for an Elixir (literally, he asks for the 
Elixir). You might get repeated requests though.
The weird one was this guy asking for boxers. I think it's the Prima Donna's
answer.

After about 10 customers this charade should stop. You'll be given an extra 
reward depending on how many customers you satisfy.

Objective: Uphold the law and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Being Robbed
           - Having gil or items stolen is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Well, this law takes the icing of the cake for one of the most retarded I've
ever seen. 

Of course, among our enemies, we have several Thieves and a Viking with
Pickpocket. Switch your P-Abilities to Safeguard if you want or just take them
out from a distance before they can do anything.

Disabling them one way or another (Immobilize, Disable, what have you) can also
work to your advantage. That, or since the map is almost a bottleneck where
you start, put the units at the edge with Safeguard and use the center units
to rain the enemies down. 

Of course, having the Sake Keeping Clan Privilege makes things a lot cheaper
here.

I can hate the Bonga Bugle, but the pay on this one sure is nice.
You won't be fighting here. Set some fast units to get this done with though.

say. 

Here's what I got...

Non-resolutions: 1
 - Picking up gil of the streets now got easier. Not a resolution, mind you.

Being healthy: 5
 - Stay healthy to follow Prima Donna around.
 - Stay fit. Lift weights and stuff.
 - Do more work. And stay healthy to do more work. (Also under work-related)
 - Making money. Go away. Leave me alone. *Cries in corner*-- I think I'll just
   be healthy instead.
 - Go on a diet.
 
Wealth/Items: 5
 - Get a bundle of gil to get a bigger house.
 - Save gil to go to more of Prima Donna's concerts.
 - Collect weapons. Never got enough. 
 - Make money to go on lots of trips with her man.
 - Pulling off a big job or two on the future. 

Work-related: 2
 - Become a better auctioneer.
 - Do more work. And stay healthy to do more work. (Also under being healthy)
 
Retarded resolutions: 1    (It's the truth, they are)
 - Do one-hundred snowhumes.

WARNING. It does seem the answers are random, according to what I've read.
So pay attention. I only provided mine so you can see any differences.

Objective: Survey points of geological interest!
Forbidden: Bludgeoning Weapons
           - Attacks with rods, staves, poles, knuckles, hammers and maces are
             forbidden.
           
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yes, inspect all of the shimmering stuff.
For our monster friends; we got the Ahriman with their gaze attacks. You 
should know the deal already. Just try to face away from them.

a melee range attack.

The Zombies can Drain you. One of them also has Dark along with Miasma, an area
skill that can Poison. 

Remember that without exorcising the zombies or using similar skills, you gotta

And there are traps, as usual.

           - Use of non-elemental weapons and abilities is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This... Well, you'll get 50 freaking gil. We sure as hell ain't taking 50
gil for that crystal right? No way. Let's kick their asses.

This is a funky law. Whatever you use must include an elemental effect or it's
bye-bye to the law. You can probably get away easily using skills, but if you
do counter or the like... Try to use elemental weapons.

Your party will be split in 3 even groups, each one dealing with a Seeq and a
Jelly of one element or another. 

The Ranger by the Ice Flan has Sten Needle, Love Potion and Camouflage. Plus
Return Fire. The Ice Flan has Acid and Blizzara/ga spells. Plus Pierce.

The Ranger by the Red Marshmallow has Silence Gas, Leech and Mirror Items.
Careful with this guy; Mirror Items is so broken. The Marsmallow has Unction
and Merge, along with Fira. Plus Blood Price.

The Berserker by the Yellow Jelly has Scream (It's like Roar, but localized),
Furore, Ground Shaker and Smite of Rage; along with Blink Counter.
The Jelly has Acid, Thunder and Thundara, plus Pierce.

Split your party as you see fit. Just remember to go around equipping elemental
weapons (preferably outside of the three basic elements to avoid trouble with
the jellies) if you don't want to break the law. It's not THAT tough of a 
fight.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Remember to fight using skills instead of normal attacks, and be careful of
your counters.

There are three Headless, all of them got Brute Strength, but only one has
Sunder Earth; that one also has Absorb Damage. The rest have Bonecrusher.

Out of the two Deathscythes, they both have Darkga, and Doom. One has
Deep Sleep and the other one has Steal Thoughts (Addle). They both know
Pierce as well.

And the Lamashtu, of course. She has Poisonous Frog, Kiss, and Eternal
Sleep. This last one is not so fun. She will try to Sleep, Doom, and sometimes
even Stop ALL UNITS, at once. And she pretty much does this right away. 
If you have a fast unit who can disable her quickly, do so. 

Thamas is a Fusilier with the Confuse, Blind, and Silence shots along with
Concentration. Since you can only get 5 units here, try to bring someone 
with Exorcise/Burial or the Item command for those Deathscythes.

There's really not much else you can say about this one. Though you are going 
to hate that Eternal Sleep. If you want, bring Debuff Resistance, then Clan
Privilege, or someone with the Item Command. Extra points if it's a Summoner
using Phoenix instead.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Well, this is just no fun. Be sure to check your gear for any elemental

kill it.

Drain Touch. The Ghoul is the only one who can cast Dark Magick, with Darkra.
Otherwise, you are dealing with an Alchemist who has Proto Meteor, and Halve
MP to boot.

Move in to attack, kill whatever comes to you as it comes to you. Remember
to exorcise or what not the undead and focus on the Alchemist.

¯¯¯¯¯

Our Ninja friend has Throw, and Fire/Water Veil (Confuse/Silence, each).
No Dual Wield because he sucks.

Take them as they come to you, there's not much else to this battle since you
start so close together. Have your fun with them if you want. Knowing the
average team, the law shouldn't be a problem for you. So waltz in and take
them out. 

Oh, right, the Blue Mage can absorb Earth, so don't be surprised if he does a
Sandstorm on himself. 

Also... What's up with the taste of the quest petitioner... Oh God...

--------------------------E4-05: The Honorable Thing---------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Ah, the sweet lips of my love. My one pleasure in this life. But the vile
Balfonne has stolen a kiss from my darling Preme, and with it, her love. I 
could not let such a slight go unanswered!

I immediately challenged Balfonne to a duel to settle this point of honor, but 
sadly, urgent matters prevent me from dealing with the scoundrel myself. I seek
someone to do battle in my place.
  Hseaunon, Preme's True Love
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 36 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Battle                   |
|   -                   | Members: 6                     |
|   -                   | Location: Ruins of Delgantua   |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   -                   |   - 4590 Gil                   |
|   -                   |   - Putrid Liquid x2           |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  33   | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  33   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Buffs
           - Buffs are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
We have... a White Mage with Cure, Cura and Refresh. Turbo MP to boot.
The Parivir can Counter, and has Wind Slash, Blade Bash (Immobilize) and 
Hoarfrost Blade (Slow). 
The Hunter can Arm and Leg Shot, plus Vitals Shot. 
A Defender with Whirl Burst, Mow Down and Bulwark. No shield, but is using
Doublehand.
Cannoneer with Mortar and Target. Plus Concentration.

Wait... Where's Balfonne? Oh...

The Law shouldn't hurt you that much. 'sides, you can still debuff them, so 
it's a fair trade. Bring a mixture of melee and long-distance units. Rush
the Parivir and rain magicks or arrows (except to the Hunter) and you should
take them down without much trouble.

Just be careful of the Parivir's Hoarfrost Blade. It hurts. And that the Hunter
might decide to spam you with Arm Shot.

-------------------------E4-10: Watching the Watchers--------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

  Zupp, Goug Youth Alliance

                       | Rank: 38 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |

| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation       |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  44   | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability  44   | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Piercing Weapons
           - Attacks with rapiers and spears are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Well, I only see one Parivir... With Hoarfrost Blade, Fire/Water Veil, plus
Doublehand and Reflex. He also has a Ninja Tabi. =3
The Fighter has Wild Swing, Air Blast, and Back Draft. MP Shield and
Doublehand included.
The Master Monk has Pummel, Dark Fist, Withering Strike, and Cross-counter.
He packs a Counter with Death Strike.
The Berserker has Scream, Furore, Ground Shaker, and Smite of Rage. He
has Counter along with Charged Attacks as well.

If you are feeling lucky, you can try stealing those Ninja Tabi. Unfortunately,
he has a Black Garb, so you can't stop him. Of course, you can rend his armor
then stop him. Or just go for it. Mind you, you gotta get those Genji Gloves
first.

Otherwise, it's your standard battle.
_______________________________________________________________________________
----------------------E4-15: Inspiration or Perspiration?----------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
I seek a guide to escort me to Neslowe Peak. There I intend to stand beside
a river of boiling magma and feel its heat on my skin. So inspired, I will 
paint a picture to capture that heat, making it real for all who look on it!
You will be well rewarded for your trouble.
  Master Painter Kismunt
                        _________________________________
                       | Rank: 40 | Fee:  500 | Days: 20 |
|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯|
|REQUIREMENTS           |QUEST INFO                      |
| Items:                | Type: Escort                   |
|   -                   | Members: 5                     |
|   -                   | Location: The Neslowe Passage  |
| Dispatch/Reccomended  | Rewards:                       |
|   - Defender          |   - 10820 Gil                  |
|   -                   |   - Stradivari x1              |
| Talents:              |                                |
|     Negotiation  44   |                                |
|        Aptitude       |                                |
|        Teamwork  44   | Dispatch? Yes                  |
|    Adaptability       | Cancellable? Yes               |
 ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Objective: Protect your guest and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Instruments and Books
           - Attacks with instruments and books are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Lots of fire enemies. Or rather, only fire enemies.

Three bombs; two of them have Self-destruct, all have Flame Attack. The one
without Self-destruct knows Firaga, while all share Fira. They have a 
combination of Absorb MP, Counter, Blood Price and Unscarred.

The Red Marshmallow has Unction and Merge, along with Fira.
Both Drakes have Fire Breath, along with Mesmerize; though one has Expose 
Weakness and the other Mighty Guard. The last two are Blue Mage magicks, in 
case you have one.

Either equip yourself with ice weapons and lay it to them, or bring
someone with magic of the element. Illusionists are nice; summoners can be 
useful, and black mages are the staple, of course.

Otherwise, there's not much more to this fight. Remember though, they are going
to do the usual thing with their magick and attacks where they hit themselves
to heal HP. Sure, that guy is gonna wander around taking hits, but you should
be able to back him up just fine. 

If you take too long, the volcano will eventually 'erupt', dealing damage to 
ALL units on screen.
(Thanks to Joshua Ficklin for correcting me on this)

-------------------------------03. Clan Section--------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
_______________________________________________________________________________
[031]-------------------------03.01. Clan Report-------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
The Clan Report is divided into 25 segments, from A to E horizontally, and
1 to 5 vertically. Each segment holding 16 different missions.

This section is merely a compilation of all the missions so that you can find
the name of any quest you might be missing and look in the Quests section above
to find the requirements to obtain the quest and everything else. Though
generally, it's just advancing the story or being on a certain month.

Within each section, quests are ordered in a left-to-right order from top-to-
bottom. I will only provide the name and the space (such as 05, or 12) it takes
on that block, for space reasons of course. More detailed information can be
found in its own section above.
Those marked with an S in ()s are Story Missions. Those with a J are missions 
that grant a Job.

SECTION A
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
