[032]-----------------------03.02. Clan Privileges-----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Here's a list of all the Clan Privileges and where to obtain them. Do note 
I'm updating these as I go through the game. In the case I don't have the
title of the trial, I'll only give the trial name.

Remember that any given difficulty that awards a certain rank of a privilege
can also grant the lower ranks if you don't have them. So you could go from
Power Up 1 to 5 using the highest difficulty of Adaptability II if you wanted.

POWER UP
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases damage given and reduces damage taken. Effect increases 
           with rank.
    Ranks: 1: Start with.
           2: Machinist difficulty of 'Aptitude I'.
           3: Dab Hands difficulty of 'Aptitude I'.
           4: Wrights difficulty of 'Aptitude II'.
           5: Master Artificers difficulty of 'Aptitude II'.

AGILITY UP
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases evasion and chance to hit enemies. Effect increases with
           rank.
    Ranks: 1: Start with.
           2: Suveyors difficulty of 'Adaptability I'.
           3: Mimics difficulty of 'Adaptability I'.
           4: Trailblazers difficulty of 'Adaptability II'.
           5: Master Improvisers difficulty of 'Adaptability II'.

SPEED UP
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases speed. Effect increases with rank.
    Ranks: 1: Start with.
           2: Relations difficulty of 'Teamwork I'.
           3: Old Friends difficulty of 'Teamwork I'.
           4: Boon Companions difficulty of 'Teamwork II'.
           5: Master Allies difficulty of 'Teamwork II'.

LUCK
¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increase Critical Hit chance, the appearance of Opportunity 
           commands, and get better drops. Effect increases with rank. 
    Ranks: 1: Start with.
           2: Barterers difficulty of 'Negotiations I'.
           3: Orators difficulty of 'Negotiations I'.
           4: Conciliators difficulty of 'Negotiations II'.
           5: Master Negotiations difficulty of 'Negotiations II'.

MOVE UP
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases the movement range of all characters by +1/+2.
    Ranks: 1: Adepts difficulty of 'General Training II'.
           2: Master Adventurers of 'General Training II'.
           
EMPOWERED HUMES
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases several parameters for Hume units.
    Ranks: 1: Novices difficulty of 'General Training I'.

EMPOWERED BANGAA
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases several parameters for Bangaa units.
    Ranks: 1: Grease Monkeys difficulty of 'Aptitude I'.

EMPOWERED NU MOU
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases several parameters for Nu Mou units.
    Ranks: 1: Hagglers difficulty of 'Negotiations I'.

EMPOWERED VIERA
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases several parameters for Viera units.
    Ranks: 1: Acquaintances difficulty of 'Teamwork I'.

EMPOWERED MOOGLES
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases several parameters for Moogle units.
    Ranks: 1: Scouts difficulty of 'Adaptability I'.

EMPOWERED SEEQ
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases several parameters for Seeq units.
    Ranks: 1: Craftsmen difficulty of 'Aptitude II'.

EMPOWERED GRIA
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases several parameters for Gria units.
    Ranks: 1: Comrades difficulty of 'Teamwork II'.

REGENE/REGENERA/REGENEGA
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Recovers 10%/12.5%/16.6% of your max HP every turn.
    Ranks: 1: Journeymen difficulty of 'General Training I'.
           2: Braves difficulty of 'General Training II'.
           3: Heroes difficulty of 'General Training II'.

MP CHANNELING
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases MP Regeneration from 10 to 20 per turn.
    Ranks: 1: Master Explorers difficulty of 'Aptitude-Adaptability'.

MP PRESERVATION
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Reduces the MP cost of skills by half.
    Ranks: 1: Master Hands difficulty of 'Teamwork-Aptitude'.

NON-ELEMENTAL ATTACKS
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Strips the elements of all actions (basic attack, spells, skills).
    Ranks: 1: Master Intercessors difficulty of 'Adaptability-Negotiation'.

DEBUFF RESISTANCE
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Protects all units against several debuffs. Protections are
           cummulative.
           R1: Poison.
           R2: Blind.
           R3: Silence.
           R4: Immobilize, and Disable.
           R5: Confuse, Charm, and Toad.
    Ranks: 1: Travelers difficulty of 'Aptitude-Adaptability'.
           2: Hands of Silver difficulty of 'Teamwork-Aptitude'.
           3: Outriders difficulty of 'Adaptability-Negotiation'.
           4: Champions difficulty of 'Negotiation-Teamwork'.
           5: Legends difficulty of 'General Training II'.

LIBRA
¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Allows you to see all traps on the map.
    Ranks: 1: Contrivers difficulty of 'Adaptability II'. 
              Or have a FFTA on the GBA slot when starting the game.

SMASH GAUGE BONUS
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases the rate at which the Smash Gauge fill up.
    Ranks: 1: Prospectors difficulty of 'Aptitude-Adaptability'.
           2: Pioneers difficulty of 'Aptitude-Adaptability'.
           3: Voyagers difficulty of 'Aptitude-Adaptability'.

BONUS AP
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases AP obtained after a quest by 20, 40, or 60, respectively.
    Ranks: 1: Speakers difficulty of 'Negotiation-Teamwork'.
           2: Lectors difficulty of 'Negotiation-Teamwork'.
           3: Advocates difficulty of 'Negotiation-Teamwork'.

BONUS EXP
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases EXP obtained after battle by 10%, 20%, or 30%.
    Ranks: 1: Hands of Steel difficulty of 'Teamwork-Aptitude'.
           2: Hands of Gold difficulty of 'Teamwork-Aptitude'.
           3: Hands of Divinity difficulty of 'Teamwork-Aptitude'.

BONUS GIL 
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases Gil obtained after a quest by 10%, 20%, or 30%.
    Ranks: 1: Followers difficulty of 'Adaptability-Negotiation'.
           2: Compromisers difficulty of 'Adaptability-Negotiation'.
           3: Catalysts difficulty of 'Adaptability-Negotiation'.

BONUS CP
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Increases CP obtained after a quest.
    Ranks: 1: Trainees difficulty of 'General Training I'.
           2: Professionals difficulty of 'General Training I'.
           3: Veterans difficulty of 'General Training I'.

ALWAYS COUNTER
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effect: Gives the Counter R-Ability to all units without an R-Ability set.
    Ranks: 1: Master Judicers difficulty of 'Negotiation-Teamwork'.

SAFE KEEPING
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
   Effects: Protects all of your items against being stolen or destroyed.
    Ranks: 1: Mediators difficulty of 'Negotiation II'.

[033]-------------------------03.03. Clan Titles-------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
This is only a compilation of the titles per given trial. Nothing else.

  2: Trainees                  2: Braves
  3: Journeymen                3: Heroes
  4: Professionals             4: Legends
  5: Veterans                  5: Master Adventurers

  3: Barterers                 3: Conciliators              3: Advocates

  5: Orators                   5: Master Negotiators        5: Master Judicers

  4: Technicians               4: Artisans                  4: Catalysts
  5: Dab Hands                 5: Mater Artificers       5: Master Intercessors

  3: Relations                 3: Boon Companions           3: Hands of Gold
  4: Confidantes               4: Countrymen               4: Hands of Divinity

  2: Observers                 2: Survivalists              2: Prospectors
  3: Surveyors                 3: Trailblazers              3: Pioneers
  4: Imitators                 4: Executors                 4: Voyagers

[034]-------------------------03.04. Clan Trials-------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Trials are basically quests, so to speak. You take them at the pub, pay CP 
(Clan Points) for them, and you go where they ask you to go.

Once there, a Judge will give you 5 different difficulties. The lower the
difficulty, the lower the bonuses and the simpler the clan privileges are.

PAY ATTENTION TO WHAT THE JUDGE SAYS. IN SOME TRIALS, BREAKING THE LAW WILL
END IN IMMEDIATE FAILURE. (Thanks to all the people who corrected me on this)

Speaking of clan privileges, sometimes, a particular difficulty might have
"multiple" ones, so to speak. Using Aptitude I for example.
Clearing rank 1 gives you Empowered Bangaa.
Rank 2 is nothing.
Rank 3 is Power Up 1, or Power Up 2 if you already had the former.
Rank 4 is nothing.
Rank 5 will give you Power Up 1. Power Up 2 if you had 1. And Power Up 3 if
you had 2. So you could get both Power Up 2 and 3 with this last rank, but you
would need to do the trial twice.

On with it.
_______________________________________________________________________________
------------------------Clan Trial: General Training I-------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Trial: General Training
    Rank: 15
    Days: 20
 Members: 6
Location: Baptiste Hill
Required: All Talents: 1
 
Objective: Defeat all foes! or Defeat all foes in # rounds!
Forbidden: Missing
           - Missing with an action is forbidden.
NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yes, that's one of the most ridiculous laws, EVER. Let me put it this way...
My Luso had Counter. Sprite comes up, hits him. I counter, miss. Law broken. =D
Fortunately, breaking the law won't mean you fail in this ocassion. 

I also hate the enemies though. They will try to Slow you down to a crawl and 
then finish you with their flimsy hits, or other assorted magicks. 
Read, Meteorite. Hits for... 50?

Gang up on them one by one. Try to have a mixture of ranged units, but 
preferably not many that use AoE attacks, such as Black Mages to minimize 
friendly fire.

CLAN RANKS & OBJECTIVES
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Novices: Defeat 4 enemies.
   +4 All Talens
   Clan Privilege: Empowered Hume

Trainees: Defeat 5 enemies.
   +8 All Talens
   Clan Privilege: Bonus CP 1

Journeymen: Defeat 6 enemies.
   +12 All Talens
   Clan Privilege: Regene

Professionals: Defeat 6 enemies in 4 turns.
   +16 All Talens
   Clan Privilege: Bonus CP 1 -> Bonus CP 2

Veterans: Defeat 6 enemies in 3 turns.
   +20 All Talens
   Quest Fees -5%
   Item Prices -5%
   Clan Privilege: Bonus CP 1 -> Bonus CP 2 -> Bonus CP 3
_______________________________________________________________________________
------------------------Clan Trial: General Training II------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Trial: General Training II
    Rank: 25
    Days: 20
 Members: 6
Location: Baptiste Hill
Required: All Talents: >40
 
Objective: Defeat all foes in # rounds!
Forbidden: >20 Damage
           - Dealing >20 damage is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
No, there's no need to respect the law, thankfully. And quite frankly, I 
wouldn't even bother.
You always begin with one Zaghnal, who is also lv45. He has the standard 
Werewolf set; from Roar, to Assault, to Slug. He has Blink Counter along
with Destroyer. Just gang up on him. Even if you are underleveled. 

If you finish before the reinforcements appear, you clear the trial ahead
of time.

CLAN RANKS & OBJECTIVES
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Adepts: Defeat all foes in 4 urns. 1 reinforcement appears after 3 turns.
   +24 All Talens
   Quest Fees -8%
   Item Prices -8%
   Clan Privilege: Move Up 1

Braves: Defeat all foes in 4 turns. 2 reinforcement appears after 3 turns.
   +28 All Talens
   Quest Fees -10%
   Item Prices -10%
   Clan Privilege: Regen -> Regenera

Heroes: Defeat all foes in 4 turns. 3 reinforcement appears after 2 turns.
   +32 All Talens
   Quest Fees -12%
   Item Prices -12%
   Clan Privilege: Regen -> Regenera -> Regenega

Legends: Defeat all foes in 3 turns. 2 reinforcement appears after 2 turns.
   +36 All Talens
   Quest Fees -16%
   Item Prices -16%
   Clan Privilege: Debuff Resistance 1 -> Debuff Resistance 2 -> 
                   Debuff Resistance 3 -> Debuff Resistance 4 -> 
                   Debuff Resistance 5

Master Adventurers: Defeat all foes in 3 turns. 3 reinforcement appears 
                    after 2 turns.
   +40 All Talens
   Quest Fees -20%
   Item Prices -20%
   Clan Privilege: Move Up 1 -> Move Up 2
_______________________________________________________________________________
---------------------------Clan Trial: Negotiation I---------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Trial: Negotiation I
    Rank: 10
    Days: 20
 Members: 6
Location: Camoa
Required: Negotiation Talent: 1

Objective: Uphold the law and defeat all foes.
Forbidden: Targeting Distant Units
           - Actions targeting units two or more tiles away are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Do note that you can use archers and mages all alike, just stand next to the 
enemy you are hitting and be sure nothing else is on range (speaking of 
magick).

Also, here's one you might dislike. If you critical hit something and it
gets pushed back, it will count against you since in the end, the unit is
away from you. Try to flank the enemies between two characters or similar.
(Thanks to a somewhat anonymous person)

--------------------------Clan Trial: Negotiation II---------------------------

Clan Trial: Negotiation II
    Rank: 20
    Days: 20
 Members: 6
Location: Moorabella
Required: Negotiation Talent: >20
 
Objective: Uphold the law and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Grouping
           - Ending the turn next to another unit is forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Yes, you must uphold the law. Yes, that means it's ranged attack time.
That, or magick. Or abilities that push back. Your call.

You are fighting tomatoes mostly. They shouldn't give you trouble. At all.
They have Blink Counter, but of course... There's a chance they'll miss when
countering. Like it happened to me. On the first attack. Christ...

want. Stack your units with MOV+ items and use your fastest units as well.

----------------------------Clan Trial: Teamwork I-----------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Trial: Teamwork I
    Rank: 10
    Days: 20
 Members: 6
Location: Camoa
Required: Teamwork Talent: 1
 
Objective: Uphold the law and defeat all foes!
Forbidden: Not Using MP
           - Abilities that don't consume MP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
No, you read it right. Abilities that DO NOT use MP are forbidden. Such as,
for example, all of your Thief skills. They don't require MP.
You can, therefore, use your Black Mages and what not. In fact, they are
almost recommended, as the things you are fighting are weak to everything.

Their skills hurt, so spread out. They have Counter, but a very low HP
count.

Yes, the Attack command is allowed. Don't fret.

CLAN RANKS & OBJECTIVES

   -4 Adaptability

   -5 Adaptability

Well... Unless you got Scion summoning items to spare, you might want to finish
this one quickly. Though I'd say that is a verys stupid law. 

Fortunately, the enemies aren't so strong. So it shouldn't be too hard to
finish this quest before getting the Smash Gauge so high. Even if all enemies
here have Regenerate.

Fun fact, you can mount the Chocobos here. I know that first-hand... XD

--------------------------Clan Trial: Adaptability I---------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Trial: Adaptability I
    Rank: 10
    Days: 20
 Members: 6
Location: Camoa
Required: Adaptability Talent: 1
 
Objective: Drive off all foes in # rounds!
Forbidden: Fire, Ice, Lightning
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire, ice, or lightning are 
             forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This one is a tad... interesting. You can't really fight. Flans are highly
resistant to physical attacks, and since you can't use the 3 main elements...

Stand next to it, and you should see an Experiment command. Select it. 
You will get three options. Which one you choose, however...

Whenever it's the Flan's turn, it will do one of 3 things.
Turn around once and jump.
Bend backwards twice.
Or simply turn around.

If it turns once and then makes a small jump. Threaten it.
Small weird dance backwards? Surprise!
Turn around like a maniac with a twirl? Stare it down. D:

CLAN RANKS & OBJECTIVES
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

   -3 Teamwork
   Clan Privilege: Empowered Moogles

   +7 Adaptability
   -4 Teamwork

   +10 Adaptability
   -5 Teamwork
   Clan Privilege: Agility Up 1 -> Agility Up 2

   +13 Adaptability
   -6 Teamwork

--------------------------Clan Trial: Adaptability II--------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Trial: Adaptability II
    Rank: 20
    Days: 20
 Members: 6
Location: Moorabella
Required: Adaptability Talent: >20
 
Objective: Drive off all foes in # rounds!
Forbidden: Fire, Ice, Lightning
           - Weapons and abilities that use fire, ice, or lightning are
             forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
This is like Adaptability I, but we now add Ice Flans and Yellow Jellies to 
the mix, each one with their own commands. 

Red Marshmallow:
  Rotate then Jump? Threaten.
  Bend backwards twice? Surprise.
  Twirl around? Stare Down.

Ice Flan: 
  Slide sideways? Insult.
  Threat you? Slap.
  Stick his tongue at you? Eat.

Yellow Jelly: 
  Squat down? Poke.
  Jump twice? Pat.
  Collapse? Praise.

Seriously... I don't know which one is easier... This, or Adaptability II...

-----------------------Clan Trial: Negotiation-Teamwork------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Trial: Negotiation-Teamwork
    Rank: 15
    Days: 20
 Members: 6
Location: The Bisga Greenlands
Required: Negotiation Talent: >10
          Teamwork Talent: >10
 
Objective: Uphold the law and defeat all foes in # rounds!
Forbidden: Using MP
           - Actions that consume MP are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Wee~ Tonberries. 
You can't use MP. You break the law, you fail this one. 

These Tonberries all have Critical: Quicken, and their Revenge skills
really hurt. They can bring you down to 1 HP, reduce your HP by 90%, 
do more damage according to how many units the target has killed, and even
stalk you.

Try to focus fire them down. And remember, no MP. Did I mention they take
half-damage from EVERY element.

---------------------Clan Trial: Adaptability-Negotiation----------------------

    Rank: 15
    Days: 20
 Members: 6
Location: Graszton
Required: Adaptability Talent: >10
          Negotiation Talent: >10
 
Objective: Uphold the law and survive # rounds!
Forbidden: Harming the Weak
           - Actions that harm a lower level unit are forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
For this trial, you only need to hold on while taking their hits and without
hitting harming the weak (though enemies here go from lv23 up to 40, depending
on the rank).

Bring White Mages or use items. Up to you. If you break the law, it's over.
The enemy might not even attack you in some cases.

-------------------------Clan Trial: Teamwork-Aptitude-------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Trial: Teamwork-Aptitude
    Rank: 15
    Days: 20
 Members: 6
Location: The Aldanna Range
Required: Teamwork Talent: >10
          Aptitude Talent: >10
 
Objective: Examine the magick urn # times in # rounds!
Forbidden: Ranged Weapons
           - Attacks with bows, greatbows, guns, hand-cannons, and cards are
             forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
No need to respect the law, by the way.
The objective here is to inspect the urn. Dedicate your fastest, high-MOV units
to go for the urn while the rest focus on trying to take down the eyes. Also,
keep one unit ready to smack your own guys if they get confused.

And be sure to gaze away. The magick urn disappears and reappears on a random
spot when examined, so you also have to try to keep your units spread out. 

It was brought to my attention (Thanks to Jonathan Emes) that the Ahrimans
here can also use Roulette. Cross your fingers if that happens. 

CLAN RANKS & OBJECTIVES
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

-----------------------Clan Trial: Aptitude-Adaptability-----------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Clan Trial: Aptitude-Adaptability
    Rank: 15
    Days: 20
 Members: 6
Location: The Galerria Deep
Required: Aptitude Talent: >10
          Adaptability Talent: >10
 
Objective: Gather the shimmering lights in # rounds!
Forbidden: Ranged Weapons
           - Attacks with bows, greatbows, guns, hand-cannons, and cards are
             forbidden.

NOTES
¯¯¯¯¯
Remember the objective are the lights, not fighting. Kill if you must on the 
way, but try not to delay your turns.

It works if you split your group, from the faster runners to get the lights, to
the slower ones to kill everything in sight.

Each time you capture a light, they all move around, so it also pays to spread
out your team.

[041]--------------------------04.01. Hume Jobs--------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
JOB LIST
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
A-Abilities list the amount of Active Abilities you need from a certain job
and if necessary, any pre-requisite quest you must complete.
 ___________________________________________________________
|   Job Name   |   A-Abilities   | Quest                    |
|--------------+-----------------+--------------------------|
| Soldier      | ---             | ---                      |
|--------------+-----------------+--------------------------|
| Thief        | Soldier x1      | ---                      |
|--------------+-----------------+--------------------------|
| White Mage   | ---             | ---                      |
|--------------+-----------------+--------------------------|
| Black Mage   | White Mage x1   | ---                      |
|--------------+-----------------+--------------------------|
| Archer       | Soldier x1      | ---                      |
|--------------+-----------------+--------------------------|
| Paladin      | Soldier x3      | ---                      |
|--------------+-----------------+--------------------------|

SOLDIER
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯ 
 Description: Soldiers employ the arts of war to weaken and slow opponents.
Requirements: None.
   Base Move: 2
   Base Jump: 4
 
 Comments: The staple of Humes. They got somewhat useful skills depending on 
 the situation, but their stat growth is... mediocre. Get Shieldbearer and
 any Active abilities you need to unlock jobs, then you might leave it
 behind.

THIEF
¯¯¯¯¯   
  Description: Dextrous and nimble, thieves excel at stealing from the unwary
               and evading blows.
 Requirements: Soldier x1
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 3
    
 Comments: While the Thief class isn't as awesome as before, since they can
 no longer steal equipped items (sans for Accessories), they get Counter
 and the other Steal Loot skills can get you some items for the bazaar.
 Decent speed growth too. You need some experience in this for the Ninja.

WHITE MAGE
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯        
  Description: White mages use curing magicks to mend allies' wounds and
               restore them to health.
 Requirements: None.
    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: The mother of all healers. So to speak. White Mages focus on
 one thing; healing of course. And that's it. There's not much difference
 between Hume and Viera WHM. 'cept Viera get a bit more MP, and Nu Mou also
 get a bit more MP and Resistance.

BLACK MAGE
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯        
  Description: Black mages wield the elemental powers of fire, ice, and
               lightning to decimate foes.
 Requirements: White Mage x1
    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 2
    
 Comments: Black Mages are the opposite to White Mages. They focus on
 destroying the enemies with all kinds of elemental spells. 
 Differences between races include more MP (Moogles) or more Magick
 Power (Nu Mou). Humes are between those two.

ARCHER
¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Archers stand out of harm's way, raining arrows upon their
               foes from afar.
 Requirements: Soldier x1
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: Archers are the basic ranged unit. They have a few useful skills
 to mess with the enemy, and Focus can increase the damage of your next
 action if you couldn't reach the enemy on the move. Concentration is alright.
 Differences between Hume and Viera are that Viera have better speed and
 attack growth.

PALADIN
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Their sturdy defense enables paladins to endure punishing 
               attacks. They also possess a variety of abilities to aid their
               allies.
 Requirements: Soldier x3
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 2
    
 Comments: Paladins are basically the guys who do everything to keep your
 party alive. They can take damage for others and they'll have their way with
 the undead so that you won't even see them coming back.

FIGHTER

               defense.
 Requirements: Soldier x3. Quest: "To Be a Fighter"
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: Fighters are the 2nd strongest hume class, in terms of raw attack
 power. They got a nice array of skills; up close or from a bit of range,
 enemies will feel their strength.

PARIVIR
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: The parivir are swift and skillful combatants who finish
               their foes with flair.
 Requirements: Fighter x4. Quest: "The Eastwatch"
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: Originally called Yojimbo, these guys will dish out the pain...
 And feel it too. They got the highest Hume Attack value, but also the
 lowest physical defense value. Pair them up with something else and you'll
 tear the battlefield.

NINJA
¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Powerful swordsmen who can wield two weapons at once and use
               ninjutsu to evoke the power of wood and earth.
 Requirements: Thief x4
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 3
 
 Comments: Can you say Dual Wield? Sure, it takes 999 AP, but it's so very
 damn worth it. Pair it up with the attack power of a Parivir, and you got
 yourself a killing machine; especially if you stack a Counter on the Ninja.
 Their other skills are useful if you want to mess with the enemy, but their
 damage is low.

ILLUSIONIST
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Mages who weave illusion to overcome their foes.
 Requirements: White Mage x2, Black Mage x4
    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: Illusionists require a lot of MP to work effectively, but the
 damage they do is fairly ok, if you can put up with the wait for that MP.
 That, or Haste/Ether them to make it work quickly. They got awesome MP and
 Magick growth, but everything else hits the floor.

BLUE MAGE
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: These peculiar mages can learn the abilities of beasts and
               monsters, making them their own.
 Requirements: Black Mage x1
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 2

 Due to the differences with Blue Mage and the rest, instead of the item you
 learn a skill from, I will list the monster type. I also won't list if they
 are Active because, well... They are. Do note you might have to control
 with a Beastmaster some enemies or just be too damn lucky.
 
 Comments: Blue Mages, one of my favorite classes back in FF XI. They'll get
 the skills from enemies, and use them back. For the same reason though, it's
 a pain to get one sometimes, since you gotta hunt down those enemies and then
 cross your fingers.

HUNTER
¯¯¯¯¯¯

               finest when fighting monsters and other beasts of the wild.

    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 2

 now. Gria Hunters tend to do slightly better.

 have are fairly useful, especially if you enjoy playing with buffs/debuffs.

THIEF
¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Dextrous and nimble, thieves excel at stealing from the unwary
               and evading blows.
 Requirements: None.
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 3

 Comments: While the Thief class isn't as awesome as before, since they can
 no longer steal equipped items (sans for Accessories), they get Counter
 and the other Steal Loot skills can get you some items for the bazaar.
 Differences here are that Moogle thieves are slightly faster than Hume.

BLACK MAGE

               lightning to decimate foes.

 destroying the enemies with all kinds of elemental spells. 
 Differences between races include more MP (Moogles) or more Magick
 Power (Nu Mou). Humes are between those two.

               bravery, if not in height.

    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 2

 Fighter. Take some skills out, add some other focused on defense and stuff,
 and you got your Knight. They get an Ultima attack though.

FUSILIER

               that moogle fusilies fire them with the greatest skill.

    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 2

 to play with if you ask me. Regardless of what people have said, I've had very

TINKER
¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: One of many bands of machinist, the tinker wages battle with his
               clockwork creations.
 Requirements: Thief x2
    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: Tinkers are an odd ball. They got decent stat growth (or insane
 Resistance growth) and they get the Equip: Ribbon skill. Their abilities are
 like flipping a coun. You can affect all your team, or all the enemies. Both
 positive or negatively.

TIME MAGE
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: These accomplished mages can alter space and time.
 Requirements: Black Mage x5
    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 2
  
 Comments: Time Mages are what they are called, play with time. Haste, Slow,
 everything like that, these are your guys. They are exactly the same as 
 Nu Mou in stats, but since you usually pair a Time Mage with another magical
 class... You might want to leave this one to a Nu Mou.

CHOCOBO KNIGHT
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Chocobo knights harness the innate abilities of their 
               feathered mounts.
 Requirements: Animist x2. Quest: "Popocho's Chocobos"
    Base Move: 3 (Modified by Chocobo)
    Base Jump: 2 (Modified by Chocobo)
    
 Comments: Chocobo Riders are pure gimmick. You can only use the abilities
 depending on the Chocobo you are riding, and you can't access ANY sub-job
 abilities. Also, since the command to get off takes presedence over everything
 else, you won't be able to examine or talk to people mid-battle. 
 I'm not saying they are bad, just don't bring them to missions that require
 exploring or talking mid-battle. 
 
 Under Chocobos, you'll see letters represting the color of the Chocobo. 
  Y: Yellow       K: Black           B: Brown
  R: Red          G: Green           W: White
 Hop down to section 07.02 to see where to find Chocobos.

GLADIATOR
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Gladiators wake the magick that sleeps within the quiet steel of
               the blade with their sparring techniques.

    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: Gladiators have awesome Attack growth. The best of them all, and 
 they also got nice skills to deal some damage, since that's their job.
 They get an Ultima skill, but it costs MP. And you know how Bangaa feel about
 MP...

CANNONEER
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Cannoneers support the front line with fire form their 
               hand-cannons.
 Requirements: Dragoon x2. Quest: "The Bangaa Brotherhood"
    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 3
 
 Comments: Cannoneers, unlike their Moogle Flintlock counterparts don't need
 to charge for all of their attacks. Some of their skills affect your next 
 regular attack, which is a nice bonus; and the Shells can be used at any
 time. You might want to train them in a high Attack growth job first though.
 (Thanks DarkLink117 for correction my previously mistaken description)

TRICKSTER
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Tricksters spin a web of deception, twisting the flow of battle
               to their advantage.
 Requirements: Master Monk x2. Quest: "Sleight of Hand"
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 3
 
 Comments: Makes you wonder, are there female Bangaa? 'cause I've never seen
 one. Err... I mean, they get Equip: Ribbon, and are fast as all hell. The
 rest of their stats are just ok, but if you raise someone as a trickster, 
 espect that Bangaa to have mediocre attack power since these guys are more
 magickal oriented. Card weapons rock, though.

WHITE MAGE
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: White mages use curing magicks to mend allies' wounds and
               restore them to health.
 Requirements: None.
    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: The mother of all healers. So to speak. White Mages focus on
 one thing; healing of course. And that's it. There's not much difference
 between Hume and Viera WHM. 'cept Viera get a bit more MP, and Nu Mou also
 get a bit more MP and Resistance.

BLACK MAGE
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Black mages wield the elemental powers of fire, ice, and
               lightning to decimate foes.
 Requirements: None.
    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: Black Mages are the opposite to White Mages. They focus on
 destroying the enemies with all kinds of elemental spells. 
 Differences between races include more MP (Moogles) or more Magick
 Power (Nu Mou). Humes are between those two.

TIME MAGE
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: These accomplished mages can alter space and time.
 Requirements: Black Mage x5
    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: Time Mages are what they are called, play with time. Haste, Slow,
 everything like that, these are your guys. They are exactly the same as 
 Nu Mou in stats, but since you usually pair a Time Mage with another magical
 class... You might want to leave this one to a Nu Mou.

ILLUSIONIST
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Mages who weave illusion to overcome their foes.
 Requirements: White Mage x2, Black Mage x5
    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: Illusionists require a lot of MP to work effectively, but the
 damage they do is fairly ok, if you can put up with the wait for that MP.
 That, or Haste/Ether them to make it work quickly. They got awesome MP and
 Magick growth, but everything else hits the floor. Leave them where you
 start and just wait until they can do something, rinse and repeat.

ARCHER
¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Archers stand out of harm's way, raining arrows upon their
               foes from afar.
 Requirements: None.
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: Archers are the basic ranged unit. They have a few useful skills
 to mess with the enemy, and Focus can increase the damage of your next
 action if you couldn't reach the enemy on the move. Concentration is alright.
 Differences between Hume and Viera are that Viera have better speed and
 attack growth.

GEOMANCER
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Geomancers utilize the power of the terrain and the weather
               to devastating effect.
 Requirements: Hunter x1, Quest: "Geomancer's Way" All 4.
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 1
 
 Comments: Geomancers have great DEF and RES growth. Their versatility however
 comes at the price they can't always use their skills when they want. It 
 depends entirely on the place.

RANGER
¯¯¯¯¯¯
  Description: Rangers favor battle in the wild, setting traps to harry the
               enemy.
 Requirements: None.
    Base Move: 4
    Base Jump: 4
 
 Comments: Highest Speed growth of Seeq (which isn't much, mind you). Though
 Mirror Items is very, very broken. Can you say 100% chance to stick a debuff?
 They can. Traps are... I suppose you can lay a trap in front of one of your 
 units so the enemy walks into it when trying to attack...

[049]------------------04.09. Passive Abilities per Race-----------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
This section is merely a compilation of all the Passive Abilities per a given
race and with which job you can find it. Meant to be a short-stop to find
that Passive Ability you want for that special unit.


HUME

BARD
¯¯¯¯
  Description: Bards wander the land with instrument in hand and song on
               tongue. Their magickal tunes are a source of delight and wonder.
 Available To: Hurdy (Moogle)
    Base Move: 3
    Base Jump: 2
 
 Comments: Bard is not a bad class in itself, but like all special jobs,
 it has HORRIBLE stat growth. Keep Hurdy here only when learning a skill and
 then go somewhere else for stat growth. Magick Ballad is one of the few
 skills that restore MP to other units, and doesn't require a charge or
 anything.

[04A]----------------------------04.11. Scions---------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Scions are the Espers from FF XII, if you every played it. These beings can 
only be summoned when you have a character equipped with the item for each
particular Scion.
During battle, doing any kind of action will raise your Smash Gauge. When it's
above 25~30%, the Scion's Wrath command should be available to use, from which
that character can summon the Scion whose item s/he has equipped.


This information was mostly obtained from the thread in the GameFAQs board,
taken with permission from the poster.

Credit goes to Shingouryu for making the post, Sh_aD_00w_Ninja for the original
idea, and Goshi3156 for assistance. Link to said thread is:
http://boards.gamefaqs.com/gfaqs/genmessage.php?board=937330&topic=44027706

Slight alterations to format from my part. In ()s you'll find the month where
the quest appears or other details relevant to it.

BELIAS, THE GIGAS
    Item: Gigas Pendant
   Quest: Camoa Cup (Silversun)
    Req.: 'The Yellow Wings'

MATEUS, THE CORRUPT
    Item: Corsage of Corruption
   Quest: Graszton Cup (Ashleaf)
    Req.: 'Camoa Cup' and 'Wanted: Gilmunto'

ADRAMMELECH, THE WROTH
    Item: Raging Brooch
   Quest: Moorabella Cup (Mistleaf)
    Req.: 'Graszton Cup' and 'Pearls in the Deep'

HASHMAL, BRINGER OF ORDER
    Item: Pin of Order
   Quest: Fluorgis Cup (Emberleaf)
    Req.: 'Moorabella Cup' and 'Grounded!'

FAMFRIT, THE DARKENING CLOUD
    Item: Ewer of Darkness
   Quest: Goug Cup (Plumfrost)
    Req.: 'Fluorgis Cup' and 'Sleepless Nights'

CHAOS, WALKER OF THE WHEEL
    Item: Ring of the Wheel
   Quest: Loar Cup (Skyfrost, in Moorabella)
    Req.: 'Goug Cup' and 'Seeking the Stone'

CÚCHULAINN, THE IMPURE
    Item: Tainted Cufflink
   Quest: Ordalia Cup (Bloodfire, in Fluorgis)
    Req.: 'Loar Cup' and 'A Request'

EXODUS, THE JUDGE-SAL
    Item: Gift of Judge-Sal
   Quest: Jylland Cup (Coppersun, in Moorabella)
    Req.: 'Ordalia Cup' and 'The Dig'

ZALERA, THE DEATH SERAPH
    Item: Earrings of the Dead
   Quest: The Last Step 
    Req.: 'The First Step' and all subsequent quests

ZEROMUS, THE CONDEMNER
    Item: Condemner's Choker
   Quest: The Five Kings
    Req.: 'Red King of Cinquleur' and all subsequent quests
          Because I'm getting questions of this... The Five Kings is an actual
          quest name, it doesn't concluded in the White King. There's one more
          after that, keep playing.

ULTIMA, THE HIGH SERAPH
    Item: High Seraph's Plume
   Quest: Hunted
    Req.: 'Under the Weather' and all subsequent quests

SHEMHAZAI, THE WHISPERER
    Item: Armlet of Whispers
  Battle: Of a Feather (This is a random battle)
    Req.: The Random Battle might appear in the Goug Region, in the Corpolk
          Field area. There's a chance that a Red Demon Bird enemy might 
          appear. After beating him, you should get the item.

ZODIARK, KEEPER OF PRECEPTS
    Item: Ring of the Precepts
   Quest: A Lasting Peace
    Req.: Complete questlines that start with 'Unfamiliar Folk' and 'I've Been
          Had, Kupo!' quests. You also have to have finished the storyline.
_______________________________________________________________________________

down and the item you get in return. No stats involved. I will arrange these 
by alphabetical order, just to make it easier to search (And write).

[056]-----------------------------05.06. Loot----------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

[006]-------------------------06. Auction System-------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Right after you've done "Now That's a Fire!" quest, you get access to the
Auction House system. Auctions are held every year. Remember you can rest in
Lezaford's hut for a lot of time.

The first time you check the AH of a region, you are fighting for control of
the regions (though only regions you've seen from that area). If you have all
regions under your control by the next auctions, you will be fighting for 
very nice items generally. So it's of your best interest to get these done.

Auctions are held using CP mostly. In the long run, after you've won several
auctions, you can enter the Token Shop, in which you can buy tokens of all
numbers using your own CP.

Here's how it works though. (The first time you go to an AH you also get
a small tutorial)

When you got to a town and enter the auction of the place, you get a set 
number of tokens. Tokens are worth 1, 2 or 3 points. And you can also get
5 point tokens as a bonus (more later). 

When bidding for an area, you will see all participants on the left side from
top to bottom, and at the very bottom of the screen is a time-bar divided
at certain intervals. Between each interval, a participant can use a token to
get those points added to their bidding for that region. In the end, s/he who
has the most points wins the area, obviously.

Of course, once you start bidding for one area, you gotta keep going, and you
can't get more tokens in between, so be careful.

Bonus tokens are worth 5 points. To get them, you might see at the bottom on
the time bar a small indicator that says '2nd', '1st' or what not. It means
if you are on that place by the end of that time-frame, you get a 5 point
token. Unless it's a very risky one (like being 4th before the last point),
it's on your best interest to get them. 

When you are bidding, Y will use a 1p Token. B is a 2p Token. A is a 3p token.
And X will use a 5p token if you have one. If you choose a token you can
still change, but R will make you pass on that particular turn.

Now, how to gauge your enemies...
 If they are shaking their head, they aren't bidding. Not even a point.
 If they just stand still, they are using a 1p token.
 If they are walking slowly, they are going up by 2 points.
 If they dash forward, that's a 3p token, careful.
 If they have a '!' above their heads, that means they are going all out, 
 that's a 5p token!
Sometimes, you require a lot of quick reactions here.  




ANYWAY... Regions. Prizes I've found so far also appear here.

 CAMOA: 
    Regions: Camoa, Targ Wood, Baptiste Hill
     Prizes: Swordbreaker, Carabineer Mail, Mirror Mail, Gastrophetes, 
             Black Garb, Tiara, Onion Sword, Francisca, Samite Coat
   G. Prize: Ever Robe, Mjolnir, Black Hat
 
 GRASZTON: 
    Regions: Graszton, Bisga Greenlands, Aldanna Range, Zedlei Forest
     Prizes: Ivory Pole, Grimoire Stone, Max's Oathbow, Chocobo Shield,
             Sumihomura, Barette, Cachusha, Chocobo Shield, Cat Claws, Oblige,
             Cachusha, Elixir, Mandragora, Magick Ring, Crescent Bow
   G. Prize: Ninja Tabi, Rangetsu-denbu, Genji Helm, Genji Armor
 
 MOORABELLA: 
    Regions: Moorabella, Galerria Deep, Rupie Mountains, Ruins of Delgantua
     Prizes: Restorer, Orb of Minwu, Artemis Bow, Lotus Mace, Gauntlets, 
             Dark Matter, Blueleaf Flute, Tiger Fangs, Gauntlets, Save the
             Queen
   G. Prize: Seventh Heaven, Soulsaber, Estrella
 
 FLUORGIS:
    Regions: Fluorgis, Tramdine Fens, Sant D'alsa Bluff, Nazan Mines
     Prizes: White Robe, Master Bow, Eight-fluter Pole, Manganese Saber,
             White Hat, Fortune Ring, Grimoire Stone, Six of Diamonds,
             Master Sword, Elixir, Maximilian, Eureka Crystal
   G. Prize: Tournesol, Galmia Shoes, Galmia Frock
 
 GOUG:
    Regions: Goug, Aisenfield, Kthili Sands, Neslowe Passage
     Prizes: Ayvuir Red, Grimoire Stone, Longbarrel, Claymore, Paraiba Blade,
             Femme Fatale, Beastsowrd, Claymore
   G. Prize: Bomb Arm, Tonberrian

After every auction, you will get 'experience' on certain aspects of the 
auction. Such as everytime you use a 1p token, every win, every bonus you
get, each one will open up a bonus for you.

Played: Requires you to play 20 auctions.
 -> Allows you to purchase extra tokens with CP before an action begins.
Bonuses: Requires you to get 20 bonuses.
 -> You get a free Ribbon. =D
1st/2nd/3rd: End in that place 10 times to master.
1/2/3/5-coin Tokens: Use said token 10 times to master.
 -> Gives you an extra token of that number at the beginning of an auction.
Passed: Requires you to pass 20 times.
 -> You get 200 CP. Yay...

When you control an entire region, you get the Region Master prize. It
makes you start an auction for an area you control with 1 free point.

-----------------------------------07. Other-----------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯

[071]--------------------------07.01. Recruiting-------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
A few tidbits of this information was obtained from Antitype's Recruiment Guide
in GameFAQs.
http://www.gamefaqs.com/portable/ds/file/937330/53332

Below are the spawning points for all races, in their months and the jobs that
zone offers. 
Two small notes.
In order to recruit Grias, you must have reached Fluorgis.
In order to recruit Vieras, you must have done 'The Moon Seal', which comes
after 'The Star Seal'.

You can also recruit them using the 'Clan Mates' quest, but the answers and all
that info is out of the scope of this guide. Feel free to refer to the guide
mentioned a few lines above for that info.

   HUMES
     -> Blackfrost and Skyfrost.
     -> Found in Targ Wood: Soldier, Thief, White Mage, Black Mage, Archer
     -> Found in Galerria Deep: Paladin, Fighter, Blue Mage, Hunter
     -> Found in Sant D'alsa Bluff: Parivir, Ninja, Illusionist, Seer
    
   BANGAA
     -> Greenfire and Bloodfire.
     -> Found in Baptiste Hill: Warrior, White Monk, Dragoon, Gladiator, Bishop
     -> Found in Aisenfield: Defender, Master Monk, Templar, Cannoneer, 
                             Trickster

   NU MOU
     -> Rosefire and Coppersun.
     -> Found in Graszton: White Mage, Black Mage, Beastmaster
     -> Found in Moorabella: Time Mage, Arcanist, Illusionist, Sage
     -> Found in Nazan Mines: Alchemist, Scholar
   
   MOOGLE
     -> Goldsun and Silversun.
     -> Found in Bisga Greenlands: Animist, Thief, Black Mage
     -> Found in Goug: Moogle Knight, Fusilier, Tinker, Time Mage
     -> Found in Neslowe Passage: Chocobo Rider, Juggler, Flintlock
   
   VIERA
     -> Ashleaf and Mistleaf.
     -> Found in Camoa: Fencer, Archer, White Mage, Green Mage, Red Mage
     -> Found in Rupie Mountains: Elementalist, Spellblade, Summoner, Assassin,
                                  Sniper

   GRIA
     -> Emberleaf and Plumfrost. Must have done the quest that leads to Frogis.
     -> Found in Fluorgis: Hunter, Raptor
     -> Found in Zedlei Forest: Ravager, Geomancer
   
   SEEQ
     -> Emberleaf and Plumfrost.
     -> Found in Aldanna Range: Berserker, Ranger
     -> Found in Tramdine Fens: Lanista, Viking

[072]---------------------------07.02. Chocobos--------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
This section merely notes down where you can find certain types of Chocobo.
Be it for your Chocobo Knight because you actually use one, or because you
need them for the quests.

Categorized by Month, of course. These are all, of course, in those random
monster encounters. If a color doesn't appear in a month, you can't get it at
that time. Of course, do remember Chocobos also appear on other quests.
I'm not listing those here.

Battles will not always show up, however. It seems fights 'spawn' on a given
timer of about 50 days per region. Shared, or not. I don't know.

TW: Targ Wood           KS: Kthili Sands         ZF: Zedlei Forest
AR: Aldanna Range       GD: Galerria Deep        RP: Rupie Mountain
GG: Goug

            _Yellow_ ___Red__ __Black_ __Green_ __Brown_ __White_ 
 Greenfire |   TW   |   --   |   GD   |   ZF   |   AR   |   RM   | 
           |--------+--------+--------+--------+--------+--------|
 Bloodfire |   TW   |   --   |   GD   |   ZF   |   --   |   RM   |
           |--------+--------+--------+--------+--------+--------|
  Rosefire |   --   |   --   |   GD   |   ZF   |   TW   |   --   |
           |--------+--------+--------+--------+--------+--------|
 Coppersun |   GG   | KS  GG |   GD   |   ZF   |   AR   |   --   |
           |--------+--------+--------+--------+--------+--------|
   Goldsun |   TW   |   KS   |   --   |   --   |   --   |   --   |
           |--------+--------+--------+--------+--------+--------|
 Silversun |   TW   |   --   |   GD   |   ZF   |   AR   |   RM   |
           |--------+--------+--------+--------+--------+--------|
   Ashleaf |   GG   | KS  GG |   --   |   ZF   |   AR   |   --   |
           |--------+--------+--------+--------+--------+--------|
  Mistleaf |   --   |   KS   |   --   |   ZF   |   AR   |   RM   |
           |--------+--------+--------+--------+--------+--------|
 Emberleaf |   --   |   --   |   --   |   --   |   --   |   --   |
           |--------+--------+--------+--------+--------+--------|
 Plumfrost |   GG   | KS  GG | RM  GD |   ZF   |   --   |   RM   |
           |--------+--------+--------+--------+--------+--------|
Blackfrost |   --   |   --   |   GD   |   ZF   |   --   |   RM   |
           |--------+--------+--------+--------+--------+--------|
  Skyfrost | TG  GG |   --   |   GD   |   ZF   |   AR   |   RM   |
            ¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯


And because I get questions about it...
In order to 'catch' a chocobo. Weaken it first (down to HP Critical), and
put your Chocobo Knight next to it. A special command should be available
which you can use to mount up. That's it.
_______________________________________________________________________________
[073]------------------07.03. Recruiting the Extra Units-----------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Here you can find the steps to recruit all of the extra units, along with 
information on the Heritor job and all its weapons.

MONTBLANC
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
 1. After doing Story Mission 15: Wanted: Sky Pirate Vaan, head over to
    Fluorgis. You should see a '!' icon named "I'm back, kupo". Watch the 
    scene.
    * It seems this might be available as soon as after story mission 14,
    but I saw it after 15, so I listed it as that. (Thanks to Eakka)
 2. In order for the quest "Wanted: Friends, kupo!" to show up, you must
    posses the following summoning items: Gigas Pendant, Earrings of the
    Dead, and the Pin of Order.
 3. Successfully completing the previous quest will get Montblanc in your clan.
    (Thanks to Everton Vasconcellos for this last part)

FRIMELDA
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
 1. Do the 'Under the Weather' quest.
 2. Do the 'A Chill in the Night' quest.
 3. Do the 'Show of Strength' quest.
 4. Do the 'Hunted' quest.
 5. Do the 'Memories Forged' quest. She will then join you as a Paladin with
    Dual Wield. 
    (Thanks to Donovan Knowles for sending this before I got to it)
_______________________________________________________________________________
[008]-------------------------------08. FAQ------------------------------------
¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯¯
Q. What's the MVP statue for? (Taken from the Boards FAQ)
A. Increases the chance of success when the unit is sent on a dispatch misison.

Q. Why didn't I find people on this region in this month when it said I could?!
A. This is a tricky, popular question. As far as my understanding on the
   system goes, which might be completely whack (my understanding), it goes
   like this...
   Come a certain 'spawning season' for a race, the game decides on a random
   seed where the recruit bubble will appear. And I assume it can choose a 
   region you haven't unlocked, wasting the whole month or season, depending
   on how it handles that.
   Or perhaps you have access to the region, but not the jobs that can
   spawn there. Best suggestion I can give is to save before the next season
   for that race starts and keep trying. Or just waste time. 
   For Gria however, you must have reached Fluorgis at least. For Viera, you
   must have done 'The Moon Seal' quest for whatever reason
   
Q. Ok, but where can I find more members?
A. Please look above in Section 07.01.

Q. Are there any one-of-a-kind loot pieces that you can miss on forever, 
   therefore barring you from a quest or an item?
A. Nope. Some loot items might be more difficult to obtain than another, but
   you can't lock yourself out of a weapon or a quest by selling loot left
   and right. Well, perhaps just a 2nd Shining Lute...
   
Q. What's with that Brightmoon Tor that appears in Graszton? Any details or
   tips for it? (Or other places)
A. It's sort of a bonus feature added in the NA version of the game.
   Details and information will be included in the guide at a later date, my
   team is not good enough yet.

Q. How do I catch a Chocobo?
A. Well, you don't 'catch' them... Weaken it down to HP Critical, put your
   Chocobo Knight right next to it, and use the newly available special command
   to mount up. Simple as that.

Q. What is the range of ranged weapons? (Information contributed by Tristam
   Izumi)
A. Here's a simple list.
   BOWS
      5 Cells: Every bow besides those below.
      6 Cells: Silver Bow, Perseus Bow
      7 Cells: Artemis Bow
   GREATBOWS
      5 Cells: Every greatbow besides those below.
      6 Cells: Elfin Bow, Max's Oathbow
      7 Cells: Arbalest, Marduk, Gastrophetes, Seventh Heaven
   GUNS
      7 Cells: Silver Cannon
      8 Cells: All other guns
   HAND-CANNONS
      7 Cells: All
   CARDS
      8 Cells: All
_______________________________________________________________________________
